adv_u58.note: Al salir de la habitación, ella… / Sin saber qué hacer…
adv_u58.exp:
Unit 58 — construcciones de participio adverbiales (lección 1).

Fenómeno: reducción de oraciones subordinadas mediante formas en -ing y -ed. Marcador clave del lenguaje formal escrito.

Tipos:
  • -ing activo (simultaneidad/consecuencia): Seeing the danger, she acted quickly. — Al ver el peligro, actuó deprisa.
  • -ed pasivo: Exhausted by the climb, they rested. — Agotados por la subida, descansaron.
  • Perfecto having + V3 (anterioridad): Having finished the report, she left. — Tras terminar el informe, se fue.
  • Negativo: Not knowing what to do, he called for help.
Regla fundamental: el sujeto de la construcción debe ser el mismo que el de la oración principal; de lo contrario, dangling participle.

Valores: tiempo, causa, condición, concesión (según el contexto).

Contraste: Being tired, I went to bed (causa) vs When tired, I get irritable (condición). El estilo formal prefiere los participios; el coloquial, oraciones completas.
adv_u58.exc: La construcción de participio debe tener el mismo sujeto que la oración principal.
mistakes: Walking home, the rain started. → While I was walking home, the rain started. — dangling participle.
Being tired, the bed looked inviting. → Being tired, I found the bed inviting.
Not knowing the answer, the teacher explained. → Not knowing the answer, I asked the teacher.

adv_u59.exp:
Unit 59 — construcciones de participio adverbiales (lección 2).

Fenómeno: modelos avanzados: participios con conectores y construcciones absolutas.

Estructuras:
  • Participio + conector: While working on the project, she discovered… / When asked, he refused. / If treated early, the illness is curable.
  • Construcción absoluta (con sujeto propio): The meeting (being) over, everyone left. — Terminada la reunión, todos se fueron. / Weather permitting, we'll go hiking. — Si el tiempo lo permite, iremos de excursión.
  • Construcciones con with: With the project completed, we can relax.
Registro: las absolute constructions son propias del estilo literario y oficial.

Ejemplos:
  • All things considered, it was a success. — En conjunto, fue un éxito.
  • The sun having set, we headed home. — Tras ponerse el sol, regresamos a casa.
Contraste: having done subraya la anterioridad respecto a la acción principal; doing, la simultaneidad.
adv_u59.exc: El participio perfecto having done subraya la anterioridad respecto a la oración principal.
mistakes: Having finished, he is leaving. → Having finished, he left. — concordancia de tiempos.
Finished the work, he went home. → Having finished the work, he went home.
Being arrived, we had dinner. → Having arrived, we had dinner.

adv_u60.note: Ella misma / él mismo / ellos mismos: casos especiales
adv_u60.exp:
Unit 60 — pronombres reflexivos en C1.

Fenómeno: los reflexive pronouns cumplen varias funciones y a menudo se confunden con each other y los pronombres personales.

Funciones:
  • Reflexiva auténtica (sujeto = objeto): She hurt herself.
  • Enfática: I did it myself. — Lo hice yo mismo.
  • Tras la preposición by = «solo/por cuenta propia»: She lives by herself.
  • Idiomática: help yourself, enjoy yourself, make yourself at home, behave yourself.
Cuando NO se usa: con verbos de cuidado personal (wash, shave, dress, hide), el reflexivo suele omitirse: She dressed quickly.

Contraste: They love each other (recíproco, A→B y B→A) vs They love themselves (cada uno a sí mismo). Tras with: I took my keys with me, no with myself (salvo énfasis). Between you and me, no myself: típica hipercorrección.
adv_u60.exc: Tras wash, dress, shave, hide el reflexivo suele omitirse: I washed, no I washed myself.
mistakes: She washed herself and dressed herself. → She washed and dressed.
I and my friend saw us in the mirror. → My friend and I saw ourselves in the mirror.
They talked themselves. → They talked to each other.

adv_u61.note: Uno como pronombre / pronombre general / unos a otros
adv_u61.exp:
Unit 61 — one/ones como sustituto y pronombre generalizador.

Fenómeno: one desempeña dos funciones: sustituir un sustantivo contable y generalizar como «la gente en general».

Funciones:
  • Sustitución: I prefer the red one. / The ones on the left are mine.
  • Generalizador (formal): One should always be polite. — Siempre hay que ser educado. El equivalente coloquial es you.
  • One another = recíproco (más formal que each other): They support one another.
Restricciones: one/ones no se usa con incontables (this music, no this one) ni tras posesivos (my book, no my one, salvo en BrE coloq.).

Ejemplos:
  • Which jacket? — The blue one.
  • One never knows what may happen. — Nunca se sabe lo que puede pasar.
Contraste: el estilo académico prefiere one como generalizador; el coloquial, you.
adv_u61.exc: One como pronombre generalizador es formal: One should be careful.
mistakes: I need a red one pen. → I need a red one / a red pen. — one sustituye al sust.
Give me the blue ones book. → Give me the blue one.
They helped themselves one to another. → They helped one another / each other.

adv_u62.note: Eso creo / eso espero / eso dicen
adv_u62.exp:
Unit 62 — so como sustituto de cláusula.

Fenómeno: so sustituye una cláusula con that entera para evitar repeticiones.

Verbos: think, hope, believe, suppose, expect, imagine, guess, be afraid, seem, appear, say, tell.

Ejemplos:
  • Will he come? — I think so. — Creo que sí.
  • Is it raining? — I'm afraid so. — Me temo que sí.
  • So I've heard. — Eso he oído.
Negación — dos patrones:
  • I don't think so (habitual con think, believe, suppose, expect, imagine).
  • I think not / I hope not / I'm afraid not (con hope, be afraid, guess, say: negación solo con not).
Contraste: Say so (confirmar) vs say that (introducir información nueva). If so — «si es así»; if not — «si no». No se puede I think it como sustituto de la cláusula.
adv_u62.exc: I'm afraid so / not; pero no I'm afraid it.
mistakes: I think it. → I think so. — so sustituye a la cláusula con that.
I hope no. → I hope not.
I don't think so that he will come. → I don't think he will come. — redundante.

adv_u63.note: Ella lo hizo de inmediato. Tal era su ira.
adv_u63.exp:
Unit 63 — do so y such.

Fenómeno: do so es un sustituto formal del grupo verbal; such es un recurso de intensificación, sobre todo en la inversión.

Do so / do it / do that:
  • Do so: formal, sustitución exacta del verbo con sus complementos: She was asked to sign the document, which she did so willingly.
  • Do it: neutro: Did you tell him? — Yes, I did it yesterday.
  • Do that: con énfasis en la acción.
Such:
  • Such + a/an + adj + sust.: It was such a beautiful day.
  • Inversión (muy formal): Such was his anger that he slammed the door. — Tal era su ira que cerró la puerta de un portazo.
  • Such as = por ejemplo: fruits such as apples.
Registro: do so y such con inversión son propios del estilo académico y literario. Contraste: so + adj (so beautiful) vs such + a + adj + sust. (such a beautiful day).
adv_u63.exc: Do so es formal; en la lengua coloquial se usa do it/that.
mistakes: Such a was the noise → Such was the noise — sin artículo en la inversión.
He asked me to leave and I did it so. → …and I did so.
It was such nice day. → It was such a nice day. — such a + sust. contable en sing.

adv_u64.note: Ella sabe nadar y él también / Elipsis
adv_u64.exp:
Unit 64 — elipsis de verbos auxiliares.

Fenómeno: omisión del grupo verbal conservando el auxiliar; es clave para la cohesión del texto y la naturalidad del habla.

Modelos:
  • Coincidencia so + aux + sujeto: She likes jazz, and so does he.
  • Coincidencia negativa neither/nor + aux + sujeto: I don't smoke, and neither does she.
  • Contraste: He works hard, but she doesn't. (se omite work hard)
  • Respuestas breves: Will you come? — I might.
Inversión: tras so/neither/nor es obligatorio el orden invertido: So do I, no So I do. So I do = confirmación («¡sí que lo hago!»).

Ejemplos:
  • I've never been to Japan. — Neither have I.
  • Tom passed, but Sam didn't.
Contraste: Me too (coloq.) = So do I (más formal). Tras el modal be: So am I.
adv_u64.exc: So/neither/nor + auxiliar + sujeto: So do I, no So I do.
mistakes: I can swim and he can too swim. → …and so can he.
She doesn't smoke and neither I do. → …and neither do I. — inversión.
I'm tired. Me too am. → I'm tired. So am I / Me too.

adv_u65.exp:
Unit 65 — elipsis de infinitivos con to.

Fenómeno: se mantiene to cuando se omite el grupo de infinitivo; es clave para la naturalidad del habla.

Estructuras:
  • To solitario: I'd love to come but I'm not able to. — Me encantaría, pero no puedo.
  • En condiciones y evasivas corteses: I would help if I wanted to.
  • Respuestas: Are you going? — I'd like to.
Verbos que suelen admitir la elipsis con to: want, plan, hope, need, try, mean, intend, would like, used to.

Reglas:
  • No puede repetirse un segundo to: I'd like to, but I can't to → I can't.
  • Tras can/must/will y otros modales, la elipsis es sin to: I can if you want me to.
Contraste: I don't want to (se omite el complemento) vs I don't want («simplemente no quiero», sin referencia). La elipsis es obligatoria en el diálogo coherente: sin ella la respuesta suena pedante.
adv_u65.exc: Tras want, would like, try se puede omitir lo que sigue a to: I'd love to, no I'd love to come.
mistakes: I'd like to, but I can't to. → I'd like to, but I can't. — el segundo to sobra.
He told me do it. → He told me to do it. — tell + to-inf.
Would you like? — Yes, I'd like. → Yes, I'd like to. — se conserva to.

adv_u66.exp:
Unit 66 — posición del adjetivo.

Fenómeno: restricciones sobre el uso atributivo (antes del sust.) y predicativo (tras un verbo copulativo). Algunos adjetivos cambian de significado según la posición.

Grupos:
  • Solo atributivos: main, chief, former, mere, outright, sheer, nuclear, atomic: the main reason, no the reason is main.
  • Solo predicativos: afraid, alive, alone, asleep, awake, aware, content, glad, ill, well, unable: The child is asleep, pero a sleeping child (no asleep child).
  • Cambio de significado: the present members (actuales) vs the members present (presentes); a certain person (cierta) vs I'm certain (seguro); my old friend (de toda la vida) vs my friend is old (viejo).
Ejemplos:
  • The late Mr Smith: el difunto (atributivo);
  • He's late: llega tarde (predicativo).
Contraste: la posición no es estilística, sino semántica. La posposición es típica de expresiones fijas (time immemorial, body politic).
adv_u66.exc: Alive, alone, afraid, asleep, awake: solo predicativos: an alive fish → a living fish.
mistakes: The afraid child → The frightened child.
The present members (con el sentido de «presentes») → The members present — la posposición cambia el significado.
An asleep baby → A sleeping baby.

adv_u67.text: Unidad 67 – Adjetivos calificables y no calificables (lección 1)
adv_u67.exp:
Unit 67 — adjetivos gradables y no gradables (lección 1).

Fenómeno: los adjetivos se dividen en relativos (admiten grados) y absolutos (ya expresan un estado extremo). El tipo determina la elección del intensificador.

Gradables: cold, hot, big, tired, interesting, important. Intensificadores: very, quite, rather, fairly, a bit, slightly, extremely, incredibly.

No gradables:
  • Absolutos: perfect, unique, impossible, essential, dead, alive, correct, wrong.
  • Extremos: freezing, boiling, exhausted, starving, furious, terrified, delicious, brilliant.
  • Clasificadores: nuclear, digital, annual, wooden.
Intensificadores para no gradables: absolutely, completely, totally, utterly, quite (con el sentido de «totalmente»).

Ejemplos:
  • It was absolutely freezing. — Hacía un frío espantoso.
  • The film was very interesting.
Contraste: very cold (gradable) vs absolutely freezing (ya es un extremo). Quite con gradables = «bastante»; con no gradables = «absolutamente».
adv_u67.exc: Very no se combina con no gradables: very perfect → absolutely perfect.
mistakes: Very delicious → Absolutely delicious. — no gradable.
Absolutely good → Very good. — gradable.
Very impossible → Completely impossible.

adv_u68.text: Unidad 68 – Adjetivos calificables y no calificables (lección 2)
adv_u68.exp:
Unit 68 — adjetivos gradables y no gradables (lección 2).

Fenómeno: la pragmática de la elección del intensificador y las colocaciones fijas típicas del C1.

Colocaciones (pares fijos):
  • Bitterly cold/disappointed: amargamente frío/decepcionado.
  • Highly successful/unlikely/controversial: sumamente exitoso/improbable.
  • Utterly ridiculous/exhausted: completamente ridículo/exhausto.
  • Deeply concerned/sorry: profundamente preocupado.
  • Perfectly reasonable/fine: perfectamente razonable.
Atenuadores: a bit, slightly, somewhat, rather, fairly: solo con gradables.

Registro:
  • Formal: highly, extremely, considerably.
  • Coloquial: really, pretty, dead (BrE), well (BrE colloq).
Ejemplos:
  • The results were highly encouraging. — Los resultados fueron sumamente alentadores.
  • She was bitterly disappointed by the outcome.
Contraste: very unique es tabú en la norma escrita, aunque aparece en el habla. Quite unique sí se admite con el sentido de «realmente único».
adv_u68.exc: Las colocaciones están estrictamente fijadas: utterly ridiculous, bitterly cold, highly successful.
mistakes: Highly cold → Bitterly cold. — colocación fija.
Absolutely tired → Completely exhausted. — con no gradable.
Very unique → Quite unique / unique. — unique es no gradable.

adv_u69.text: Unidad 69 – Adjetivos participios y adjetivos compuestos
adv_u69.exp:
Unit 69 — adjetivos de participio y adjetivos compuestos.

Fenómeno: recurso productivo para crear modificadores adjetivales a partir de verbos y de sintagmas.

Participle adjectives:
  • -ing: propiedad activa que provoca la emoción: a boring lecture (la clase es aburrida porque provoca aburrimiento).
  • -ed: estado pasivo/resultante: a bored audience (público aburrido).
Compound adjectives:
  • Con guion antes del sust.: a well-known writer, a ten-year-old girl, state-of-the-art technology.
  • Sin guion tras verbo copulativo: The writer is well known.
  • Modelos productivos: N + -ed (big-eyed, left-handed); adj + N + -ed (good-natured); adv + V3 (air-conditioned, purpose-built); adj + V-ing (good-looking, slow-moving).
Ejemplos:
  • A thought-provoking book. — Un libro que invita a reflexionar.
  • A soft-spoken diplomat.
Contraste: -ing vs -ed se confunden con regularidad: I'm interested (estoy interesado) vs it's interesting (es interesante).
adv_u69.exc: -ing describe la causa de la emoción; -ed, el estado que se experimenta: boring film / bored viewer.
mistakes: I am boring with this lesson. → I am bored with this lesson. — estado = -ed.
The film was bored. → The film was boring.
A well-know artist → A well-known artist. — compuesto con -ed.

adv_u70.note: Con ganas de aprender / capaz de hacer / seguro de que
adv_u70.exp:
Unit 70 — adjetivos con to-inf, -ing y cláusula con that.

Fenómeno: los adjetivos exigen complementos específicos; su confusión genera errores.

Modelos:
  • Adj + to-inf: able, eager, reluctant, willing, likely, unlikely, due, liable, keen, happy, ready: She was eager to learn.
  • Adj + prep + -ing: capable of doing, fond of reading, interested in learning, afraid of failing, tired of waiting, good at singing.
  • Adj + cláusula con that: sure that, certain that, aware that, convinced that, surprised that, sorry that, glad that.
  • Valorativos con it formal: It is kind of you to help. It is difficult for us to decide.
Ejemplos:
  • I'm afraid of flying. — Tengo miedo a volar.
  • He's keen to start / keen on starting. — doble modelo con keen.
Contraste: sorry to + inf (acción futura) vs sorry for -ing (acción pasada). Good at, no good in. Las preposiciones con adjetivos forman parte del léxico mínimo del C1.
adv_u70.exc: Keen on + ing, pero keen to + inf: keen on swimming / keen to learn.
mistakes: Capable to swim → Capable of swimming. — capable + of + -ing.
Interested to music → Interested in music.
Afraid to spiders → Afraid of spiders.

adv_u71.text: Unidad 71 – Adjetivos y adverbios: diferencias sutiles
adv_u71.note: Habló en voz alta. Es un orador ruidoso.
adv_u71.exp:
Unit 71 — diferencias sutiles entre adjetivos y adverbios.

Fenómeno: muchas palabras tienen dos formas, con -ly y sin él, con significados distintos, algo crucial en C1.

Pares:
  • Hard (con esfuerzo) / hardly (apenas): He works hard / He hardly works.
  • Late (tarde) / lately (últimamente): She arrived late / I've been busy lately.
  • Near (cerca) / nearly (casi): Come near / I nearly fell.
  • High (alto, físicamente) / highly (sumamente): Birds fly high / highly respected.
  • Deep (hondo, físicamente) / deeply (hondamente, emocional): dig deep / deeply concerned.
  • Wide (de par en par) / widely (ampliamente).
Adjetivos tras verbos copulativos: feel, look, sound, taste, smell, seem, appear, become, get: adj., no adv.: She looks happy, no happily.

Contraste: good/well: caso clásico: He speaks good English (adj. de English) vs He speaks English well (adv. de speaks).
adv_u71.exc: Hard/fast/late: igual forma para adj. y adv.; hardly, lately: otros significados.
mistakes: He works hardly. → He works hard. — hardly = «apenas».
She arrived lately. → She arrived late. — lately = «últimamente».
He speaks good English well. → He speaks English well.

adv_u72.note: Comparación en C1: elipse y construcciones especiales
adv_u72.exp:
Unit 72 — comparativo y superlativo en C1.

Fenómeno: modelos complejos de comparación: estructuras paralelas, intensificadores enfáticos, elipsis.

Estructuras:
  • Duplicación para indicar aumento: more and more expensive, colder and colder.
  • Paralela the + comp…, the + comp…: The more you practise, the better you get. — Cuanto más practiques, mejor lo harás.
  • Superlativo + Present Perfect: the best film I've ever seen.
  • Intensificadores del comparativo: much, far, a lot, considerably, significantly, slightly, a bit, rather + comp: far better, significantly higher.
Irregulares: good/well → better → best; bad/badly → worse → worst; far → further/farther → furthest/farthest; little → less → least.

Ejemplos:
  • This is by far the most interesting book. — Este es con mucho el libro más interesante.
Contraste: further (abstracto — further details) vs farther (distancia, sobre todo en AmE). El comparativo doble (more better) es tabú.
adv_u72.exc: Los bisílabos terminados en -y/-ow/-er suelen hacerlo con -er/-est: narrower, cleverer.
mistakes: More taller → Taller. — comparativo doble.
The most best → The best. — superlativo doble.
More good → Better.

adv_u73.note: De lo esperado / según lo planeado / tan bueno como parece
adv_u73.exp:
Unit 73 — sintagmas y cláusulas comparativas.

Fenómeno: construcciones comparativas reducidas y elípticas para una expresión económica.

Estructuras:
  • Incompletas: than expected, than planned, than usual, than before: The results were better than expected.
  • As…as: as soon as possible, as good as new, as often as not.
  • The same as / different from / similar to.
  • No sooner…than: No sooner had we arrived than it started raining. — Apenas llegamos, empezó a llover.
  • Expresiones: more or less, sooner or later, as good as it gets.
  • Rather than — conector: Rather than complain, she took action.
Ejemplos:
  • She's not as clever as she thinks. — No es tan lista como cree.
  • It's more difficult than it looks.
Registro: formal, than I am completo; coloquial, than me. Contraste: different from (estándar) / different to (BrE) / different than (AmE).
adv_u73.exc: The more… the more: The more he studies, the better he becomes.
mistakes: He is taller than me tall. → He is taller than I am / than me.
As soon as possible as → As soon as possible.
More expensive as → More expensive than.

adv_u74.note: Posición del adverbio en una oración: principio, medio, final.
adv_u74.exp:
Unit 74 — posición del adverbio (lección 1).

Fenómeno: el inglés tiene reglas estrictas de colocación de los adverbios con matices de significado sutiles.

Tres posiciones:
  • Front (inicial): énfasis, comentario: Suddenly, the lights went out.
  • Mid (media): antes del verbo léxico, tras be, tras el primer auxiliar: She always arrives early. / She is always on time. / She has always lived here.
  • End (final): posición neutra para adverbios de modo, lugar y tiempo.
Reglas para la posición media: frecuencia (often, always, usually, rarely, never), certeza (probably, definitely, certainly), foco (just, only, even), compleción (already, still, yet).

Ejemplos:
  • He has probably forgotten. — Probablemente se ha olvidado.
  • I only saw him yesterday.
Contraste: She's never late / She is never late: tras be. Con respuestas breves, el énfasis puede alterar la regla: Yes, she always has.
adv_u74.exc: Los adverbios de frecuencia suelen ir antes del verbo léxico, pero tras be: She is often late.
mistakes: I always am tired. → I am always tired. — tras be.
She speaks always loudly. → She always speaks loudly.
He goes usually to work by bus. → He usually goes to work by bus.

adv_u75.exp:
Unit 75 — posición del adverbio (lección 2): varios circunstantes.

Fenómeno: cuando hay varios complementos circunstanciales al final, rige un orden claro.

Orden: Manner (cómo) → Place (dónde) → Time (cuándo).

Ejemplos:
  • She sang beautifully in the theatre last night. — Cantó maravillosamente en el teatro anoche.
  • He works hard at the office every day.
Excepciones y matices:
  • El complemento de tiempo puede anteponerse: Last night, she sang beautifully in the theatre.
  • Verbos de movimiento: el lugar va justo después del verbo: She went home quickly, normalmente no quickly home.
  • Varios adverbios del mismo tipo: del más general al más específico: every Tuesday at 3 p.m.
Regla: el objeto directo no se separa del verbo con un adverbio: She speaks English fluently, no She speaks fluently English.

Contraste: I always play tennis in the park on Sundays: frecuencia en posición media + MPT.
adv_u75.exc: Orden: manner → place → time. She sang beautifully in the theatre last night.
mistakes: I go every day to work. → I go to work every day. — el tiempo va al final.
She plays in the park tennis. → She plays tennis in the park. — el objeto va antes del complemento.
He drives quickly always. → He always drives quickly.

adv_u76.text: Unidad 76 – Adverbios de lugar, dirección, regularidad y tiempo
adv_u76.exp:
Unit 76 — adverbios de lugar, dirección, frecuencia y tiempo.

Fenómeno: clasificación de los adverbios por tipo de significado y particularidades de cada grupo.

Grupos:
  • Lugar: here, there, everywhere, nowhere, somewhere, indoors, outdoors, abroad, upstairs.
  • Dirección: home, away, forward, backward, up, down.
  • Tiempo: now, then, soon, recently, yesterday, today, tomorrow, already, still, yet.
  • Frecuencia: always, usually, often, frequently, sometimes, occasionally, rarely, seldom, hardly ever, never.
Inversión tras here/there: con sustantivo, inversión: Here comes the bus!; con pronombre, orden normal: Here she comes.

Ejemplos:
  • I've been abroad recently. — Estuve en el extranjero hace poco.
  • She rarely complains.
Registro: hardly ever (coloq.), seldom (formal). Contraste: home y abroad son adverbios y no requieren preposición: go home, no go to home.
adv_u76.exc: Here/there provocan inversión: Here comes the bus (pero no con pronombre: Here she comes).
mistakes: Here comes he. → Here he comes. — con pronombre, sin inversión.
Recently I have see him. → Recently I have seen him / I have seen him recently.
I go there to home. → I go home. — home ya es adverbio.

adv_u77.text: Unidad 77 – Adverbios de grado y adverbios de foco
adv_u77.note: muy / extremadamente / incluso / sólo / sólo / casi
adv_u77.exp:
Unit 77 — adverbios de grado y de foco.

Fenómeno: dos clases importantes: unos modifican la intensidad; otros restringen la referencia y dependen críticamente de la posición.

Adverbios de grado: very, extremely, incredibly, really, quite, rather, fairly, pretty, somewhat, a bit, slightly. Antes de adj./adv.: very quickly, extremely happy.

Adverbios de foco: only, just, even, also, too, mainly, especially, particularly, merely, simply. Su posición determina qué se destaca.

Ejemplos de cambios posicionales:
  • Only she told him the truth. — Solo ella se lo dijo (nadie más).
  • She only told him the truth. — Solo se lo dijo (no hizo nada más).
  • She told only him the truth. — Solo a él (no a otros).
  • She told him only the truth. — Solo la verdad (nada más).
Registro: rather con connotación negativa (rather disappointing); quite ambiguo (BrE «bastante», AmE «totalmente»).

Contraste: fairly good (aceptable) < quite good (bien) < very good.
adv_u77.exc: Only/even/just: la posición cambia el significado: I only saw Tom vs I saw only Tom.
mistakes: I almost never meet him. (más bien) → I hardly ever meet him.
<s>Only he wants to help.</s> (con el sentido de «solo él») puede leerse de dos formas; concreta la posición.
He is very asleep. → He is fast / sound asleep.

adv_u78.text: Unidad 78 – Adverbios de comentario y adverbios de punto de vista
adv_u78.exp:
Unit 78 — adverbios comentadores y de punto de vista.

Fenómeno: estos adverbios modifican toda la oración y expresan la actitud del hablante o delimitan el ámbito de aplicación.

Comment adverbs (valoración de la situación): fortunately, unfortunately, surprisingly, obviously, clearly, naturally, admittedly, undoubtedly, hopefully, presumably, apparently.

Viewpoint adverbs (punto de vista/ámbito): personally, frankly, honestly, technically, scientifically, politically, financially, theoretically.

Posición: habitualmente al principio, separados por coma; con menos frecuencia, en posición media.

Ejemplos:
  • Frankly, I disagree with his approach. — Francamente, no estoy de acuerdo con su enfoque.
  • Technically, that's not allowed. — Técnicamente eso no está permitido.
  • Unfortunately, the meeting was cancelled.
Registro: son fundamentales en la escritura académica y de negocios para matizar y posicionar al autor.

Contraste: She spoke frankly (adv. de modo: cómo hablaba) vs Frankly, she's wrong (comentador: actitud del autor).
adv_u78.exc: Los comment adverbs suelen ir al principio + coma: Frankly, I disagree.
mistakes: Personal, I think so. → Personally, I think so.
Hopefully he comes. → Hopefully, he will come. — con futuro.
Obvious, he is right. → Obviously, he is right.

adv_u79.note: Cuando / tan pronto como / una vez / hasta / cuando llegue el momento
adv_u79.exp:
Unit 79 — subordinadas de tiempo.

Fenómeno: conjunciones temporales con distintos matices y la regla férrea: nada de tiempos de futuro tras ellas.

Conjunciones y matices:
  • When: relación temporal general.
  • As soon as / the moment (that) / immediately: secuencia inmediata.
  • Once: tras haberse completado (= «una vez que»): Once you finish, we can go.
  • Until / till: hasta qué momento.
  • By the time: para el momento en que, a menudo con Perfect: By the time he arrived, we had eaten.
  • While / as: simultaneidad.
  • Before / after: secuencia.
Regla férrea: presente en lugar de futuro, y Past Perfect en lugar de would-have: I'll call when I arrive, no when I will arrive.

Ejemplos:
  • By the time you read this, I will have left. — Para cuando leas esto, ya me habré ido.
  • As soon as she heard the news, she called him.
Contraste: when interrogativo sí admite futuro (When will you come?).
adv_u79.exc: Tras when, as soon as, once, until, by the time: presente en lugar de futuro.
mistakes: I'll call you when I will arrive. → …when I arrive.
Until you won't finish → Until you finish.
By the time he will come → By the time he comes.

adv_u80.text: Unidad 80 - Dar razones: como, porque; para y con
adv_u80.note: Como / porque / desde / para / con - todos los métodos de causa
adv_u80.exp:
Unit 80 — expresión de la causa: as, because, since, for, with.

Fenómeno: cinco recursos con diferencias de registro y de posición.

Diferencias:
  • Because: neutro, información nueva, responde a «¿por qué?»: We stayed in because it was raining.
  • As / since: causa conocida u obvia, a menudo al principio: Since it's late, let's stop.
  • For: formal y literario, información nueva, solo tras la oración principal: He was anxious, for she was late. — Estaba inquieto, pues ella tardaba.
  • With + sust. / -ing: causa concomitante: With everyone watching, I got nervous.
  • Because of / due to / owing to / on account of + sust./-ing: preposicionales.
Registro: for como causa es libresco, casi arcaico; due to es formal; because es universal.

Contraste: because + cláusula, because of + sust. Due to tradicionalmente atributivo (delays due to fog), aunque en el estilo moderno se admite también como adverbial.
adv_u80.exc: For con valor causal es formal y literario: He was worried, for she was late.
mistakes: Because of he was late → Because he was late / Because of his lateness.
As/since en posición media a veces suenan forzados; because es más neutro.
With her smart, she succeeded. → With her being smart / Being smart, she succeeded.

adv_u81.text: Unidad 81 – Propósitos y resultados: con el fin de, para que
adv_u81.note: Ella se fue para evitar discusiones.
adv_u81.exp:
Unit 81 — finalidad y resultado.

Fenómeno: distinción entre construcciones de propósito (intención) y resultativas (consecuencia).

Propósito:
  • To-inf (mismo sujeto): She left early to catch the train.
  • In order to / so as to: más formal: He worked hard in order to succeed.
  • So that / in order that + cláusula (sujetos distintos, a menudo con can/could/would): He whispered so that nobody could hear.
  • Finalidad negativa: so as not to / in order not to: He left early so as not to be late.
Resultado:
  • So + adj/adv + that: It was so cold that we stayed in.
  • Such + (a/an) + adj + sust. + that: It was such a shock that he fainted.
  • So much/many/little/few + sust. + that.
Contraste: so that puede expresar finalidad o resultado según el contexto: He spoke clearly so that everyone could understand (finalidad) vs He spoke so clearly that everyone understood (resultado). No se usa for + to-inf: I came for to see you → to see you.
adv_u81.exc: So as not to / in order not to para la finalidad negativa: He left early so as not to miss the train.
mistakes: I came for to see you. → I came to see you. — for + inf no es aceptable.
So that he can't hear (si es finalidad) → so that he couldn't hear / so as not to let him hear.
For not being late → In order not to be late / So as not to be late.

adv_u82.text: Unidad 82 – Cláusulas de contraste: aunque, mientras, mientras, mientras que
adv_u82.note: Aunque / aunque / mientras / mientras / a pesar de
adv_u82.exp:
Unit 82 — contraposición: although, while, whilst, whereas, despite.

Fenómeno: dos tipos: concesivas (a pesar de) y adversativas (en contraposición).

Conjunciones concesivas:
  • Although / though / even though + cláusula: Although it was cold, we went out. Even though: reforzada.
  • Despite / in spite of + sust./-ing/the fact that: Despite the cold, we went out / Despite being cold…
Adversativas:
  • While / whilst / whereas: contraponen dos ideas: While he prefers tea, she likes coffee. Whilst: BrE más formal.
Conectores adverbiales: however, nevertheless, nonetheless, yet, still, on the other hand: unen dos oraciones: It was cold; however, we went out.

Registro: though al final de la oración es coloquial: It's cold. I'll go, though.

Contraste: Although y but no se combinan: no Although it rained, but we went. Despite requiere sintagma nominal, no subordinada.
adv_u82.exc: Despite / in spite of + sust./-ing, no + cláusula: despite he was tired → despite being tired / although he was tired.
mistakes: Despite of the rain → Despite the rain / In spite of the rain.
Although tired, but he went. → Although tired, he went. — sin but.
Whereas he is tall, his brother short. → Whereas he is tall, his brother is short.

adv_u83.exp:
Unit 83 — oraciones condicionales (lección 1): tipos 0, 1 y 2.

Fenómeno: tres tipos básicos de condiciones reales e hipotéticas.

Tipos:
  • Zero (leyes siempre válidas): If + Present, Present: If you heat water to 100°C, it boils.
  • First (futuro real): If + Present, will/can/may…: If it rains, we'll stay in.
  • Second (presente irreal/futuro poco probable): If + Past, would/could/might…: If I had more time, I'd travel more.
Variantes en la oración principal: will, can, may, might, should, must, be going to: expresan distintos matices modales. If you see him, tell him (imperativo).

Regla: tras if, nunca will en las condicionales estándar.

Ejemplos:
  • If I were you, I would apologize. — En tu lugar pediría disculpas.
  • Should you need help, call me. (inversión formal del tipo 1.)
Contraste: If you will es posible en peticiones corteses (If you will excuse me): significado aparte.
adv_u83.exc: En Zero/First Conditional, tras if no se usa will: if you will come → if you come.
mistakes: If I will have time, I'll call. → If I have time, I'll call.
If he comes, I tell him. → …I'll tell him.
If I would be rich → If I were rich. — tipo 2.

adv_u84.exp:
Unit 84 — oraciones condicionales (lección 2): tipo 3 y mixtas.

Fenómeno: condiciones totalmente referidas al pasado y combinaciones con referencias temporales distintas.

Third Conditional: If + Past Perfect, would have + V3. Pasado no realizado: If I had studied harder, I would have passed. — Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado.

Mixed Conditionals:
  • Causa pasada → consecuencia presente: If I had taken the job, I would be rich now. — Si entonces hubiera aceptado el trabajo, ahora sería rico.
  • Cualidad presente → consecuencia pasada: If she weren't so careless, she wouldn't have broken it. — Si no fuera tan descuidada, no lo habría roto.
Registro/estilo: variante formal con inversión: Had I known, I would have helped.

Ejemplos:
  • If they hadn't warned us, we might be dead. (mixta)
  • We would have arrived earlier if the train hadn't been delayed.
Contraste: el tipo 3 expresa un lamento pleno sobre el pasado; las mixtas separan el tiempo de la causa y el de la consecuencia.
adv_u84.exc: Tipo mixto: If I had studied, I would be a doctor now.
mistakes: If I would have known → If I had known.
If she didn't miss the bus, she would be here. (si la causa es pasada) → If she hadn't missed the bus, she would be here now.
I would passed if I had studied. → I would have passed if I had studied.

adv_u85.text: Unidad 85 - Si yo fuera tú; imagina que lo fuera...
adv_u85.exp:
Unit 85 — subjuntivo con were e inversión en las condicionales.

Fenómeno: alternativas formales a las condicionales habituales, propias del estilo de los negocios, jurídico y literario.

Subjuntivo were: en todas las personas en condiciones irreales: If I were you… If she were here… If he were to… Coloquialmente se admite was, salvo en la fórmula fija If I were you.

Inversión sin if:
  • Tipo 2: Were + sujeto + to-inf: Were she to call, I would answer.
  • Tipo 3: Had + sujeto + V3: Had I known, I would have helped.
  • Condicional cortés: Should + sujeto + inf: Should you require assistance, please contact us.
Fórmulas fijas: I wish I were… Imagine he were… As it were (por así decirlo).

Ejemplos:
  • Should the weather deteriorate, the event will be postponed. (estilo de negocios)
  • Were it not for your help, I would have failed. — De no haber sido por tu ayuda…
Contraste: las formas invertidas son económicas y prestigiosas en la comunicación formal.
adv_u85.exc: If I were you: formal; if I was you se admite coloquialmente.
mistakes: If I was the boss, I would change things. (formalmente) → If I were the boss…
I wish I was taller. (formalmente) → I wish I were taller.
Imagine he was here. → Imagine he were here. — subjuntivo.

adv_u86.text: Unidad 86 – Si no y a menos que; si y si
adv_u86.note: A menos que llueva / No sé si
adv_u86.exp:
Unit 86 — if not, unless; if vs. whether.

Fenómeno: conjunciones a menudo confundidas con restricciones semánticas distintas.

Unless vs. if not:
  • Unless = except if, presenta la única excepción posible: I won't go unless you come. — No iré a menos que vengas.
  • No se puede sustituir unless si se trata de «si no ocurre X» en subjuntivo: I'd be upset if you didn't come, no unless you came.
If vs. whether:
  • Whether en las preguntas indirectas (alternativa a if, más formal): I don't know whether/if he'll come.
  • Tras preposición: solo whether: It depends on whether…
  • Ante to-inf: solo whether: I'm not sure whether to go.
  • Con or not contiguo: solo whether…or not: whether or not she comes.
  • En función de sujeto: solo whether: Whether he comes is unclear.
Ejemplos:
  • Unless it rains, we'll have a picnic.
  • The question is whether they will agree.
Contraste: la doble negación con unless es un error: unless you don't come.
adv_u86.exc: Unless = except if; no lo uses con casos poco probables o irreales: Unless I were you → If I weren't you.
mistakes: Unless you don't come → Unless you come. — unless ya es negativo.
I don't know if or not he will come. → I don't know whether or not he will come.
Whether you like, tell me. → Whether you like it or not, tell me.

adv_u87.text: Unidad 87 - Cómo combinar partes de una oración compleja
adv_u87.note: Sin embargo / por lo tanto / además / en contraste
adv_u87.exp:
Unit 87 — conectores y organización del texto.

Fenómeno: los connectors garantizan la cohesión lógica del texto: clave en el estilo académico y profesional.

Grupos funcionales:
  • Adición: moreover, furthermore, in addition, besides, what's more.
  • Contraposición: however, nevertheless, nonetheless, on the other hand, in contrast, conversely, on the contrary (para corregir, no significa «por otro lado»).
  • Consecuencia: therefore, consequently, as a result, thus, hence, accordingly.
  • Ejemplificación: for instance, for example, such as, namely, in particular.
  • Resumen: in short, in brief, to sum up, overall, in conclusion.
  • Tiempo: meanwhile, subsequently, previously, simultaneously.
Puntuación: los connectors suelen llevar coma detrás y punto y coma o punto delante: It rained; however, we went out.

Contraste: on the contrary (refutación) ≠ on the other hand (punto de vista alternativo). Besides es más coloquial que moreover. Thus y hence son formales y típicos de la prosa científica.
adv_u87.exc: However / therefore / moreover unen oraciones con ; o con punto: It rained; however, we went out.
mistakes: It rained, however we went out. → It rained; however, we went out.
Therefore of the rain → Because of the rain / Therefore, we stayed in.
In contrary → On the contrary / In contrast.

adv_u88.text: Unidad 88 – Preposiciones de lugar y movimiento
adv_u88.note: A lo largo / a través / pasado / a través - diferencias sutiles
adv_u88.exp:
Unit 88 — preposiciones de lugar y de movimiento.

Fenómeno: diferencias sutiles entre preposiciones parecidas, decisivas para la precisión descriptiva.

Pares:
  • Across: atravesando una superficie, de un borde a otro: walk across the street/field.
  • Through: a través de un espacio tridimensional cerrado por los lados: walk through the forest/tunnel/crowd.
  • Along: a lo largo de un objeto lineal: walk along the river/road.
  • Past: por delante de: walk past the shop.
  • Towards: en dirección a (no necesariamente alcanzándolo): walk towards the door.
  • Over: por encima, salvando un obstáculo: jump over the fence / fly over the city.
  • Under / beneath / below: matices distintos de posición.
  • Against: físicamente contra o metafóricamente «en contra».
Ejemplos:
  • The dog ran across the lawn. — El perro cruzó corriendo el césped.
  • The river flows through the valley.
Contraste: above/over: posición por encima; over puede significar también «cruzando un obstáculo», mientras que above es solo posición estática.
adv_u88.exc: Across = atravesando una superficie; through = por dentro de un espacio tridimensional.
mistakes: Walk across the forest → Walk through the forest.
Swim through the river → Swim across the river.
Pass by the bridge (si es por el puente) → Pass over the bridge.

adv_u89.note: Entre dos / entre muchos - y excepciones
adv_u89.exp:
Unit 89 — betweenamong.

Fenómeno: no es la regla mecánica «dos/tres+», sino la lógica de relaciones nítidas frente a un grupo indefinido.

Reglas:
  • Between: con participantes claramente delimitados e individualizados (incluso 3+): a treaty between the three countries, choose between five options.
  • Among: con un grupo difuso de 3+ o cuando el foco está en pertenecer al conjunto: among the crowd, among friends.
  • Amongst: variante literaria de among.
Expresiones: between you and me (entre nosotros), among other things (entre otras cosas), between a rock and a hard place (entre la espada y la pared), divide X between Y and Z.

Ejemplos:
  • The disagreement between the three partners… — El desacuerdo entre los tres socios.
  • She was among the finalists. — Estaba entre los finalistas.
Contraste: divide between (a cada uno personalmente) vs distribute among (repartir en el grupo). Tras between: caso objeto: between you and me, no between you and I.
adv_u89.exc: Among se usa también con dos si forman parte de un grupo: among friends.
mistakes: Between three people → Among three people (habitualmente), pero between para pares claros.
Divide among the two of them → Divide between the two of them.
Among you and me → Between you and me.

adv_u90.note: Al mediodía / para entonces / a lo largo de los años / a lo largo
adv_u90.exp:
Unit 90 — preposiciones de tiempo en C1.

Fenómeno: conjunto ampliado de preposiciones temporales con matices sutiles de duración y límites.

Preposiciones avanzadas:
  • Over: a lo largo de (con énfasis en el proceso): over the last decade, over the years.
  • Throughout: en toda la extensión, sin excepción: throughout history, throughout the meeting.
  • Within: dentro de (no más tarde de): within 24 hours, within a week.
  • By: para el momento en que (plazo): by Friday.
  • Until / till: hasta el momento (duración): stay until 5.
  • Towards / toward: cerca del final del periodo: towards the end of May.
  • Prior to: antes de (formal): prior to the meeting.
  • Following: después de (formal): following the announcement.
Ejemplos:
  • The report must be submitted within two weeks.
  • Support grew steadily throughout the campaign.
Contraste: by Friday (como fecha límite) vs until Friday (duración). In + periodo (in 2023) vs on + día (on Monday) vs at + punto (at noon).
adv_u90.exc: By = para un momento concreto; until = hasta ese momento (proceso). stay by 5 → stay until 5.
mistakes: Finish until Friday. → Finish by Friday.
In Monday → On Monday.
At the morning → In the morning.

adv_u91.text: Unidad 91 - La preposición excepto y sus variantes
adv_u91.note: Excepto / excepto por / aparte de / pero
adv_u91.exp:
Unit 91 — except y variantes de exclusión.

Fenómeno: diferencias entre except, except for, apart from, but, besides.

Diferencias:
  • Except (for): exclusión: Everyone came except (for) Tom. Except for es preferible al inicio de la oración y tras sustantivos generales.
  • Apart from: doble función: (1) exclusión = except for; (2) adición = in addition to: Apart from being expensive, it's slow.
  • But con el sentido de «excepto»: tras all, every, no, nobody, nothing, anybody: nobody but me, nothing but the truth.
  • Besides: adición (no exclusión): Besides Tom, Anna also came. (Tom vino Y Anna también.)
Ejemplos:
  • All the shops were open except (for) the bakery. — Todos los comercios estaban abiertos excepto la panadería.
  • Apart from English, she speaks French. — Además del inglés, habla francés.
Contraste: except (excluye) vs besides (añade): confusión típica. Las formas except of, except from son erróneas.
adv_u91.exc: Except for suele ir al principio; except en posición media tras generalizadores como all, every, no.
mistakes: Everyone except of Tom → Everyone except (for) Tom.
Except he is late, he is nice. → Apart from being late, he is nice.
Nobody but me knew. (coloquialmente «but me» es aceptable) — en registro formal: except (for) me.

adv_u92.exp:
Unit 92 — preposiciones tras verbos.

Fenómeno: los verbos rigen determinadas preposiciones; cambiarlas modifica el significado.

Verbos con preposición variable:
  • Agree with (con alguien) / agree to (una propuesta) / agree on (algo concreto): I agree with you / I agreed to the plan / We agreed on Friday.
  • Talk to (con quién) / talk about (de qué) / talk over (tratar algo).
  • Charge with (acusar) / charge for (cobrar por).
  • Apply to (dirigirse a) / apply for (solicitar).
  • Consist of / in / with: distintos significados.
Verbos sin preposición (error típico: añadir una de más): discuss, enter, marry, approach, lack, reach, contact, answer, attack, attend, resemble: discuss the issue, no discuss about.

Ejemplos:
  • She applied for the position.
  • The success depends on effort.
Contraste: listen to (requiere preposición), pero hear (sin preposición). Explain to me, no explain me: to obligatorio.
adv_u92.exc: Discuss / enter / marry / approach no requieren preposición: discuss about → discuss.
mistakes: Explain me this. → Explain this to me.
Listen music → Listen to music.
Depend of → Depend on.

adv_u93.note: Efecto sobre / causa de / excepción a / actitud hacia
adv_u93.exp:
Unit 93 — preposiciones tras sustantivos.

Fenómeno: los sustantivos forman colocaciones fijas con preposiciones, especialmente importantes en el inglés escrito de nivel C1.

Colocaciones:
  • Reason for, cause of, effect on, impact on, influence on.
  • Solution to, answer to, response to, approach to, key to.
  • Increase in, decrease in, change in, rise in, fall in.
  • Attitude to(wards), reaction to, objection to.
  • Lack of, shortage of, absence of, advantage of, disadvantage of.
  • Exception to, alternative to, access to.
  • Difference between, contrast between, connection between.
  • Relationship with, connection with, problem with, trouble with.
Tras la preposición: sustantivo o gerundio: the reason for postponing the meeting.

Ejemplos:
  • There has been a significant increase in applications.
  • We need a solution to this problem.
Contraste: muchos pares de la lengua materna se traducen con una sola preposición, pero en inglés difieren: español «razón de X» → reason for X, no reason of X.
adv_u93.exc: Reason for / solution to / attitude towards / increase in: colocaciones fijas.
mistakes: The reason of the delay → The reason for the delay.
Solution of the problem → Solution to the problem.
Attitude to him (aceptable) / attitude towards him: equivalentes; attitude against es error.

adv_u94.text: Unidad 94 – Verbos compuestos (dos y tres palabras)
adv_u94.note: Verbos compuestos: tres componentes e inseparables
adv_u94.exp:
Unit 94 — phrasal verbs de dos y tres componentes.

Fenómeno: combinaciones idiomáticas de verbo y partícula(s), a menudo con significado opaco.

Tipos:
  • Intransitivos (sin complemento): break down, take off, come round.
  • Transitivos separables: con sustantivo antes o después de la partícula, con pronombre solo entre ambos: turn the TV on / turn on the TV / turn it on, no turn on it.
  • Transitivos inseparables: look after (the kids), come across (a book), get over (illness).
  • De tres palabras (siempre inseparables): look forward to, come up with, put up with, run out of, get away with, make up for, catch up with, stand up for.
Tras un phrasal de tres componentes: sustantivo o gerundio: I look forward to seeing you.

Registro: los phrasal verbs son coloquiales; sus equivalentes formales suelen ser de origen latino: put off = postpone, look into = investigate, find out = discover.

Contraste: get over (recuperarse) vs get on with (llevarse bien). El C1 exige dominio activo de más de 200 phrasal verbs.
adv_u94.exc: Los phrasal verbs de tres componentes son inseparables: put up with him, no put him up with.
mistakes: Look after for the kids → Look after the kids.
I put up with it him → I put up with him.
Turn the TV on it → Turn it on. — el pronombre va entre el verbo y la partícula.

adv_u95.note: Hay + estructuras extendidas en texto académico.
adv_u95.exp:
Unit 95 — construcciones con there is/was/has been.

Fenómeno: there como sujeto formal, herramienta central para presentar información nueva.

Modelos básicos: There is/are + SN + lugar/tiempo: There is a problem with the system.

Construcciones avanzadas de C1:
  • There appears/seems to be: There seems to be a misunderstanding.
  • There is said/believed/thought/known to be: There are believed to be several causes.
  • There is bound/likely/certain to be: There is likely to be opposition.
  • There tends to be / there happens to be / there used to be.
  • Expresiones fijas: There is no point in doing, there is no need to, there is no way, there is no knowing/telling.
Concordancia: con el sustantivo más cercano: There is a book and two pens.

Ejemplos:
  • There's no point in arguing. — No tiene sentido discutir.
  • There have been several complaints.
Contraste: there presenta existencia; it se usa para remitir a una cláusula: It is reported that…, no There is reported…
adv_u95.exc: There is + sing.; there are + pl. En la lengua coloquial: there's three of us (no estándar).
mistakes: It is a book on the table. → There is a book on the table.
There is many people. → There are many people.
There has been reported that… → It has been reported that… — para reports se usa it.

adv_u96.note: Está claro que / Se decidió que
adv_u96.exp:
Unit 96 — it formal (lección 1).

Fenómeno: it como sujeto ficticio que permite trasladar una cláusula pesada al final: clave del estilo objetivo e impersonal.

Construcciones:
  • It + be + adj + cláusula con that: It is clear/obvious/important/likely that he's right.
  • It + be + adj + to-inf: It is important to consider all options.
  • It + be + V3 + cláusula con that: It is reported/said/believed/assumed that…
  • It + was + decided/agreed/arranged + that (a menudo con should): It was agreed that all members should attend.
  • It + takes + tiempo + to-inf: It takes effort to learn a language.
Función: desplaza el foco hacia la acción/valoración y evita un sujeto engorroso.

Ejemplos:
  • It is widely acknowledged that climate change is accelerating.
  • It is essential that everyone be informed.
Registro: base del estilo académico y periodístico. Contraste: It is (con cláusula) vs There is (existencia).
adv_u96.exc: It como sujeto formal con cláusula con that / to-inf. No confundir con el it referencial.
mistakes: Is clear that he is right. → It is clear that he is right.
That he lied is obvious (formalmente). → It is obvious that he lied.
It was decided he to go. → It was decided that he should go.

adv_u97.exp:
Unit 97 — it formal (lección 2): verbos copulativos.

Fenómeno: grupo de verbos que presentan inferencias, suposiciones y casualidades en registro formal.

Modelos:
  • It seems/appears + (cláusula con that / as if / as though): It seems that he was right / It seems as if nobody cared.
  • It turns out / it emerges / it transpires + that: It turns out she was lying. — Resulta que mentía.
  • It happens / so happens that: As it happens, I know her personally.
  • It follows that / it means that / it implies that: inferencias.
  • Con to-inf: He seems to be tired / He appears to know the answer.
Ejemplos:
  • It would seem that the plan has failed. (con matización) — Parece que el plan ha fracasado.
  • It so happens that I have the key. — Da la casualidad de que tengo la llave.
Registro: it transpires/emerges: muy formal; it turns out: neutro.

Contraste: He seems to be tired (sujeto directo) vs It seems (that) he is tired (con it formal): equivalentes pero con estructura distinta.
adv_u97.exc: It seems/appears + cláusula con that o to-inf: He seems to be tired / It seems that he is tired.
mistakes: It seems he to be late. → He seems to be late / It seems he is late.
It turns that he lied. → It turns out that he lied.
It happens me to know him. → I happen to know him.

adv_u98.text: Unidad 98 - Cláusulas it y cláusulas what
adv_u98.note: Oraciones hendidas: Lo que necesito es... / Fue Tom quien...
adv_u98.exp:
Unit 98 — oraciones hendidas (cleft sentences).

Fenómeno: destacar información clave reformateando la oración: un poderoso recurso retórico.

Tipos:
  • It-cleft: It + be + elemento focalizado + cláusula con that/who: It was Maria who called me (no otro); It was yesterday that I saw him (precisamente ayer); It is precision that matters.
  • Wh-cleft (pseudo-cleft): What/Where/Why + cláusula + be + foco: What I need is a break. What she did was (to) resign.
  • Reversed wh-cleft: A break is what I need.
  • All-cleft: All I want is peace. All you have to do is call.
Ejemplos:
  • It wasn't the money that upset him — it was the principle.
  • What we need now is a new strategy.
Función: foco contrastivo, refutación, énfasis.

Contraste: el base Maria called me es neutro; It was Maria who called me pone el acento en Maria. La inversión contraria es rara.
adv_u98.exc: Construcciones hendidas: What I need is rest / It was Tom who broke it.
mistakes: <s>What I need is to rest and to eat</s> es aceptable, pero cuida el paralelismo.
It was Tom that did broke it. → It was Tom who broke it.
What I want is you come. → What I want is for you to come.

adv_u99.note: Nunca lo he visto / Sólo entonces me di cuenta
adv_u99.exp:
Unit 99 — inversión (lección 1).

Fenómeno: orden invertido sujeto-auxiliar tras un elemento negativo o restrictivo antepuesto: marca de registro formal alto.

Detonantes de la inversión:
  • Adverbios negativos o semi-negativos: Never, Rarely, Seldom, Hardly, Scarcely, Little, Nowhere.
  • Only + adverbial: Only then, only after, only by, only when, only if.
  • Not only…(but also).
  • At no time, under no circumstances, in no way, on no account.
Estructura: Elemento detonador + aux + sujeto + verbo principal (como en la pregunta): Never have I seen such chaos.

Ejemplos:
  • Rarely does one find such dedication. — Rara vez se encuentra semejante dedicación.
  • Only after the meeting did I understand. — Solo tras la reunión lo comprendí.
  • Not only did he arrive late, but he also forgot the papers.
Registro: literario, oratorio, académico. En el habla coloquial suena arcaico o muy enfático.

Contraste: I have never seen it (neutro) vs Never have I seen it (enfático).
adv_u99.exc: Inversión con adverbios negativos: Never have I seen / Only then did I realise. Orden de pregunta.
mistakes: Never I have seen such a thing. → Never have I seen such a thing.
Only then I realised. → Only then did I realise.
Rarely he comes. (formalmente) → Rarely does he come.

el_u01.text: Unidad 1 – Soy, es, son y Presente simple
el_u01.note: soy / es / son · yo trabajo, ella trabaja
el_u01.exp:
Unit 1 — el verbo to be en presente y la forma básica del Present Simple. Se usa para describir hechos, cualidades y acciones habituales.

Estructura:
  • I am / he, she, it is / we, you, they are
  • Present Simple: I/you/we/they work, he/she/it works (+ terminación -s/-es)
Ejemplos:
  • 'I am a student.' — Soy estudiante.
  • 'She is my sister.' — Ella es mi hermana.
  • 'They work in a bank.' — Trabajan en un banco.
  • 'He speaks French.' — Él habla francés.
Formas cortas: I'm, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're. To be describe estado/pertenencia, y Present Simple — acciones regulares.
el_u01.exc: El pronombre I siempre se combina solo con am, no con is/are. Formas cortas: I'm, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're. En el habla coloquial am not no se contrae (no existe la forma amn't), se usa I'm not.
mistakes: I is a student. → I am a student. — con I siempre am.
She work every day. → She works every day. — en 3ª persona del singular se añade -s.
He are tired. → He is tired. — con he/she/it se usa is.
They is happy. → They are happy. — con we/you/they se usa are.

el_u02.exp:
Unit 2 — preguntas y negaciones con el verbo to be. En la pregunta am/is/are se coloca delante del sujeto (inversión); en la negación se añade not.

Estructura:
  • ? Am/Is/Are + sujeto + …?
  • — sujeto + am/is/are + not …
  • Respuestas cortas: Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
Ejemplos:
  • 'Is she a teacher?' — ¿Es ella profesora?
  • 'Are you tired?' — ¿Estás cansado?
  • 'I am not ready.' — No estoy listo.
  • 'Where are the keys?' — ¿Dónde están las llaves?
Contracciones: isn't, aren't. Tras las palabras interrogativas (what, where, who) va inmediatamente is/are.
el_u02.exc: En respuestas cortas negativas se usan las contracciones isn't/aren't, pero en las afirmativas no se usan contracciones: Yes, I am (no Yes, I'm). Con am no hay forma negativa corta — se dice I'm not.
mistakes: You are a doctor? → Are you a doctor? — en la pregunta are va antes del sujeto.
Yes, I'm. → Yes, I am. — en la respuesta corta afirmativa no se usa contracción.
Where she is? → Where is she? — tras la palabra interrogativa va inmediatamente is/are.
Is they ready? → Are they ready? — con they se necesita are.

el_u03.exp:
Unit 3 — Present Continuous para acciones que ocurren en el momento del habla o en el período actual.

Fórmula: am/is/are + V-ing.
Marcadores: now, at the moment, right now, Look!, Listen!

Ejemplos:
  • 'I am reading a book now.' — Estoy leyendo un libro ahora.
  • 'She is cooking dinner.' — Ella está preparando la cena.
  • 'The children are playing in the garden.' — Los niños están jugando en el jardín.
  • 'Look! It is raining.' — ¡Mira! Está lloviendo.
Ortografía del -ing: write→writing (se omite la e), sit→sitting (duplicación de consonante), lie→lying (ie→y). También se usa para situaciones temporales: I am staying with my aunt this week.
el_u03.exc: Los verbos de estado (know, like, want, love, understand, need, believe) normalmente no se usan en Continuous. Al añadir -ing: lie→lying, die→dying (ie→y); sit→sitting, run→running (duplicación de consonante); write→writing (se omite -e).
mistakes: I am knowing the answer. → I know the answer. — los verbos de estado no se usan en Continuous.
She is writeing a letter. → She is writing a letter. — antes de -ing se omite -e.
He running now. → He is running now. — se necesita el auxiliar is.
They are siting here. → They are sitting here. — la consonante final se duplica.

el_u04.text: Unidad 4 - Preguntas en el presente continuo
el_u04.exp:
Unit 4 — preguntas y negaciones en Present Continuous. El auxiliar am/is/are se antepone.

Estructura:
  • ? Am/Is/Are + sujeto + V-ing …?
  • — sujeto + am/is/are + not + V-ing …
  • Pregunta especial: Wh- + am/is/are + sujeto + V-ing?
Ejemplos:
  • 'Are you working now?' — Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
  • 'What is she doing?' — ¿Qué está haciendo ella?
  • 'Where are the kids playing?' — ¿Dónde están jugando los niños?
  • 'He isn't sleeping.' — Él no está durmiendo.
A diferencia del Simple, aquí no hay auxiliar do — el papel lo cumple el propio be.
el_u04.exc: En preguntas con verbos de estado no se usa el Continuous: Do you know? (no Are you knowing?). En respuestas cortas la contracción solo en la forma negativa: Yes, I am / No, I'm not.
mistakes: What you are doing? → What are you doing? — el auxiliar va antes del sujeto.
Where he is going? → Where is he going? — el orden directo de palabras no es válido en la pregunta.
Are you knowing him? → Do you know him? — know no se usa en Continuous.
Yes, I'm. → Yes, I am. — en la respuesta corta afirmativa sin contracción.

el_u05.exp:
Unit 5 — Present Simple para acciones regulares, hábitos, hechos generales y horarios.

Fórmula: I/you/we/they + V1; he/she/it + V + -s/-es.
Marcadores: always, usually, often, sometimes, never, every day, on Mondays.

Ejemplos:
  • 'I drink coffee every morning.' — Bebo café cada mañana.
  • 'She works in a hospital.' — Ella trabaja en un hospital.
  • 'Water boils at 100 °C.' — El agua hierve a 100 °C.
  • 'The train leaves at 6 p.m.' — El tren sale a las 18:00.
Ortografía de la 3ª persona: tras -s/-sh/-ch/-x/-o → -es (watches, goes); tras consonante + y → -ies (study→studies).
el_u05.exc: Formas especiales de la 3ª persona: go→goes, do→does, have→has. Tras -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o → -es: watches, washes, goes. Tras consonante + y → -ies: study→studies, pero play→plays (tras vocal).
mistakes: She have a car. → She has a car. — en 3ª persona have se convierte en has.
He studys English. → He studies English. — tras consonante y cambia a ies.
My brother go to school. → My brother goes to school. — tras o se necesita -es.
She watch TV every evening. → She watches TV every evening. — tras -ch se añade -es.

el_u06.text: Unidad 6 - Negaciones en Presente Simple (No lo hago)
el_u06.note: No me gusta. Ella no trabaja.
el_u06.exp:
Unit 6 — negaciones en Present Simple con ayuda de don't / doesn't.

Fórmula:
  • I/you/we/they + do not (don't) + V1
  • he/she/it + does not (doesn't) + V1
Tras doesn't el verbo principal pierde la terminación -s.

Ejemplos:
  • 'I don't like fish.' — No me gusta el pescado.
  • 'She doesn't speak Chinese.' — Ella no habla chino.
  • 'They don't live here.' — No viven aquí.
  • 'My brother doesn't work on Sundays.' — Mi hermano no trabaja los domingos.
A diferencia de to be, los verbos léxicos requieren el auxiliar do/does en la negación.
el_u06.exc: Tras doesn't/don't el verbo principal va solo en infinitivo — sin -s y sin forma de pasado. En el habla coloquial se usan formas contraídas; do not/does not completas suenan formales o enfáticas.
mistakes: She doesn't works here. → She doesn't work here. — tras doesn't — infinitivo.
I no like coffee. → I don't like coffee. — la negación se construye con don't.
He don't know. → He doesn't know. — con he/she/it — doesn't.
They doesn't speak English. → They don't speak English. — con they se necesita don't.

el_u07.exp:
Unit 7 — preguntas en Present Simple con do / does.

Fórmula:
  • ? Do/Does + sujeto + V1 …?
  • Pregunta especial: Wh- + do/does + sujeto + V1?
  • Respuesta corta: Yes, I do. / No, she doesn't.
Ejemplos:
  • 'Do you speak English?' — ¿Hablas inglés?
  • 'Does he like music?' — ¿Le gusta la música?
  • 'Where do they live?' — ¿Dónde viven?
  • 'What does she do?' — ¿A qué se dedica?
Excepción: cuando who/what es sujeto, el auxiliar no es necesario: Who lives here?
el_u07.exc: Tras does el verbo principal va en infinitivo sin -s. Con who/what como sujeto el auxiliar no es necesario: Who lives here? (no Who does live here?).
mistakes: Does she works? → Does she work? — tras does — infinitivo.
Where she lives? → Where does she live? — se necesita el auxiliar does.
Do he like tea? → Does he like tea? — con he — does.
Yes, I like. → Yes, I do. — en la respuesta corta se repite el auxiliar.

el_u08.text: Unidad 8 - Presente simple versus presente continuo
el_u08.note: Trabajo todos los días. Estoy trabajando ahora.
el_u08.exp:
Unit 8 — comparación entre Present Simple y Present Continuous.

Simple — acciones regulares/permanentes, hechos: always, usually, every day.
Continuous — en el momento del habla o situación temporal: now, at the moment, today, this week.

Ejemplos:
  • 'I usually drive to work, but today I'm taking the bus.' — Normalmente voy al trabajo en coche, pero hoy voy en autobús.
  • 'She speaks three languages.' (hecho) — Habla tres idiomas.
  • 'She is speaking to her boss.' (ahora) — Está hablando con su jefe.
Los verbos de estado (know, like, want, need, believe, love) se usan solo en Simple, incluso si la situación es «ahora».
el_u08.exc: Algunos verbos cambian de significado en Continuous: I think (opino) / I'm thinking (reflexiono); I have (tengo) / I'm having lunch (almuerzo); I see (veo) / I'm seeing him (me veo con él).
mistakes: I am knowing her. → I know her. — verbo de estado — en Simple.
Look! He runs. → Look! He is running. — la acción ocurre ahora mismo.
She is going to school every day. → She goes to school every day. — acción regular en Simple.
I am wanting a coffee. → I want a coffee. — want no se usa en Continuous.

el_u09.exp:
Unit 9 — have got y have con el significado de posesión (pertenencia, parentesco, enfermedades, apariencia).

Fórmula:
  • BrE: I've got / she's got; ? Have you got…?; — I haven't got…
  • AmE: I have / she has; ? Do you have…?; — I don't have…
Ejemplos:
  • 'I've got two sisters.' / 'I have two sisters.' — Tengo dos hermanas.
  • 'She's got blue eyes.' — Ella tiene ojos azules.
  • 'Have you got a pen?' — ¿Tienes un bolígrafo?
  • 'He doesn't have a car.' — Él no tiene coche.
En pasado solo had (sin got): I had a bike as a child.
el_u09.exc: En inglés americano have got casi no se usa. En pasado solo had (no hay had got en este sentido): I had a car in 2010. Con have en el sentido de acciones (have breakfast) no se añade got.
mistakes: Do you have got a car? → Have you got a car? o Do you have a car? — no se pueden mezclar construcciones.
I haven't a car. → I don't have a car. o I haven't got a car.
She has got breakfast at 8. → She has breakfast at 8. — have got = posesión, no acción.
He has got a headache yesterday. → He had a headache yesterday. — en pasado solo had.

el_u10.exp:
Unit 10 — was / were: pasado del verbo to be.

Fórmula:
  • I / he / she / it + was
  • you / we / they + were
  • — wasn't / weren't; ? Was/Were + sujeto…?
Ejemplos:
  • 'I was at home yesterday.' — Estuve en casa ayer.
  • 'They were very tired.' — Estaban muy cansados.
  • 'Was she at the party?' — ¿Estuvo ella en la fiesta?
  • 'We weren't ready.' — No estábamos listos.
Marcadores: yesterday, last week, two days ago, in 2010, when I was a child.
el_u10.exc: En oraciones corteses se mantiene were con I/he/she: If I were you…. En todos los demás casos was para I/he/she/it. Negativas cortas: wasn't, weren't.
mistakes: I were at home. → I was at home. — con I en pasado — was.
They was tired. → They were tired. — con they — were.
Was you there? → Were you there? — con you siempre were.
She weren't happy. → She wasn't happy. — con she — wasn't.

el_u100.exp:
Unit 100 — Second Conditional: condición irreal o poco probable en presente/futuro.

Expresa una situación imaginaria o hipotética contraria a la realidad.

Estructura: If + Past Simple, would + infinitivo sin to.

Importante: Past Simple aquí es un pasado «subjuntivo», no un pasado real.

  • If I had a million dollars, I would travel the world. — Si tuviera un millón, viajaría por el mundo. (En realidad no hay dinero.)
  • If I were you, I would apologise. — Yo en tu lugar me disculparía.
  • What would you do if you saw a ghost?

Con el verbo be — formalmente were para todas las personas: If he were here, he would help. En habla coloquial se admite was, pero fijo solo If I were you.

En la principal en vez de would — could, might: If I had time, I could help you.

Diferencia con First Conditional: First — posibilidad real (If it rains, I'll stay); Second — hipótesis, poco probable (If I had wings, I would fly).
el_u100.exc: En estilo formal were para todas las personas: If I were you. En conversacional — was para I/he/she: If I was rich. Fijo solo If I were you.
mistakes: If I would have money → If I had money — would solo en la principal.
If I am rich I would travel → If I were/was rich, I would travel — Past en la condición.
If he had time, he will help → he would help — concordancia de tiempos.
If she was you (formal) → If she were you.

el_u101.note: el hombre que llamó / el libro que leí
el_u101.exp:
Unit 101 — oraciones de relativo especificativas (defining relative clauses) con who y that/which.

La oración de relativo precisa de qué persona u objeto se habla. Va justo tras el sustantivo.

Elección del pronombre:

  • who — para personas: The woman who called is my aunt. — La mujer que llamó es mi tía.
  • that — para personas y cosas: The man that lives next door. The book that I bought.
  • which — solo para cosas: The car which I bought is red.

Omisión del pronombre:

  • Si es sujeto de la subordinada (le sigue directamente el verbo), no se puede omitir: The man who called me.
  • Si es objeto (le sigue un nuevo sujeto + verbo), se puede omitir: The book (that) I read was interesting.

Sin repetir sujeto/objeto: no vale the man who he called — correcto the man who called.

Ejemplos: I have a friend who speaks five languages. The film (which) we saw yesterday was boring.
el_u101.exc: Si el pronombre relativo es objeto, puede omitirse: the book (that) I read. Si es sujeto, no se omite: the man who called, no the man called me.
mistakes: the man which called → the man who/that called — para personas.
the book who I read → the book that/which I read — para cosas.
the woman who she lives here → the woman who lives here — sin repetir sujeto.
the film that I saw it → the film that I saw — sin repetir objeto.

el_u102.note: el lugar donde / el día cuando / la razón por la cual
el_u102.exp:
Unit 102 — ampliación de las oraciones de relativo: where, when, why, whose.

  • where — de lugar: This is the hotel where we stayed. — Este es el hotel donde nos alojamos.
  • when — de tiempo: I remember the day when we met. — Recuerdo el día en que nos conocimos.
  • why — de causa: Tell me the reason why you're late. A menudo se omite: the reason (why) I called.
  • whose — pertenencia (para personas y cosas): The woman whose car was stolen called the police. The book whose cover is red.

Equivalentes con preposición (formales): the house where I live = the house in which I live; the day when we met = the day on which we met.

Las reglas de omisión son iguales a las del Unit 101: objeto se omite, sujeto no.

Distinción: whose ≠ who's (who is / who has). The man whose bag was stolen (cuyo bolso) vs The man who's here (que está aquí).

Ejemplos: I visited the village where my grandmother was born. That's the woman whose son won the prize.
el_u102.exc: Whose se usa con personas y con cosas: the book whose cover is torn. Where puede sustituirse por in/at which en estilo formal: the house in which she lives.
mistakes: the place which I was born → the place where I was born.
the day which we met (de tiempo) → the day when we met.
the man who's car → the man whose car — who's = who is.
the reason because → the reason why / the reason (that).

el_u103.text: Unidad 103 – Preposiciones de tiempo: at, on, in
el_u103.note: a las 17h / el lunes / en marzo / en 2020
el_u103.exp:
Unit 103 — preposiciones de tiempo at, on, in.

Distribución por unidades de tiempo:

  • at — hora exacta, puntos: at 7 o'clock, at noon, at midnight, at night, at the weekend (BrE), at Christmas, at Easter, at the moment.
  • on — días y fechas: on Monday, on 5 May, on my birthday, on Christmas Day, on New Year's Eve, on a rainy day, on the morning of 1 May.
  • in — periodos: meses, años, siglos, estaciones, partes del día: in January, in 2024, in the 21st century, in summer, in the morning / afternoon / evening.

Excepciones importantes:

  • at night, pero in the morning / afternoon / evening.
  • at the weekend (BrE) vs on the weekend (AmE).
  • Ante this, last, next, every, all la preposición se omite: see you next Monday, last summer, every day, all day.

Sentido de in = «dentro de» (en el futuro): I'll be back in an hour. — Vuelvo en una hora.

Ejemplos: We met on Friday at 6 o'clock in the evening.
el_u103.exc: No hay preposición ante this/that/next/last/every: see you next Monday (no on next Monday). At night, pero in the night (durante la noche).
mistakes: in Monday → on Monday — días de la semana.
at March → in March — meses.
on 2020 → in 2020 — años.
on next week → next week — sin preposición.

el_u104.note: de 9 a 5 / hasta el viernes / desde el lunes / durante 3 horas
el_u104.exp:
Unit 104 — preposiciones de periodo: from … to, until, since, for.

from A to/till B — marca un intervalo:

  • I work from 9 to 5. — Trabajo de 9 a 5.
  • The shop is open from Monday to Friday.

until / till — «hasta cierto momento» (la acción continúa y acaba):

  • Wait until Friday. — Espera hasta el viernes.
  • I'll be here till 6.

since — «desde un momento» (punto de partida hasta el presente); normalmente con Present Perfect:

  • I've lived here since 2010. — Vivo aquí desde 2010.
  • She has been sick since Monday.

for — «durante» (duración); con cualquier tiempo:

  • I lived there for five years. — Viví allí cinco años.
  • We've been waiting for two hours.

Contraste clave since vs for: since + momento (since Monday, since 2020, since I was a child); for + periodo (for two days, for a long time, for ages).

Afines: by + momento = «no más tarde de» (by Friday); before + momento = «antes de» (before lunch).
el_u104.exc: Since + momento temporal (since Monday), for + periodo (for two days). Until/till son intercambiables; by — para un momento (by Friday = no más tarde).
mistakes: since two hours → for two hours — duración.
for Monday → since Monday — punto de partida.
I'll wait till to Friday → until Friday / till Friday — sin to.
from 9 till to 5 → from 9 to 5 / from 9 till 5.

el_u105.note: antes del almuerzo / después del trabajo / durante la película
el_u105.exp:
Unit 105 — before, after, during, while para relacionar eventos en el tiempo.

before / after pueden ser preposiciones y conjunciones:

  • Preposición + sust./gerundio: after dinner, before leaving.
  • Conjunción + subordinada: before you leave, after we had finished.
  • En subordinadas de tiempo — Present en vez de Future: Call me before you leave (no before you will leave).

during — solo preposición, ante sustantivo («durante algo»):

  • during the film, during the holidays, during lunch.
  • I fell asleep during the lecture.

while — solo conjunción, ante subordinada con sujeto y verbo:

  • while I was watching TV, while she slept.
  • He called while I was cooking.

Contraste clave: during + sustantivo; while + oración completa. No vale during I slept → solo while I slept o during my sleep.

Diferencia during / for: during = cuándo exactamente (during the war), for = cuánto tiempo (for five years).
el_u105.exc: During + sustantivo, while + subordinada. No vale during I was reading. Cabe during the meeting o while I was in the meeting.
mistakes: during I slept → while I slept — ante verbo while.
while the film → during the film — ante sustantivo during.
after to leave → after leaving / after I leave — no infinitivo con to.
before I will go → before I go — sin will.

el_u106.text: Unidad 106 – Preposiciones de lugar: in, at, on (lección 1)
el_u106.note: en la habitación / en la puerta / sobre la mesa
el_u106.exp:
Unit 106 — preposiciones básicas de lugar in, at, on.

in — dentro de un espacio/volumen delimitado:

  • in the room, in the box, in the garden, in a car, in the water, in Paris, in the sky.
  • The keys are in my bag.

on — sobre una superficie (encima, al lado):

  • on the table, on the wall, on the floor, on the ceiling, on the right / left, on the first floor, on a bus / train / plane.
  • There's a picture on the wall.

at — en un punto, junto a un lugar concreto (sin énfasis en volumen):

  • at the door, at the bus stop, at the traffic lights, at the top / bottom, at home, at work, at the station.
  • Meet me at the entrance.

Pares contrastivos:

  • in the corner (rincón de la habitación) vs on the corner (esquina de la calle) vs at the corner (junto a la esquina).
  • in the bus (dentro) vs on the bus (viaja en autobús).
  • in bed (durmiendo) vs on the bed (sentado encima).
el_u106.exc: At home, at work, at school — sin artículo. On the bus/train, pero in the car/taxi. In bed (dormir), on the bed (sentarse encima).
mistakes: in the door → at the door — junto a la puerta, no dentro.
on the room → in the room.
in the street corner → on the corner.
at the picture on the wall → in the picture / on the wall.

el_u107.text: Unidad 107 – Preposiciones de lugar: in, at, on (lección 2)
el_u107.note: en el trabajo / en el hospital / de vacaciones
el_u107.exp:
Unit 107 — expresiones fijas con preposiciones de lugar (Unit 2).

Se memorizan en bloques — la lógica no siempre es literal.

Con at:

  • at home, at work, at school, at university, at the airport, at the station, at a concert / party / meeting.
  • at the top / bottom, at the end of (the street).

Con in:

  • in hospital, in prison, in bed (como estado).
  • in the middle, in the front / back of (the car).
  • in a photo, in a picture, in a book, in a newspaper, in a queue / row / line.

Con on:

  • on holiday, on business, on a trip / tour / cruise / journey.
  • on TV, on the radio, on the phone, on the internet, on the menu, on page 5, on a farm, on an island.

Ejemplos: He's on holiday in Spain. I saw it on TV last night. There are three people in the picture.

Pares contrastivos: in hospital (paciente, BrE) vs in the hospital (AmE / visitante); at the end (al final de algo) vs in the end (al final, al fin y al cabo).
el_u107.exc: In hospital (BrE, como paciente) vs in the hospital (AmE o como visitante). On holiday (BrE), on vacation (AmE).
mistakes: in holiday → on holiday.
at the bed (dormir) → in bed.
in work → at work.
on the end (al final) → in the end (al fin y al cabo) / at the end (al final de algo).

el_u108.text: Unidad 108 – Preposiciones de lugar: to, in, at (lección 3)
el_u108.note: ir a la escuela / llegar a Londres / llegar a la estación
el_u108.exp:
Unit 108 — preposiciones de dirección y llegada: to, in, at.

to — dirección del movimiento (adónde):

  • go to school / work / London, travel to Japan, drive to the airport, send a letter to John.

arrive — caso especial, sin to:

  • arrive in + ciudad/país/territorio grande: arrive in London, arrive in Russia.
  • arrive at + edificio/lugar concreto/evento: arrive at the station, arrive at the hotel, arrive at work, arrive at the party.

Excepciones importantes sin preposición (home — adverbio):

  • go home, come home, arrive home, get home, leave home.
  • También con here, there: come here, go there.

Ejemplos:

  • We arrived in Paris on Monday and arrived at our hotel in the afternoon.
  • She went home after work. — Se fue a casa después del trabajo.
  • I'm going to the supermarket.

El verbo get to = «llegar a»: How do you get to work? — ¿Cómo llegas al trabajo? (y aquí también get home sin to).
el_u108.exc: Arrive in + ciudad/país, arrive at + lugar/edificio. Go home — sin preposición (home como adverbio). No vale to home.
mistakes: arrive to London → arrive in London.
arrive to the station → arrive at the station.
go to home → go home.
come in Paris → come to Paris — dirección.

el_u109.text: Unidad 109 – Debajo, detrás, enfrente y otros
el_u109.note: debajo de la mesa / detrás de la puerta / frente al banco
el_u109.exp:
Unit 109 — preposiciones de posición relativa de los objetos.

  • under (debajo) / over, above (encima): The cat is under the table. The lamp hangs above the desk.
  • behind (detrás) / in front of (delante): A garden behind the house. He parked in front of the shop.
  • opposite (enfrente): The bank is opposite the post office.
  • next to / beside / by (al lado): She sat next to me.
  • between (entre dos) / among (entre varios): between you and me, among the trees.

Matices:

  • above — más arriba (no necesariamente justo encima); over — directamente encima o a través: a plane flew over the city.
  • below ↔ above; under ↔ over.
  • beside (al lado) ≠ besides (además).

Las preposiciones compuestas se escriben enteras: in front of (no in front), next to, on top of. Opposite se usa sin to: opposite the bank.

Ejemplos: There's a mirror above the sink. The cat is sleeping between the two cushions.
el_u109.exc: Between — entre dos; among — entre varios. Above (más arriba, no obligatoriamente justo encima) vs over (directamente encima / a través). Beside (al lado) ≠ besides (además).
mistakes: between the crowd → among the crowd — muchas personas.
