  • one — para sg.: I need a pen. Have you got one? — Necesito un boli. ¿Tienes uno?
  • ones — para pl.: These apples are bad. The red ones are better. — Estas manzanas están malas. Las rojas son mejores.

Combinaciones típicas:

  • Tras adjetivo: the blue one, a big one, a new one.
  • Tras this/that/which: Which one do you want? I'll take this one.
  • Tras the con complemento: the one on the table, the ones I bought yesterday.

Ejemplos: My car is old. I want a new one. — Mi coche es viejo. Quiero uno nuevo. These cups are dirty; give me clean ones. — Estas tazas están sucias; dame unas limpias.

No se usa con incontables: no vale I need water. Give me one. → Give me some. Tras my/his/her/your sin adjetivo — sin one: That's mine, no my one.
el_u75.exc: One/ones no se usa con incontables: no vale I need some water. Give me one. → Give me some. Tras my, his, her sin sustantivo — sin one: That's mine, no my one (aunque aparece en conversación).
mistakes: I want a red one shoes → I want red ones — con pl. forma ones.
This bread is better than that one → than that — incontable, one sobra.
the blue ones shirt → the blue one — one sustituye al sustantivo.
Which ones you want? → Which ones do you want? — hace falta el auxiliar.

el_u76.exp:
Unit 76 — distribución de some y any en la oración.

Regla: some — en afirmaciones y ofrecimientos corteses; any — en negaciones, preguntas y tras if.

  • Afirmación: There is some milk in the fridge.
  • Negación: There isn't any milk.
  • Pregunta: Is there any milk?
  • Ofrecimiento/petición (esperando «sí»): Would you like some tea? Can I have some water?

Los derivados siguen el mismo principio:

  • someone / somebody, something, somewhere — en afirmaciones.
  • anyone / anybody, anything, anywhere — en negaciones y preguntas.

Caso especial: any en afirmación = «cualquiera» (sin restricción de elección):

  • You can take any book you like. — Puedes coger cualquier libro.
  • Anyone can do this. — Cualquiera puede hacerlo.

Tras if normalmente any: If you have any questions, ask. — Si tienes preguntas, pregunta.
el_u76.exc: Any en afirmación = «cualquiera»: Take any book you like. En condicionales con if normalmente any: If you have any questions, ask.
mistakes: I have any money → I have some money — en afirmación some.
There isn't some bread → There isn't any bread — en negación any.
somewhere to go? No, nowhere → en pregunta anywhere: Anywhere to go?
I don't know something → I don't know anything — tras negación any-.

el_u77.note: No hay leche. Ninguno de ellos vino.
el_u77.exp:
Unit 77 — cuantificadores negativos no, none y la construcción not … any.

Tres formas de expresar «nada / ninguno»:

  • no + sustantivo (enfático): There is no time. I have no idea.
  • not … any + sustantivo (neutro): There isn't any time. I don't have any idea.
  • none — sin sustantivo, como sustituto: How much money do you have? — None. — ¿Cuánto dinero tienes? — Ninguno.

Regla clave: en inglés solo una negación en la oración. Si el verbo ya es negativo, tras él solo any, no no/none.

  • I don't have any friends here. = I have no friends here.
  • There weren't any people in the shop. = There were no people in the shop.

None of + determinante + sustantivo: None of my friends came. None of the milk was fresh. — Ninguno de mis amigos vino. Nada de la leche estaba fresca. Tras none of + pl. el verbo puede ir en sg. (formal) o pl. (coloquial).
el_u77.exc: None of + sustantivo pl. admite verbo en sg. (formal) o pl. (coloquial): None of us knows / know.
mistakes: I don't know nothing → I don't know anything / I know nothing — una sola negación.
There is none milk → There is no milk — ante sustantivo no.
No of them came → None of them came — ante of — none.
He hasn't no money → He has no money / He hasn't any money.

el_u78.note: Nadie vino. No vi a nadie.
el_u78.exp:
Unit 78 — pronombres negativos nobody / no one, nothing, nowhere y su relación con not anybody, etc.

Equivalencia:

  • nobody / no one = not anybody / not anyone
  • nothing = not anything
  • nowhere = not anywhere

Regla de una sola negación: tras nobody/nothing/nowhere el verbo va en afirmativo.

  • Nobody knows the answer. — Nadie sabe la respuesta.
  • Nothing happened yesterday. — No pasó nada ayer.
  • There is nowhere to go. — No hay adónde ir.
  • Cf.: I didn't see anybody. = I saw nobody.

Tras nobody/no one/nothing — verbo en sg., pero el pronombre de referencia suele ser they/their/them (neutro): Nobody brought their umbrella. Did anyone forget their ticket?

En las tag-questions tras sentido negativo — cola afirmativa: Nobody came, did they? — No vino nadie, ¿verdad?
el_u78.exc: Nobody/no one/nothing concuerdan con el verbo en sg., pero como pronombre de referencia suele ir they/their: Nobody brought their umbrella.
mistakes: Nobody don't like it → Nobody likes it — sin segunda negación.
I didn't see nothing → I didn't see anything / I saw nothing.
Anyone is here (como negación) → No one is here — en afirmación sobre nadie — nobody/no one.
Nothing happened, didn't it? → Nothing happened, did it? — tag afirmativa.

el_u79.note: ¿Hay alguien ahí? Cualquiera puede hacer esto.
el_u79.exp:
Unit 79 — sistema completo de pronombres indefinidos con sufijos -body / -one / -thing / -where.

Matriz de formas:

  • Personas: somebody / someone, anybody / anyone, nobody / no one, everybody / everyone.
  • Cosas: something, anything, nothing, everything.
  • Lugares: somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere.

-body y -one son intercambiables, el significado es el mismo. La elección entre some-/any-/no- sigue la regla general: afirmación — some-, negación/pregunta — any-, negación sin partícula not — no-.

Ejemplos:

  • Someone is knocking at the door. — Alguien llama a la puerta.
  • Is there anything to eat? — ¿Hay algo de comer?
  • There's nothing in the box. — No hay nada en la caja.
  • Everyone was happy at the party. — Todos estaban contentos en la fiesta.

Tras estas palabras el verbo va en singular: Everybody is here. El sentido de «cualquiera» de any- en afirmación: Anyone can learn English. — Cualquiera puede aprender inglés.
el_u79.exc: Tras somebody/anybody/nobody — verbo en sg., pero el pronombre de referencia suele ser they: Someone left their bag. En AmE: somebody y someone son intercambiables.
mistakes: Somebody are waiting → Somebody is waiting — sg.
I need nothing anywhere → I don't need anything anywhere — no mezclar sistemas.
Is there somebody? (pregunta real) → Is there anybody? — estándar para pregunta.
Nobody didn't come → Nobody came — una sola negación.

el_u80.note: todos los días / todo el día / todos
el_u80.exp:
Unit 80 — diferencia entre every y all.

Every significa «cada» con acento en las unidades individuales y se usa con sustantivo en sg. y verbo en sg.:

  • Every student has a book. — Cada estudiante tiene un libro.
  • Every day I drink coffee. — Cada día tomo café.
  • Every + derivados: everyone / everybody, everything, everywhere — también con verbo en sg.: Everyone is ready.

All significa «todos / todo» como conjunto, se usa con pl. o incont.:

  • All students must come. — Todos los estudiantes deben venir.
  • I worked all day. — Trabajé todo el día.
  • All the water was dirty. — Toda el agua estaba sucia.

Contraste: Every day (cada día, 365 veces) ≠ all day (todo el día, de la mañana a la noche). Expresiones de tiempo: every year / month / Monday y all year / month / Monday. Los derivados con every- se escriben juntos y funcionan como pronombres sg.
el_u80.exc: All day/all night/all week — sin artículo (= «todo el día»). Every day ≠ everyday (adj. «cotidiano»): an everyday routine.
mistakes: every students → every student — tras every sg.
Everyone are happy → Everyone is happy — verbo en sg.
all the day I worked → all day I worked — fijo sin the.
I go there every days → every day — sg.

el_u81.text: Unidad 81 - Todos, la mayoría, algunos, alguno, no
el_u81.note: Todos los gatos / La mayoría de las personas / Un poco de agua / Ni idea
el_u81.exp:
Unit 81 — cuantificadores all, most, some, any, no con sustantivos y con of.

Dos registros de uso:

  • Sin of — afirmaciones generales sobre la clase: All birds can fly. Most children like sweets. Some people are lucky. I have no money.
  • Con of — ante pronombre o sustantivo con determinante (the, my, this): All of us agree. Most of the students passed. Some of my friends live abroad. None of them came.

La forma no ante of es imposible — en su lugar se usa none: none of the books, none of us.

All ante the/my/this permite omitir of: all (of) my friends, all (of) the time. Con pronombre personal of es obligatorio: all of us, no all we.

Ejemplos: Some people like tea; some of my friends drink it every day. — A algunos les gusta el té; algunos de mis amigos lo beben cada día. Most animals sleep at night, but most of my cats sleep all day.
el_u81.exc: All puede ir ante the/my/this sin of: all (of) my friends. Con pronombres of es obligatorio: all of us, no all we.
mistakes: All of people → All people / All of the people — of exige determinante.
Most of students → Most students / Most of the students.
All we → All of us — con pronombre of obligatorio.
No of them → None of them — ante of la forma none.

el_u82.note: Ambas hermanas / Cualquier día / Ninguna opción
el_u82.exp:
Unit 82 — cuantificadores pareados both, either, neither para dos objetos.

Significados:

  • both — ambos / uno y otro (positivo).
  • either — cualquiera de los dos / uno u otro.
  • neither — ni uno ni otro (negativo sin not).

Formas:

  • Ante sustantivo: both sisters, either day, neither answer.
  • Con of + determinante: both of the girls, either of us, neither of them.
  • Conjunciones pareadas: both … and, either … or, neither … nor.

Concordancia del verbo:

  • Tras both — siempre pl.: Both are correct. — Los dos son correctos.
  • Tras either/neither — normalmente sg. (formal): Neither of the answers is right. En habla coloquial se admite pl.

Ejemplos: Both of my parents are teachers. — Mis dos padres son profesores. You can come on Monday or Tuesday — either day is fine. — Ven lunes o martes — cualquier día vale. Neither book was interesting. — Ninguno de los dos libros fue interesante. She speaks neither French nor German.
el_u82.exc: Neither + verbo en sg. (formal): Neither of them is right. En conversación se admite pl. Tras both el verbo solo va en pl.: Both are correct.
mistakes: Both of sisters → Both sisters / Both of the sisters.
Neither answers is right → Neither answer is right — tras neither sg.
I don't like neither → I don't like either — no doble negación.
Both my parents is → Both my parents are — pl.

el_u83.note: mucha agua / muchos amigos / mucho trabajo
el_u83.exp:
Unit 83 — cuantificadores de cantidad much, many, a lot of / lots of.

La elección depende de la contabilidad del sustantivo y del tipo de oración.

  • many + contable pl.: many books, many people, many problems.
  • much + incontable: much time, much money, much information.
  • a lot of / lots of + ambos tipos: a lot of friends, a lot of water.

Regla estilística:

  • En afirmaciones se prefiere a lot of: I have a lot of work. (much work — formal.)
  • En negaciones e interrogativas — much/many: Do you have many friends? I don't have much money.

Tras how, so, too, as — siempre much/many, no a lot of: How much is it? There are too many cars. I have so much work!

Sin sustantivo: He works a lot. Thanks a lot. Ejemplos: How many languages do you speak? — I speak three. How much time do you need? — Not much.
el_u83.exc: En afirmaciones much/many suenan formales: He has a lot of money — neutro, He has much money — inusual. Pero too much/so much/as much son normales en afirmación.
mistakes: much books → many books — contable.
many water → much water / a lot of water.
I have much friends → I have a lot of friends / many friends — en afirmación.
How many money? → How much money? — incontable.

el_u84.note: pocos amigos / poco tiempo / algunas cosas
el_u84.exp:
Unit 84 — contraste cuantitativo (a) few y (a) little.

La forma depende de la contabilidad, y el significado — de la presencia del artículo a.

  • few + cont. pl. = «pocos, casi nadie» (valoración negativa): He has few friends and feels lonely.
  • a few + cont. pl. = «unos cuantos, varios» (positivo): He has a few good friends.
  • little + incont. = «poco, insuficiente»: We have little time — hurry!
  • a little + incont. = «un poco, suficiente»: We have a little time — we can rest.

Combinaciones típicas:

  • very few / very little refuerzan «poco»: Very few people came.
  • quite a few / quite a lot of = «bastantes» (para contables).
  • only a few / only a little = «solo unos pocos / un poco».

Ejemplos: Can you lend me a little money? — ¿Me prestas un poco de dinero? I understood little of what she said. — Entendí poco de lo que dijo.
el_u84.exc: Quite a few / quite a little = «bastantes». Only a few / only a little = «solo unos pocos». Es incorrecto decir a few water — con incontables solo a little.
mistakes: a few water → a little water — incontable.
little friends (en el sentido de «pequeños») → small friends — aquí little = «pocos».
I have few money → I have little money.
There are a little chairs → a few chairs — contable.

el_u85.note: un hermoso día / el cielo es azul
el_u85.exp:
Unit 85 — adjetivos (adjectives): descripción de sustantivos.

El adjetivo nombra una cualidad y no varía en género, número ni caso: a big house, big houses, big cars.

Dos posiciones:

  • Atributiva — ante el sustantivo: a beautiful garden, an interesting book.
  • Predicativa — tras verbos copulativos be, seem, look, feel, taste, smell, become, get: The garden is beautiful. She looks tired. This tastes good.

Orden de varios adjetivos: Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose: a lovely small old round wooden dining table.

Pares -ed / -ing:

  • -ed — sentimiento: I am bored / tired / interested / excited.
  • -ing — causa del sentimiento: The film is boring. The news is exciting.

Ejemplos: I'm interested in this interesting topic. — Estoy interesado en este tema interesante. She was surprised by the surprising result.
el_u85.exc: Los adjetivos en -ed describen un sentimiento (I'm bored), los en -ing — la causa (The book is boring). Orden: Opinion-Size-Age-Shape-Colour-Origin-Material (a lovely small old round wooden table).
mistakes: a house beautiful → a beautiful house — adjetivo antes del sustantivo.
I'm boring of this film → I'm bored with this film — sentimiento con -ed.
happys children → happy children — los adjetivos no cambian.
a red big car → a big red car — primero tamaño, luego color.

el_u86.note: rápido, con cuidado, bueno, duro, rápido
el_u86.exp:
Unit 86 — adverbios (adverbs) de modo: responden a «¿cómo?».

Formación: adjetivo + -ly.

  • slow → slowly, quick → quickly, careful → carefully, beautiful → beautifully.
  • En -y: y → ily — happy → happily, easy → easily.
  • En -le: le → ly — simple → simply.

Formas especiales (adjetivo = adverbio):

  • fast, hard, late, early, high, low, near, far, daily, weekly.
  • He runs fast. She works hard. We arrived late.

Excepción: good → well. She plays the piano well.

Los pares cambian de significado:

  • hard (duro, con empeño) vs hardly (apenas): He works hard ≠ He hardly works.
  • late (tarde) vs lately (últimamente).
  • near (cerca) vs nearly (casi).

El adverbio describe verbo (drive carefully), adjetivo (incredibly beautiful) u otro adverbio (very quickly).
el_u86.exc: Hardly ≠ hard: He works hard (con empeño) vs He hardly works (apenas trabaja). Late/lately: arrive late (tarde) vs lately (últimamente). Friendly, lovely, silly — adjetivos, no adverbios.
mistakes: She speaks very good → She speaks very well — tras verbo adverbio.
He drives careful → He drives carefully.
She sings beautiful → She sings beautifully.
He runs quick → He runs quickly (o fast).

el_u87.note: más viejo, más caro, mejor, peor
el_u87.exp:
Unit 87 — grado comparativo de los adjetivos.

La formación depende del número de sílabas.

  • 1 sílaba: + -er — old → older, cheap → cheaper, fast → faster.
  • 1 sílaba + conso.+vocal+conso.: duplicación — big → bigger, hot → hotter, thin → thinner.
  • En -y (2 sílabas): y → ier — happy → happier, busy → busier, easy → easier.
  • 2+ sílabas: more + adjetivo — more expensive, more interesting, more beautiful.

Formas irregulares:

  • good → better, bad → worse, far → farther / further, little → less, much/many → more.

Algunos bisílabos admiten ambas formas: clever → cleverer / more clever, quiet → quieter / more quiet, simple → simpler / more simple.

Ejemplos: My new car is faster than my old one. This book is more interesting than that one. Today is hotter than yesterday. — Mi coche nuevo es más rápido que el viejo. Este libro es más interesante que aquel. Hoy hace más calor que ayer.
el_u87.exc: Los bisílabos en -y toman -ier: happy→happier, busy→busier. Algunos bisílabos admiten ambas: clever→cleverer / more clever; quiet→quieter / more quiet.
mistakes: more big → bigger — adjetivo monosílabo.
more better → better — doble grado no admitido.
more happy → happier — bisílabos en -y.
expensiver → more expensive — adjetivo largo.

el_u88.note: Ella es más alta que yo. Es mejor de lo que pensaba.
el_u88.exp:
Unit 88 — comparación con la conjunción than.

Estructura: A + grado comparativo + than + B.

  • Tom is taller than Jim. — Tom es más alto que Jim.
  • This book is more interesting than that one.

Forma del pronombre tras than:

  • Norma coloquial — caso objetivo: taller than me / him / her / them.
  • Norma formal — nominativo con verbo: taller than I am / than he is.

Intensificadores ante el comparativo:

  • «Considerablemente»: much, far, a lot, way — much taller, far more expensive, a lot better.
  • «Un poquito»: a bit, a little, slightly — a bit older, slightly cheaper.
  • No se puede very + comparativo (very taller es imposible).

Comparación de cifras: more than 100 people, less than an hour. Aumento paralelo: The older he gets, the wiser he becomes. — Cuanto más viejo, más sabio. It's getting colder and colder. — Cada vez hace más frío.
el_u88.exc: En comparaciones con intensificación: much/a lot/far + comp. (much taller); a bit/slightly + comp. (a bit older). No: very taller.
mistakes: taller that me → taller than me — conjunción than, no that.
very taller → much taller — la intensificación no va con very.
more better than → better than — sin more.
She is tall than him → She is taller than him — se necesita forma comparativa.

el_u89.note: No soy tan alto como tú.
el_u89.exp:
Unit 89 — construcción (not) as … as para comparar igualdad.

Afirmación de igualdad — as + adjetivo / adverbio + as:

  • Tom is as tall as Jim. — Tom es tan alto como Jim.
  • She runs as fast as her brother. — Corre tan rápido como su hermano.

Negación de igualdad — not as … as (= más suave que than):

  • This hotel isn't as expensive as the other one. = The other hotel is more expensive.
  • Today isn't as cold as yesterday.

Construcciones con cantidad:

  • as much + incont. + as: I have as much money as you.
  • as many + cont. pl. + as: She has as many books as me.

Expresiones relacionadas:

  • the same as (igual a): My bag is the same as yours. (no the same like).
  • different from (diferente de): Her answer is different from mine.
  • Equivalente coloquial de not as … as — not so … as.
el_u89.exc: The same as (no the same like): My bag is the same as yours. Cabe as much/many as: I have as many books as you.
mistakes: as tall than → as tall as — construcción as…as.
the same like → the same as.
She is so tall as me → She is as tall as me — en afirmación as…as.
not as taller as → not as tall as — forma base del adjetivo.

el_u90.note: el más antiguo, el más caro, el mejor
el_u90.exp:
Unit 90 — grado superlativo — grado extremo de la cualidad.

Las formas son paralelas al comparativo:

  • 1 sílaba: the + adj. + -est — the oldest, the cheapest, the biggest.
  • En -y: the + adj. + -iest — the happiest, the easiest.
  • 2+ sílabas: the most + adj. — the most expensive, the most beautiful.

Irregulares: good → the best, bad → the worst, far → the farthest / furthest, little → the least, much/many → the most.

El artículo the es obligatorio (a veces posesivo: my best friend).

Preposiciones tras el superlativo:

  • in con lugar/grupo: the tallest building in the world, the best student in the class.
  • of ante enumeración: the youngest of the three brothers.

A menudo sigue Present Perfect: It's the best film I've ever seen. — Es la mejor película que he visto nunca.

Ejemplos: Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth. She's the most intelligent person I know.
el_u90.exc: Tras el superlativo normalmente in (lugar/grupo): the best in the world, pero of ante personas/objetos: the youngest of the three. Después — Present Perfect: the best film I've ever seen.
mistakes: the most tall → the tallest — monosílabo con -est.
best student → the best student — hace falta artículo.
the most best → the best — sin most.
the interestingest → the most interesting — adjetivo largo con most.

el_u91.note: suficientemente bueno / suficiente dinero / edad suficiente para conducir
el_u91.exp:
Unit 91 — la palabra enough (suficiente).

La posición depende de la parte de la oración:

  • Tras adjetivo/adverbio: fast enough, old enough, warm enough, well enough.
  • Ante sustantivo: enough money, enough time, enough people.

Construcción enough + infinitivo con to (suficientemente… para):

  • She is old enough to drive. — Es suficientemente mayor para conducir.
  • Is it warm enough to swim? — ¿Hace bastante calor para nadar?
  • I don't have enough money to buy it. — No tengo bastante dinero para comprarlo.

Construcción alternativa con for + sust.: This box is big enough for all my books. Con sujeto del infinitivo: The coffee is hot enough for me to drink.

Negación: not … enough = «no lo suficiente»: He isn't tall enough to reach the shelf. — No es lo bastante alto para alcanzar la estantería.

Solo: Have you got enough? — Yes, thanks, that's enough.
el_u91.exc: Enough va ante el sustantivo, pero tras el adjetivo/adverbio: enough money, warm enough. A veces enough va tras el sustantivo (raro, anticuado): time enough.
mistakes: enough old → old enough — tras adjetivo.
money enough → enough money — ante sustantivo.
old enough for drive → old enough to drive — infinitivo con to.
She isn't tall enough for reach it → tall enough to reach it.

el_u92.note: demasiado cansado para trabajar / demasiado / demasiados
el_u92.exp:
Unit 92 — la palabra too (demasiado, en exceso).

Siempre tiene matiz negativo — la cualidad «impide» hacer algo.

Estructura too + adj./adv. (+ infinitivo con to):

  • It's too cold to swim. — Hace demasiado frío para nadar.
  • She's too young to drive. — Es demasiado joven para conducir.
  • He speaks too quickly for me to understand.

Con sustantivos — too much / too many:

  • too much + incont.: too much sugar, too much noise, too much work.
  • too many + cont. pl.: too many people, too many mistakes.

Contraste too vs very:

  • very — neutro: The coffee is very hot (pero me lo tomo).
  • too — en exceso: The coffee is too hot (to drink) — no puedo bebérmelo.

No confundir con el homónimo too = «también» (al final de la frase): I'm tired too. — Yo también estoy cansado.

Con for + persona: These shoes are too small for me. — Estos zapatos me quedan pequeños.
el_u92.exc: Too ≠ very: very cold (neutro) vs too cold (excesivo, impide algo). Too en el sentido de «también» va al final: I'm tired too.
mistakes: too much books → too many books — contable.
too many water → too much water.
She is too beautiful (como cumplido) → She is very beautiful — too tiene matiz negativo.
too tired for work → too tired to work — infinitivo con to.

el_u93.text: Unidad 93 - Orden de las palabras en una oración
el_u93.note: Ella siempre trabaja duro en la oficina.
el_u93.exp:
Unit 93 — orden básico de las palabras en la oración inglesa.

Esquema estándar: Subject → Verb → Object → Manner → Place → Time (SVOMPT).

  • She (S) reads (V) books (O) carefully (M) in the library (P) every evening (T).

Regla clave: nunca colocar un complemento circunstancial entre el verbo y el objeto directo. No vale She reads carefully books — correcto She reads books carefully.

Orden de los circunstanciales: primero lugar, después tiempo: I met him at the station yesterday. Aunque circunstanciales cortos de tiempo pueden ir al principio: Yesterday I met him at the station.

Ejemplos:

  • I drink coffee every morning at home. — Tomo café cada mañana en casa.
  • He speaks English very well. — Habla inglés muy bien.
  • We went to the cinema last Saturday.

Un objeto largo puede «empujar» el circunstancial más cerca del verbo: She opened carefully the box she had been given.
el_u93.exc: Los adverbios de modo suelen ir al final: She speaks English well. Si el objeto es largo, el adverbio puede ir antes: He opened carefully the box he had found.
mistakes: I like very much chocolate → I like chocolate very much — adverbio tras el objeto.
She reads every day books → She reads books every day.
He speaks very well English → He speaks English very well.
I saw yesterday him → I saw him yesterday.

el_u94.text: Unidad 94 – Lugar de los adverbios de frecuencia
el_u94.note: siempre, generalmente, a menudo, a veces, nunca
el_u94.exp:
Unit 94 — posición de los adverbios de frecuencia.

Adverbios por frecuencia decreciente: always (100%) → usually → normally → often → sometimes → occasionally → seldom → rarely → hardly ever → never (0%).

Reglas de posición:

  • Ante el verbo léxico: I always get up at 7. She often visits her parents.
  • Tras el verbo be: He is always late. They are usually busy.
  • Tras el primer auxiliar, ante el léxico: I have never been to Paris. She doesn't usually drink coffee.

Solo una negación: He never smokes, no He doesn't never smoke.

Adverbios de posición flexible — sometimes, usually, often, occasionally — pueden ir al principio o al final para enfatizar: Sometimes I work late. / I work late sometimes.

Never, rarely, seldom, hardly ever — solo en mitad; al inicio exige inversión (formal): Never have I seen such a thing.
el_u94.exc: Sometimes, usually, often pueden ir al principio o al final de la oración: Sometimes I work late. Never, rarely, seldom — solo en el medio (o al principio con inversión, que es formal).
mistakes: I go always to school → I always go to school — ante verbo léxico.
She always is late → She is always late — tras be.
He doesn't never smoke → He never smokes — una sola negación.
Always I wake up early → I always wake up early.

el_u95.note: Todavía estoy esperando. Ella ya se fue.
el_u95.exp:
Unit 95 — adverbios still, yet, already y su posición.

still (todavía, aún) — la situación dura más de lo esperado:

  • Posición: ante el verbo léxico, tras be/auxiliar: I still live here. She is still waiting. He still hasn't called.

yet (¿ya? / todavía no) — en preguntas y negaciones, al final:

  • Have you finished yet? I haven't finished yet. — ¿Ya has terminado? Todavía no he terminado.

already (ya) — en afirmaciones, antes de lo esperado; en medio:

  • She has already left. — Ya se ha ido.
  • I've already seen that film.

Matices contrastivos:

  • still + negación expresa fastidio: He still hasn't answered. — Todavía no ha contestado (y debería).
  • not yet — «todavía no» neutro: Not yet, but soon.
  • already en pregunta = sorpresa: Have you finished already? — ¿Ya has terminado?

Con tiempos: yet / already más con Present Perfect (BrE); AmE admite Past Simple.
el_u95.exc: Still en negaciones expresa descontento por el retraso: She still hasn't called (y debería). Not yet — «todavía no» neutro. Already en preguntas = sorpresa: Have you finished already?
mistakes: I haven't finished already → I haven't finished yet — en negación yet.
She already is here → She is already here — tras be.
Did you see him yet? (BrE) → Have you seen him yet? — yet con Perfect.
He still not came → He still hasn't come / He hasn't come yet.

el_u96.text: Unidad 96 – Dar, prestar, pasar, enviar, mostrar + dos adiciones
el_u96.note: Dame el libro. / Dame el libro.
el_u96.exp:
Unit 96 — dos complementos tras verbos de dar y transmitir.

Los verbos give, send, lend, pass, show, tell, teach, offer, bring, buy, make, write toman objeto directo (¿qué?) e indirecto (¿a quién?).

Dos órdenes posibles:

  • Sin preposición: verb + indirect (a quién) + direct (qué). Give me the book. She told us the story.
  • Con preposición to / for: verb + direct + to/for + indirect. Give the book to me. She told the story to us.

La elección depende del foco:

  • Con pronombre-objeto directo normalmente solo la forma con preposición: Give it to me (y no Give me it).
  • El objeto largo o nuevo va al final: She gave her friend a beautiful handmade scarf.

La preposición for se usa con buy, make, cook, get, find: I bought a present for my mother. / I bought my mother a present.

Excepciones: explain, describe, suggest, mention, introduce exigen solo la construcción con preposición: Explain the rule to me (no Explain me the rule).
el_u96.exc: Con los verbos explain y describe no es posible el doble complemento sin to: explain something to somebody (no explain me something).
mistakes: Give me it → Give it to me — con pronombre-objeto directo la preposición es obligatoria.
Explain me the rule → Explain the rule to me.
She showed to me the photo → She showed me the photo / showed the photo to me.
He said me the news → He told me the news — say no toma doble complemento.

el_u97.text: Unidad 97 - Y, pero, o, así, porque
el_u97.note: Estaba cansada, así que me fui a la cama.
el_u97.exp:
Unit 97 — conjunciones coordinantes y subordinantes básicas: and, but, or, so, because.

  • and — adición: I got up and had breakfast.
  • but — contraste: I'm tired but happy.
  • or — alternativa: Tea or coffee?
  • so — consecuencia: It was late, so I went home. — Era tarde, así que me fui a casa.
  • because — causa: I went home because it was late. — Me fui a casa porque era tarde.

La lógica de so / because es opuesta — el orden de causa y efecto es especular:

  • Causa + so + efecto.
  • Efecto + because + causa.

Puntuación: ante and, but, or, so en oraciones compuestas suele ir coma. Ante because no se pone coma si va en medio; sí cuando la subordinada va al principio: Because it was raining, we stayed in.

Conectores afines: although / though (concesivo): Although it rained, we had fun.
el_u97.exc: So y because son opuestos: It rained, so I stayed home = I stayed home because it rained. Although/though — concesivo: Although it rained, we went out.
mistakes: Because it was late, so I left → elija uno: Because it was late, I left / It was late, so I left.
I was tired, because I slept → I was tired, so I slept — consecuencia, no causa.
But however → elija uno: but o however.
I like tea and I don't like coffee (contraste) → I like tea but I don't like coffee.

el_u98.text: Unidad 98 - Cuando y tipo condicional nulo (si)
el_u98.note: Cuando llueve, me quedo en casa. Si calientas hielo, se derrite.
el_u98.exp:
Unit 98 — conjunción when y Zero Conditional (verdades generales).

Regla principal: en las subordinadas de tiempo y condición no se usa tiempo futuro, aunque el sentido sea futuro — en vez de will va Present Simple.

  • When I arrive, I'll call you. — Cuando llegue, te llamaré. (no when I will arrive)
  • If it rains, we'll stay home.

Zero Conditional — situaciones siempre verdaderas (leyes de la naturaleza, hábitos, hechos generales):

Estructura: If / When + Present Simple, Present Simple.

  • If you heat water to 100°C, it boils. — Si calientas el agua a 100°C, hierve.
  • When the sun sets, it gets dark.
  • If I'm tired, I go to bed early.

En Zero Conditional if y when son casi intercambiables: ambos significan «siempre que».

Diferencia con First Conditional: Zero — verdad general (If I eat fish, I feel sick), First — evento futuro concreto (If I eat fish tonight, I'll feel sick).
el_u98.exc: When sobre el futuro = tiempo presente: When I see him, I'll tell him. En Zero Conditional if y when son casi intercambiables para verdades generales.
mistakes: When I will arrive, I'll call → When I arrive, I'll call — tras when no va will.
If water will boil → If water boils — en Zero Conditional ambos en Present.
When I'll be ready → When I'm ready.
If it rains I will staying home → I'll stay home — tras will infinitivo.

el_u99.note: Si llueve, me quedaré en casa.
el_u99.exp:
Unit 99 — First Conditional: condición real en el futuro.

Se usa para eventos futuros probables o posibles: «si ocurre X, pasará Y».

Estructura:

  • If + Present Simple, will + infinitivo sin to.
  • Las partes pueden intercambiarse: si la subordinada va delante, se pone coma.

Ejemplos:

  • If it rains tomorrow, I'll stay at home. — Si llueve mañana, me quedaré en casa.
  • She'll be angry if you're late. — Se enfadará si llegas tarde.
  • If you study hard, you'll pass the exam.

En la principal, en lugar de will pueden ir:

  • Verbo modal: If you're tired, you can rest.
  • Imperativo: If you see John, tell him to call me.
  • be going to: If he doesn't hurry, he's going to miss the train.

En la subordinada con if nunca va will (salvo en peticiones corteses: If you will wait a moment…). Sinónimos de if: unless (a menos que), provided that, as long as.
el_u99.exc: En lugar de will en la principal puede ir imperativo o modal: If you see him, tell him / you should tell him. No se puede will tras if, salvo en peticiones: If you will wait a moment…
mistakes: If it will rain, I stay → If it rains, I'll stay — will solo en la principal.
If I will have time → If I have time.
If he comes, I will to help → I will help — tras will sin to.
I'll go if it won't rain → if it doesn't rain — tras if Present.

int_u01.note: Estoy haciendo / Ella está trabajando ahora mismo
int_u01.exp:
Unit 1 — Present Continuous describe una acción que ocurre en este momento, una situación temporal o un futuro previamente planeado.

Fórmula: (+) S + am/is/are + V-ing; (−) am/is/are + not; (?) Am/Is/Are + S + V-ing?

Ejemplos:
  • I'm writing an email now. — Estoy escribiendo un correo ahora.
  • She is staying with friends this week. — Se queda con amigos esta semana (temporal).
  • We're flying to Rome on Friday. — Volamos a Roma el viernes (plan).
  • The climate is changing rapidly. — El clima está cambiando rápidamente (proceso).
Palabras señal: now, at the moment, currently, today, this week, Look!, Listen!
Contraste: con Present Simple — permanente vs. temporal (He works in a bank vs. He's working from home this month).
int_u01.exc:
Los verbos de estado (know, want, believe, need, like, love, hate) no se usan en Continuous.
Excepciones: have con el significado de «desayunar/pasar tiempo» (I'm having lunch), see = «encontrarse» (I'm seeing her tonight).
mistakes: I am knowing the answer. → I know the answer. — los stative verbs no se usan en Continuous.
She works on a project now. → She is working on a project now. — ahora/en este momento requiere Continuous.
I'm wanting some coffee. → I want some coffee. — want es verbo de estado.

int_u02.note: Yo trabajo / Ella trabaja / verdades generales
int_u02.exp:
Unit 2 — Present Simple expresa verdades permanentes, acciones regulares, horarios y hábitos.

Fórmula: (+) S + V(-s/-es en 3ª pers. sing.); (−) do/does + not + V; (?) Do/Does + S + V?

Ejemplos:
  • Water boils at 100°C. — El agua hierve a 100°C (hecho).
  • She teaches English at a university. — Enseña inglés (permanente).
  • I usually have coffee for breakfast. — Suelo tomar café en el desayuno (hábito).
  • The museum opens at 10. — El museo abre a las 10 (horario).
Palabras señal: always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never, every day, twice a week.
Ortografía: 3ª pers. sing. — -es tras -o, -s, -x, -ch, -sh; -y → -ies (study → studies).
int_u02.exc:
Horarios y programas — Present Simple, incluso para el futuro: The train leaves at 8.
En 3ª pers. sing. es obligatorio -s/-es: she works, he goes, it does.
mistakes: She work in a bank. → She works in a bank. — falta -s en 3ª persona.
Do she like tea? → Does she like tea? — en 3ª pers. se necesita does y el verbo sin -s.
The Earth is revolving around the Sun. → The Earth revolves around the Sun. — verdades permanentes requieren Simple.

int_u03.text: Unidad 3 – Presente simple o presente continuo
int_u03.note: Yo trabajo aquí. / Estoy trabajando desde casa esta semana.
int_u03.exp:
Unit 3 — elección entre Present Simple y Present Continuous: permanente vs. temporal.

Regla: Present Simple — para situaciones generales/permanentes (trabajo, residencia, hábitos). Present Continuous — para lo que ocurre ahora o en el período que rodea ahora (cambios temporales, procesos actuales).

Ejemplos:
  • Tom lives in London, but he's living in Berlin at the moment. — Tom vive en Londres, pero está en Berlín temporalmente.
  • Prices are rising very fast. — Los precios suben rápido (proceso ahora).
  • Water freezes at 0°C. — El agua se congela a 0°C (ley natural).
Caso especial — emociones: always/constantly + Continuous expresa irritación: He's always losing his keys! — ¡Siempre pierde las llaves!
Pregunta clave: «¿Es una verdad general u ocurre justo ahora/temporalmente?»
int_u03.exc:
Los verbos de estado siempre Simple, aunque la situación sea «ahora»: I know it now.
Always + Continuous = irritación: He's always complaining!
mistakes: She lives with her parents this month. → She is living with her parents this month. — situación temporal.
I'm usually going to work by bus. → I usually go to work by bus. — acción regular — Simple.
What you are doing? → What are you doing? — en la pregunta inversión are you.

int_u04.text: Unidad 4 - Verbos estátivos: verbos que no están en continuo
int_u04.exp:
Unit 4 — los verbos de estado (stative verbs) normalmente no se usan en Continuous.

Grupos de stative verbs:
  • Pensamiento/opinión: know, believe, think (=creer), understand, remember, suppose, mean
  • Sentimientos: like, love, hate, prefer, want, need, wish
  • Percepción: see, hear, smell, taste, seem, appear
  • Posesión/relaciones: have (=poseer), own, belong, contain, consist, depend
Ejemplos:
  • I know what you mean. — Sé a qué te refieres.
  • This soup tastes great. — Esta sopa sabe genial.
Dobles significados: think — opinión (Simple) vs. proceso de pensar (Continuous); have — poseer (Simple) vs. «tomar/pasar» (having lunch/a shower — Continuous); see — ver (Simple) vs. encontrarse (I'm seeing John tonight).
int_u04.exc:
Think, have, see cambian de significado: I'm thinking (reflexionando) vs. I think (creo). I'm having a bath (tomando) vs. I have a car (poseo).
Feel y look sobre el estado/apariencia admiten ambas formas: I feel / I'm feeling tired.
mistakes: I am understanding now. → I understand now. — understand es verbo de estado.
Are you believing me? → Do you believe me? — believe no se usa en Continuous.
This dress is belonging to her. → This dress belongs to her. — belong es estado.

int_u05.exp:
Unit 5 — Past Simple indica una acción terminada en un momento concreto del pasado.

Fórmula: (+) regulares: V+ed, irregulares: V2; (−) did not (didn't) + V1; (?) Did + S + V1?

Ejemplos:
  • We met in 2015. — Nos conocimos en 2015.
  • She didn't call yesterday. — No llamó ayer.
  • Did you enjoy the film? — ¿Te gustó la película?
  • Columbus discovered America in 1492. — Colón descubrió América en 1492.
Palabras señal: yesterday, last week/year, in 2010, ago, when I was young, then.
Ortografía -ed: studied, stopped, liked (duplicación/suavizado). Importante: tras did el verbo siempre en V1 sin -ed.
int_u05.exc:
Los verbos irregulares (go/went, see/saw, take/took) se memorizan.
En pregunta/negación con did el verbo principal va en V1: Did you go? (no Did you went?).
mistakes: I goed home. → I went home. — go es irregular.
Did you saw the film? → Did you see the film? — tras did, infinitivo sin to.
She didn't knew. → She didn't know. — en negación didn't + V1.

int_u06.exp:
Unit 6 — Past Continuous describe una acción en curso en un momento concreto del pasado o una acción de fondo que fue interrumpida.

Fórmula: was/were + V-ing.

Usos:
  • En curso en un momento: At 8 p.m. I was having dinner. — A las 8 de la tarde estaba cenando.
  • Acción interrumpida: I was reading when the lights went out. — Leía cuando se fue la luz.
  • Procesos paralelos: While he was cooking, she was setting the table. — Mientras él cocinaba, ella ponía la mesa.
  • Fondo narrativo: The sun was shining and birds were singing.
Contraste con Past Simple: Past Simple — evento breve completado; Past Continuous — fondo en curso (when/while + fondo, evento breve — en Simple).
int_u06.exc:
Los stative verbs no van en Past Continuous: I knew (no I was knowing).
Para fondo y evento interruptor: Past Continuous + Past Simple.
mistakes: I was knowing him well. → I knew him well. — know es estado.
When she called, I cooked. → When she called, I was cooking. — fondo en curso — Continuous.
I was watching TV while I did homework. → I was watching TV while I was doing homework. — dos procesos paralelos.

int_u07.note: He terminado. Ella se ha ido.
int_u07.exp:
Unit 7 — Present Perfect conecta una acción pasada con el presente: importa el resultado, no cuándo ocurrió exactamente.

Fórmula: have/has + V3 (past participle).

Tres significados:
  • Resultado en el presente: I've lost my keys (no las tengo ahora).
  • Experiencia de vida: Have you ever been to Japan? — ¿Alguna vez has estado en Japón?
  • Noticias/eventos recientes: The President has resigned. — El presidente ha dimitido.
Ejemplos:
  • She has written three books. — Ha escrito tres libros (hasta ahora).
  • The price has gone up. — El precio ha subido.
Importante: no se combina con tiempo pasado concreto (yesterday, ago, in 1990). Compara: I've seen this film (experiencia) vs. I saw it last night (hecho).
int_u07.exc:
Con yesterday, ago, last week, in 2010 — solo Past Simple.
Con today, this week, this morning (si aún no terminaron) — Present Perfect.
mistakes: I have seen him yesterday. → I saw him yesterday. — con pasado concreto — Past Simple.
She has went home. → She has gone home. — se necesita V3 (gone).
I finished my homework (resultado importa). → I have finished my homework. — resultado ahora.

int_u08.exp:
Unit 8 — Present Perfect con adverbios típicos: just, already, yet, ever, never, recently, lately.

Significados y posición:
  • just (recién) — entre have y V3: She has just left. — Acaba de irse.
  • already (ya, antes de lo esperado) — entre have y V3: I've already eaten. — Ya he comido.
  • yet (aún/ya) — al final de negaciones/preguntas: Has he arrived yet?
  • ever/never (alguna vez/nunca): Have you ever tried sushi? / I've never smoked.
  • recently/lately: I haven't seen him lately.
BrE vs. AmE: en británico — Present Perfect; en americano con just/already/yet a menudo Past Simple: Did you eat yet?
int_u08.exc:
Yet — al final en preguntas/negaciones. Already — entre have y V3 (o al final para sorpresa). Just — entre have y V3.
En inglés americano con just/already/yet a menudo Past Simple: Did you eat yet?
mistakes: I have yet finished. → I haven't finished yet. — yet en negaciones/preguntas.
Have you ever been to Paris? Yes, I have been there last year. → Yes, I went there last year. — tiempo concreto → Past Simple.
She has already went. → She has already gone. — se necesita V3.

int_u09.note: He estado trabajando todo el día.
int_u09.exp:
Unit 9 — Present Perfect Continuous enfatiza la duración de una acción que comenzó en el pasado y continúa ahora (o acaba de terminar con resultado visible).

Fórmula: have/has + been + V-ing.

Significados:
  • Proceso hasta ahora: She has been studying for three hours. — Lleva tres horas estudiando.
  • Resultado visible: You look tired — have you been running? — Pareces cansado — ¿has corrido?
  • Recién terminado: It's been raining (el suelo está mojado).
Señales: for, since, all day/morning, how long, recently, lately.
Importante: los stative verbs (know, love, have=poseer) no van en Continuous — usa Present Perfect: I've known him for years.
int_u09.exc: Los stative verbs no van en Continuous — usamos Present Perfect: I've known her for years (no I've been knowing).
mistakes: I am working here for 5 years. → I have been working here for 5 years. — con for/since se necesita Perfect.
I've been knowing him since 2010. → I've known him since 2010. — know es estado.
How long you have been waiting? → How long have you been waiting? — se necesita inversión.

int_u10.text: Unidad 10 – Presente perfecto versus presente perfecto continuo
int_u10.exp:
Unit 10 — elección entre Present Perfect y Present Perfect Continuous: resultado vs. proceso.

Present Perfect (have+V3) — cuánto hecho / qué resultado, acción terminada: I've read 50 pages. — He leído 50 páginas.
Present Perfect Continuous (have been+V-ing) — cuánto tiempo dura el proceso, énfasis en duración: I've been reading all morning. — He estado leyendo toda la mañana.

Ejemplos de contraste:
  • She's painted the kitchen (terminado) vs. She's been painting the kitchen (proceso, quizá no terminado).
  • I've written three emails (cantidad) vs. I've been writing emails (ocupación en proceso).
Con verbos live, work, teach, study: ambas formas son a menudo intercambiables sin diferencia de sentido: I've lived / I've been living here for 5 years.
Con cantidad (how many, three times) — solo Perfect.
int_u10.exc: Con live, work, study, teach Perfect y Perfect Continuous son a menudo equivalentes: I've lived / I've been living here for 5 years.
mistakes: I've been reading three books this month. → I've read three books this month. — resultado contable — Perfect.
She's painted the wall all morning. → She's been painting the wall all morning. — proceso sin terminar.
How many times have you been visiting Rome? → How many times have you visited Rome? — cantidad de veces — Perfect.

int_u100.note: rápido, con cuidado, bueno, rápido, duro
int_u100.exp:
Unit 100 — formación y uso de los adverbios en -ly.

Regla básica: adjetivo + -ly → adverbio: quick→quickly, careful→carefully. Cambios ortográficos: y→ily (happy→happily), -le→-ly (simple→simply).
Excepciones (forma idéntica): fast, hard, early, late, high, low, straight — adjetivo = adverbio.
  • She drives fast. It's a fast car.
  • He works hard. It's a hard job.
Falsos amigos:
  • hardly = apenas (no «con dureza»);
  • lately = últimamente (no «tarde»);
  • highly = mucho, altamente (no «en alto»);
  • nearly = casi.
Good → well: She sings well, no good. Pero: I feel good/well — ambos valen.
Adjetivos en -ly (¡no son adverbios!): friendly, lovely, lonely, silly, ugly. El adverbio se expresa con perífrasis: in a friendly way.

La posición del adverbio de modo suele ser tras el verbo/objeto.
int_u100.exc: Adjetivos en -ly: friendly, lovely, lonely, silly, ¡no son adverbios! El adverbio se forma con perífrasis: in a friendly way.
mistakes: She sings good. → She sings well. — well es el adverbio de good.
He works hardly. → He works hard. — hardly = apenas.
She spoke friendly. → spoke in a friendly way — friendly es adjetivo, no adverbio.

int_u101.text: Unidad 101 – Adjetivos y adverbios: bien, rápido, tarde, duro
int_u101.note: Es un auto rápido. Ella conduce rápido.
int_u101.exp:
Unit 101 — pares con la misma forma o con formas distintas de adjetivo y adverbio.

Formas idénticas (adj. = adv.): fast, hard, late, early, high, low, near, deep, straight.
  • It's a fast train. The train goes fast.
  • She arrived late. It was a late arrival.
Pares con significados distintos:
  • hard (duro/con ahínco) vs. hardly (apenas): I work hard. I can hardly hear you.
  • late (tarde) vs. lately (últimamente): She arrived late. I haven't seen him lately.
  • high (alto) vs. highly (muy): The bird flew high. She is highly qualified.
  • near (cerca) vs. nearly (casi): Come near. I nearly fell.
  • free (gratis) vs. freely (libremente): Children travel free. Speak freely.

El contexto decide el significado: hardly tiene matiz negativo, cercano a almost not.
mistakes: I haven't seen him late. → I haven't seen him lately. — «últimamente».
I can hard hear you. → I can hardly hear you. — «apenas» es hardly.
He arrived lately. (= tarde) → He arrived late. — «tarde» es late.

int_u102.exp:
Unit 102 — intensificadores so y such.

So — ante adjetivo o adverbio (sin sustantivo):
  • It's so cold. — Hace tanto frío.
  • She speaks so quickly.
  • so much / so many: I have so much work.
Such — ante (artículo y) sustantivo, con adjetivo opcional:
  • such a + adj + sg. contable: such a cold day, such a nice person;
  • such + adj + pl./incont.: such nice people, such good weather.
Construcciones de resultado:
  • so + adj/adv + that: It was so cold that we left.
  • such a + adj + noun + that: It was such a cold day that we left.
Comparación: It was so cold = It was such cold weather.

Recuerda: ante sustantivo, such; ante adjetivo «desnudo», so.
int_u102.exc: Such a — con contable en sg.: such a cold day. Sin a con plural e incontables: such good ideas, such good food.
mistakes: It was so a cold day. → It was such a cold day. — con sustantivo, such.
Such cold (sin sustantivo) → so cold — ante adjetivo solo, so.
Such a good weather → such good weather — weather es incontable, sin a.

int_u103.note: demasiado cansado para trabajar/no tiene suficiente calor
int_u103.exp:
Unit 103 — enough (suficiente) y too (demasiado).

Enough:
  • tras adjetivo/adverbio: warm enough, fast enough;
  • ante sustantivo: enough money, enough time;
  • construcción: adj/noun + enough + to-inf: She's old enough to drive. We have enough money to buy it.
Too:
  • ante adjetivo/adverbio: too tired, too quickly;
  • construcción: too + adj/adv + to-inf: He's too young to vote. It's too late to call.;
  • too + adj + for somebody: It's too difficult for me.
Contraste de sentido:
  • enough — suficiencia positiva: todo bien;
  • too — exceso que impide algo: matiz negativo.
Very vs. too: very cold — hace mucho frío (dato); too cold — demasiado frío (impide actuar).

Ojo: too ≠ very; enough money, pero old enough (posiciones distintas).
int_u103.exc: Enough va tras el adjetivo pero delante del sustantivo: warm enough, enough money.
mistakes: She is enough old to drive. → old enough — enough tras el adjetivo.
I'm too much tired. → too tired — too sin much ante adjetivo.
He is too young for vote. → too young to vote — construcción too + adj + to-inf.

int_u104.exp:
Unit 104 — adverbios de grado moderado: quite, rather, pretty, fairly.

Quite (bastante) — neutro-positivo: quite good, quite nice. Con algunos adjetivos = completely (del todo): quite right, quite wrong, quite sure, quite impossible.
Rather — más fuerte que quite, a menudo con matiz negativo: rather difficult, rather cold. Con adjetivos positivos indica sorpresa agradable: It was rather good!
Pretty — coloquial, = quite: pretty good, pretty difficult.
Fairly — más débil que quite, neutro: fairly easy, fairly good.
Posición ante sustantivo:
  • quite/rather van ANTES del artículo: quite a good film, rather a nice day;
  • fairly/pretty tras él: a fairly good film, a pretty nice day.
Escala de intensidad: fairly < quite/pretty < rather < very.

Rather suele implicar un contraste con lo esperado.
int_u104.exc: Quite + ciertos adjetivos absolutos = «completamente»: quite right, quite impossible, quite sure.
mistakes: A quite good film → quite a good film — quite va antes del artículo.
Rather a good result o a rather good result — se dan ambos, pero es preferible a rather good result.
He's quite right? — aquí quite = «del todo» y no combina con un matiz de «bastante».

int_u105.note: más barato / más caro / mejor / peor
int_u105.exp:
Unit 105 — grado comparativo de adjetivos y adverbios.

Monosilábicos: +er: cheap→cheaper, old→older, fast→faster.
Bisílabos en -y: y→ier: happy→happier, easy→easier, busy→busier.
Largos (2+ sílabas): more +: more expensive, more difficult, more interesting.
Excepciones (irregulares):
  • good → better;
  • bad → worse;
  • far → farther/further;
  • little → less;
  • much/many → more;
  • old → older / elder (solo para la familia).
Doblar consonante (1 sílaba, cons.+vocal+cons.): big→bigger, hot→hotter, thin→thinner.
Older vs. elder: my elder brother (en familia), pero en comparación solo older: He is older than me.

No se combinan: more better → better.
int_u105.exc: Elder/eldest solo para familiares (my elder brother). Con otros, older.
mistakes: More big → bigger — adjetivos cortos con -er.
More better → better — better ya es comparativo.
He is elder than me. → older than me — en comparación, older.

int_u106.exp:
Unit 106 — modificación del grado comparativo.

Intensificadores (mucho más/menos):
  • much, far, a lot, considerably + comparative: much better, far more expensive, a lot cheaper.
Atenuadores (un poco):
  • a bit, a little, slightly: a bit warmer, slightly more difficult.
Any / no en preguntas y negaciones:
  • Is it any better? — ¿Algo mejor?
  • It's no better. — Nada mejor.
Progresión: comparative and comparative = cada vez más: getting better and better, more and more expensive.
The … the … : The more you practise, the better you get. The sooner, the better.
Prohibido very: very NO se combina con comparativo: no very better → much better.

Solo una forma cada vez: more cheaper → cheaper.
int_u106.exc: No se puede very + comparativo. Se usan much, far, a lot, a bit, slightly.
mistakes: Very better → much better / far better — el comparativo se intensifica con much.
It's a bit more cheaper. → a bit cheaper — solo una forma comparativa.
Is it more better? → Is it any better? — en preguntas, any.

int_u107.note: tan rápido como / más rápido que / no tan bueno como
int_u107.exp:
Unit 107 — todas las construcciones de comparación.

Igualdad: as + adj/adv + as:
  • She's as tall as her brother. — Tan alta como.
  • He runs as fast as me.
Desigualdad (más suave que el comparativo): not as … as / not so … as:
  • It's not as cold as yesterday. — No hace tanto frío como ayer.
Comparativo + than:
  • She runs faster than me/I.
  • This book is more interesting than that one.
Tras than/as: en conversación, caso objetivo (than me, as her); en registro formal, nominativo (than I am, as she does).
Twice/three times as:
  • It's twice as expensive as before. — El doble de caro.
The same as: My car is the same colour as yours.

No se mezclan formas: no as tall than, no more cheap than.
int_u107.exc: Tras than/as, en registro formal, nominativo: taller than I (am). En conversación, taller than me.
mistakes: She's taller as me. → taller than me — tras comparativo, than.
He's as tall than her. → as tall as her — igualdad, as…as.
It's more cheap than the other. → cheaper than — corto, con -er.

int_u108.note: el más antiguo / el más interesante / el mejor
int_u108.exp:
Unit 108 — grado superlativo.

Formas:
  • cortos: +est: oldest, biggest, happiest;
  • largos: the most +: the most beautiful;
  • irregulares: best, worst, furthest, least, most, eldest.
Siempre con the: the oldest, the most interesting — el artículo definido es obligatorio.
Preposiciones tras el superlativo:
  • in + lugar/grupo: the tallest in the class, the best player in the team;
  • of + tiempo/conjunto: the hottest day of the year, the best of all.
Con Present Perfect — combinación típica:
  • It's the best film I've ever seen. — La mejor que he visto.
  • She's the nicest person I've ever met.
Alternativas: by far the best, easily the best — aún más enfático.

No the most best — solo the best.
int_u108.exc: Tras el superlativo, in + lugar/grupo, no of: the tallest in the class, no of the class.
mistakes: She is most beautiful. → the most beautiful — con superlativo, the es obligatorio.
The best player of the team → the best player in the team — con grupo, in.
It's the most best film. → the best film — una sola forma superlativa.

int_u109.text: Unidad 109 - Orden de las palabras: lugar y tiempo (Lección 1)
int_u109.note: Trabaja en Londres todos los días.
int_u109.exp:
Unit 109 — orden de los complementos al final de la oración.

Regla general: verbo → objeto → modo → lugar → tiempo.
  • She sang the song beautifully at the concert last night.
  • He drove carefully to the airport yesterday.
Lugar antes que tiempo:
  • correcto: I went to the cinema last night.
  • incorrecto: I went last night to the cinema.
Varios complementos de lugar de menor a mayor:
  • I live at 12 Oxford Street in London in England.
Varios complementos de tiempo de lo concreto a lo general:
  • She called at 5pm yesterday.
  • We'll meet at 7 on Monday next week.
Desplazamiento al inicio para enfatizar (estilo):
  • Yesterday, I went to the cinema. — Se resalta el tiempo.

No se coloca un adverbio entre verbo y objeto: She sang beautifully the song.
int_u109.exc: Los adverbios de tiempo pueden desplazarse al inicio para enfatizar: Yesterday she went to the cinema.
mistakes: She went last night to the cinema. → She went to the cinema last night. — lugar antes que tiempo.
I live in London at 12 Oxford Street. → at 12 Oxford Street in London — de menor a mayor.
He works every day in the office. → He works in the office every day. — tiempo al final.

int_u11.exp:
Unit 11 — pregunta How long…? sobre la duración de una situación iniciada en el pasado y que continúa ahora.

Estructuras:
  • How long have you + V3/been V-ing?
  • Respuesta: for + período o since + momento.
Ejemplos:
  • How long have you lived in Paris? — For ten years. — ¿Cuánto tiempo llevas viviendo en París? — Diez años.
  • How long has she been waiting? — Since 8 o'clock. — ¿Desde cuándo espera? — Desde las 8.
  • How long have you known him? (stative — Perfect, no Continuous).
Importante: el presente español («¿cuánto tiempo vives?») → Perfect en inglés. How long are you…? es error. Con stative verbs — solo Perfect, no Perfect Continuous. For = intervalo de tiempo, since = punto inicial.
int_u11.exc: Con stative verbs — Present Perfect: How long have you known her? (no been knowing).
mistakes: How long do you live here? → How long have you lived here? — situación desde el pasado hasta ahora.
I'm here since Monday. → I've been here since Monday. — se necesita Perfect.
How long are you waiting? → How long have you been waiting? — duración hasta el presente.

int_u110.text: Unidad 110 - Orden de las palabras: Adverbios de verbos (Lección 2)
int_u110.note: siempre, a menudo, normalmente - lugar en una oración
int_u110.exp:
Unit 110 — posición de los adverbios junto al verbo.

Adverbios de frecuencia (always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never, hardly ever):
  • DELANTE del verbo principal: I always drink coffee in the morning.
  • DESPUÉS de be: She is always late.
  • DESPUÉS del auxiliar: I have never been to Paris. You can always ask me.
Adverbios de seguridad/comentario (probably, definitely, certainly, obviously, clearly) — misma posición:
  • She will probably come.
  • He is definitely right.
Adverbios de modo (well, quickly, carefully) — normalmente al final:
  • She speaks English well.
Para enfatizar, al inicio: Sometimes I feel sad. Often, she arrives early.
No se coloca entre verbo y objeto directo: She speaks well English.

Señal: con be, detrás; con otros, delante; con Perfect/Modal, entre auxiliar y principal.
int_u110.exc: Con be, el adverbio de frecuencia va DETRÁS: She is always late. Con otros verbos, DELANTE: She always arrives late.
mistakes: I always am happy. → I am always happy. — detrás de to be.
She eats always breakfast. → She always eats breakfast. — delante del verbo principal.
I have been never there. → I have never been there. — detrás del auxiliar.

int_u111.text: Unidad 111 – Aún, todavía, ya; más / ya no
int_u111.note: Todavía estoy esperando. Ya no trabaja aquí.
int_u111.exp:
Unit 111 — adverbios de situación prolongada/finalizada.

Still — la situación sigue pese a que se esperaba que acabase:
  • I'm still waiting for him.
  • She still lives with her parents.
  • Posición: delante del verbo principal, detrás de be: He is still here.
Yet — «todavía/ya» en preguntas y negaciones, casi siempre al final:
  • Have you finished yet? — ¿Ya has terminado?
  • I haven't seen him yet. — Todavía no lo he visto.
Already — «ya», en afirmaciones, normalmente entre las partes del verbo o al final:
  • She has already left.
Any more / any longer / no longer — cese:
  • He doesn't work here any more / any longer.
  • He no longer works here. (más formal, no longer delante del verbo principal)

Contraste: still — continúa; any more/no longer — dejó de ser; yet — aún no ocurre; already — antes de lo esperado.
int_u111.exc: Still en afirmaciones, delante del verbo principal. Yet al final en preguntas/negaciones.
mistakes: I don't know still. → I still don't know. — still delante del verbo (incluso en negación).
Have you already finished? (neutro) → Have you finished yet? — en pregunta suele ser yet.
He doesn't work here no longer. → He no longer works here. / doesn't work here any more.

int_u112.note: Incluso yo lo sabía. Ni siquiera puede caminar.
int_u112.exp:
Unit 112 — adverbio de foco even, que intensifica la sorpresa.

Significado básico: «incluso, hasta»; subraya que algo sale de lo esperado.
Posición: delante de la palabra/grupo intensificada (o delante del verbo principal):
  • Even a child could solve it. — Hasta un niño lo resolvería.
  • She can't even walk. — Ni siquiera puede caminar.
  • He works even on Sundays. — Incluso los domingos.
Con comparativo: even = aún:
  • It's even better than I expected.
  • She's even more talented than her sister.
Intensificación negativa: not even:
  • He didn't even say sorry. — Ni siquiera se disculpó.
Even though / even if — conjunciones concesivas (ante subordinada):
  • Even though it was cold, we went out.
  • I'll come even if it rains.

No: Even it rained → Even though it rained.
int_u112.exc: Even no se usa al inicio sin cambio de orden. El comparativo se intensifica así: even better, even more.
mistakes: Even it was cold, we went out. → Even though it was cold… — ante subordinada, even though.
It's more better. → It's even better. — para reforzar el comparativo, even.
He not even said sorry. → He didn't even say sorry. — even detrás del auxiliar.

int_u113.text: Unidad 113 – Aunque, aunque, aunque, a pesar de, a pesar de
int_u113.exp:
Unit 113 — formas de expresar concesión (contraste, «a pesar de»).

Conjunciones (ante subordinada):
  • although, though, even though — sinónimos; even though más fuerte;
  • Although it was cold, she went out.
  • Even though he was tired, he kept working.
  • though suele ir al final como adverbio: It was cold. I went out, though.
Preposiciones (ante sustantivo, pronombre o -ing):
  • in spite of, despite — mismo valor;
  • Despite the rain, we continued.
  • In spite of being tired, she worked.
  • Despite the fact that it was cold, we went out. (the fact that añade subordinada)
Restricciones clave:
  • despite of — sin of;
  • tras although, solo oración, no sustantivo.

Contraste: although + clause, despite/in spite of + noun/-ing.
int_u113.exc: Although/though con subordinada; despite/in spite of con sustantivo o -ing. No existe despite of.
mistakes: Despite of the rain → Despite the rain / In spite of the rain — despite sin of.
Although the rain, we went out. → Although it was raining… — tras although, subordinada.
In spite she was tired, she worked. → In spite of being tired — in spite of + noun/-ing.

int_u114.exp:
Unit 114 — in case (por si acaso): medidas tomadas de antemano.

Sentido: in case indica una acción hecha con antelación para prepararse ante una posible situación.
  • Take an umbrella in case it rains. — Lleva paraguas (por si acaso), para estar listo si llueve.
  • I'll write down the address in case I forget. — Apunto la dirección por si se me olvida.
Diferencia con if:
  • I'll call you if I'm late. — Llamaré solo si llego tarde (condición).
  • I'll call you in case I'm late. — Llamaré de antemano (por si acaso).
Gramática: tras in case, Present Simple/Past Simple, NO will/would, incluso hablando del futuro:
  • Take water in case you get thirsty.
  • I took a map in case we got lost.
Just in case — giro coloquial «por si las moscas»: Take your phone just in case.

In case of + noun (formal, señales) — «en caso de»: In case of fire, break glass.
int_u114.exc: Tras in case, Present Simple, incluso refiriéndose al futuro: I'll take an umbrella in case it rains, no will rain.
mistakes: Take money in case you will need it. → in case you need it — tras in case, Present Simple.
Call me if I'm late vs. Call me in case I'm late: se confunde el sentido: if = condición, in case = por adelantado.
In case of he comes → In case he comes — sin of ante subordinada.

int_u115.text: Unidad 115 — A menos que, siempre y cuando, se proporcione, se proporcione
int_u115.note: A menos que te apresures, llegarás tarde.
int_u115.exp:
Unit 115 — conjunciones condicionales alternativas a if.

Unless = if not:
  • Unless you hurry, you'll be late. = If you don't hurry…
  • I won't go unless you come with me. = if you don't come.
Prohibida la doble negación: no se dice unless … not.
As long as / so long as — «mientras/con tal de que»:
  • You can borrow my car as long as you're careful.
  • I'll help so long as you tell me the truth.
Provided (that) / providing (that) — más formal, «siempre que»:
  • You can stay provided that you pay rent.
  • Providing he apologises, I'll forgive him.
Gramática: tras todas estas conjunciones, Present Simple (como en las if-clauses), NO will:
  • Unless it rains, we'll go out.
  • As long as you are ready, we can leave.

Todas introducen condiciones con matices distintos: unless — condición negativa; provided — requisito que limita.
int_u115.exc: Unless = if not; por eso no se dice unless … not: Unless you hurry, no unless you don't hurry.
mistakes: Unless you don't hurry → Unless you hurry — sin doble negación.
As long as you will be careful → as long as you are careful — tras la conjunción, Present Simple.
Provided that he will come → provided that he comes — presente.

int_u116.note: Cuando me iba, sonó el teléfono.
int_u116.exp:
Unit 116 — conjunción polisémica as.

Sentido 1: «porque» (= because, since):
  • As it was late, I went home. — Como era tarde.
  • As she knew French, she translated.
Sentido 2: «cuando/mientras» — acciones simultáneas prolongadas:
  • As I was walking home, I saw an accident.
  • She sang as she cooked.
Just as — «en el mismo momento en que»:
  • Just as I was leaving, the phone rang.
Elección entre as / when / while:
  • as — acciones simultáneas prolongadas o desarrollo gradual;
  • when — para sucesos breves e instantáneos: When I opened the door, I saw him.;
  • while — subraya la duración: While I was cooking, he called.

Otros valores de as: papel (work as a teacher), comparación (as tall as) — se ven en otras unidades.
int_u116.exc: As con valor de «mientras» se usa con acciones prolongadas (a menudo Past Continuous). Para lo breve, when.
mistakes: As I opened the door, I dropped the keys. (instantáneo) → When I opened the door… — para lo breve, when.
As that it was late, I went home. → As it was late… — sin that.
Because as it rained → As it rained / Because it rained — no se duplica la causa.

int_u117.text: Unidad 117 – Como y como: tales como
int_u117.note: Se parece a su madre. Ella trabaja como maestra.
int_u117.exp:
Unit 117 — like vs. as en comparación y papel.

Like (preposición) + sustantivo/pronombre/-ing — «como, parecido a»:
  • She sings like a bird. — Canta como un pájaro (parecido).
  • He's like his father. — Se parece a su padre.
  • It tastes like chicken.
As (conjunción) + oración — «como, tal como»:
  • Do as I say, not as I do.
  • She works hard, as her mother did.
As (preposición) + sustantivo — papel o función real:
  • She works as a teacher. — Es maestra (es su trabajo).
  • He used the book as a pillow. — Lo usó como almohada.
Contraste clave:
  • As a teacher, I understand students. — Soy maestro.
  • Like a teacher, he spoke clearly. — Parece un maestro, pero no lo es.

En registro coloquial, like a veces se usa como conjunción (Do like I told you), pero es informal; por escrito, as.
int_u117.exc: As señala papel real: She works as a teacher (es maestra). Like a teacher = parece maestra, pero no lo es.
mistakes: She works like a nurse. (ella es enfermera) → works as a nurse — papel real, as.
Do like I say. → Do as I say. — ante subordinada, as.
He's like his father's. → He's like his father. — tras like, sustantivo en caso común.

int_u118.text: Unidad 118 – Me gusta / como si / como si: como si
int_u118.note: Parece como si hubiera visto un fantasma.
int_u118.exp:
Unit 118 — impresiones y comparaciones: like, as if, as though.

As if / as though — conjunciones «como si» (equivalentes, intercambiables):
  • He looks as if he's seen a ghost. — Parece que haya visto un fantasma.
  • She acts as though she owns the place.
Like — variante coloquial, frecuente en inglés americano: It looks like it's going to rain. En registro formal, as if.
Situaciones reales (probablemente ciertas): Present/Perfect:
  • It looks as if it's raining. — Parece que llueve.
  • She looks as if she has seen something scary.
Irreales/imaginarias — Past (como en Subjunctive):
  • He talks as if he knew everything. — Pero no lo sabe.
  • She walks as if she were a queen. (formal were, coloquial was)

Diferencia: as if he is rich (posiblemente cierto) vs. as if he were rich (irreal, no lo es).
int_u118.exc: Tras as if/as though en situación irreal, Past (como en Subjunctive): He acts as if he knew — pero no lo sabe.
mistakes: He looks as if he was a ghost. (aspecto real) → as if he has seen a ghost — situación real, presente/Perfect.
She speaks as if she know everything. → as if she knew everything — irreal, Past.
Like he's tired (formal) → as if he's tired — por escrito, as if/as though.

int_u119.note: durante tres días / durante la película / mientras estuve allí
int_u119.exp:
Unit 119 — for, during, while para indicar tiempo.

For + periodo de tiempo — cuánto duró la acción:
  • I waited for two hours. — Esperé dos horas.
  • She's lived here for five years.
During + sustantivo — cuándo (en qué periodo) ocurrió la acción:
  • I fell asleep during the film. — Me dormí en algún momento de la película.
  • We met during the conference.
While + oración — conjunción «mientras»:
  • I fell asleep while I was watching the film.
  • She called while I was cooking.
Diferencias clave:
  • for responde a «¿cuánto tiempo?»: for 2 hours;
  • during responde a «¿cuándo?» + sustantivo: during lunch;
  • while — mismo sentido que during, pero ante oración.

No: during three days (duración) → for three days; while the film (ante sustantivo) → during the film.
int_u119.exc: During indica «cuándo» (en qué periodo), for — «cuánto tiempo». Se confunden fácil: for 2 hours vs. during the meeting.
mistakes: I was there during three days. → for three days — duración, for.
During I was watching TV → While I was watching TV — ante subordinada, while.
While the film → during the film — ante sustantivo, during.

int_u12.text: Unidad 12 – Presente perfecto con since y for
int_u12.note: La conozco desde hace años. Desde el lunes.
int_u12.exp:
Unit 12 — preposiciones for y since con Present Perfect y Perfect Continuous.

for + período de duración: for two hours, for a week, for ages, for a long time.
since + momento inicial: since Monday, since 2010, since breakfast, since we met. Tras since puede ir una oración entera: since I was a child.

Ejemplos:
  • We've been friends for 20 years. — Somos amigos desde hace 20 años.
  • I haven't seen her since June. — No la veo desde junio.
  • He has worked here since he graduated. — Trabaja aquí desde que se graduó.
ago vs. since: ago — solo con Past Simple, contando desde ahora: two years ago. No se puede since two years — hay que decir for two years.
En lenguaje coloquial for puede omitirse: I've been here (for) ten years.
int_u12.exc:
Ago se usa solo con Past Simple: two years ago (no since two years ago).
For puede omitirse: I've lived here (for) ten years.
mistakes: I've known her since 10 years. → I've known her for 10 years. — período — for.
I've worked here for 2010. → I've worked here since 2010. — momento — since.
She lives here since March. → She has lived here since March. — con since — Perfect.

int_u120.text: Unidad 120 - Por, hasta, por el momento
int_u120.note: Terminaré el viernes. Para cuando ella llegó...
int_u120.exp:
Unit 120 — by, until, by the time para indicar un punto final.

Until (till) — acción continua hasta un momento:
  • I waited until 9 pm. — Esperé sin interrupción hasta las 9.
  • She slept until noon.
  • We can't leave until he arrives.
By — «no más tarde de»; la acción se cumplirá para ese plazo (no necesariamente continua):
  • I'll finish the report by Friday. — Para el viernes como muy tarde.
  • Be home by 10. — Estate en casa para las 10.
By the time + oración — «para cuando»:
  • By the time she arrived, I had already left. — Past Perfect en la principal.
  • By the time you read this, I'll be in Paris. — Futuro en la principal.
Contraste until vs. by:
  • I'll wait until 9. (espera continua)
  • I'll call you by 9. (llamada para ese momento)

No: I'll finish until Friday → by Friday; I'll wait by 9 → until 9.
int_u120.exc: By the time + Past Simple en la subordinada y Past Perfect en la principal: By the time she arrived, I had left.
mistakes: I'll wait by 9pm. (espera continua) → I'll wait until 9pm. — continua, until.
I'll finish until Friday. → I'll finish by Friday. — plazo, by.
By the time she arrived, I left. → I had already left — hace falta Past Perfect.

int_u121.text: Unidad 121 – Preposiciones de tiempo: at, on, in
int_u121.note: a las 5 / el lunes / en julio / en 2020
int_u121.exp:
Unit 121 — preposiciones de tiempo at / on / in: visión sistemática.

At — tiempo puntual: horas (at 7.30), comidas (at lunch), fiestas-periodos (at Easter), at night, at the weekend (BrE). On — días y fechas: on Friday, on 3 March, on Christmas Day, on my birthday. On Friday morning — con la especificación del día gana on. In — periodos más amplios: meses, años, siglos, estaciones (in May, in 1999, in summer, in the 20th century), y también partes del día: in the morning/afternoon/evening.

Ejemplos: I'll see you on Monday evening — nos vemos el lunes por la tarde. She was born in 1990 — nació en 1990. The lesson starts at 9 — la clase empieza a las 9.

Sin preposición antes de this, last, next, every, tomorrow, yesterday: See you next week. Compare los pares: in time (con anticipación) ↔ on time (puntualmente) — véase Unit 122.
int_u121.exc:
Sin preposición: this/last/next/every — see you next Monday, I saw her last week.

at night, pero in the night (una noche concreta).

at Christmas (periodo), on Christmas Day (día).
mistakes: in Monday → on Monday — días de la semana con on.
on the morning → in the morning — partes del día con in.
at next week → next week — next/last/this sin preposición.
in 9 o'clock → at 9 o'clock — hora exacta con at.

int_u122.text: Unidad 122 - A tiempo y a tiempo; al final y al final
int_u122.note: a tiempo (a tiempo) / a tiempo (a tiempo)
int_u122.exp:
Unit 122 — dos pares de expresiones idiomáticas que suelen confundirse.

On time — puntual, según lo programado: The train left on time — el tren salió puntual. In time (for sth / to do sth) — con anticipación, a tiempo para algo: We got to the airport just in time to catch our flight — llegamos al aeropuerto justo a tiempo para tomar nuestro vuelo.

At the end (of sth) — al final de un objeto/periodo concreto: at the end of the film, at the end of May. In the end = finally, eventually — al final, en definitiva: We couldn't decide where to go. In the end we stayed home — no podíamos decidir y al final nos quedamos en casa.

Mnemónica: on time = puntualidad (marca en el horario), in time = margen (dentro de la ventana). At the end = punto en la línea del tiempo, in the end = desenlace.
int_u122.exc:
in the end = finally (al final); at the end = al final de algo concreto.

on time no admite grado comparativo — no se dice more on time.
mistakes: in time for the meeting (= punctual) → on time — «puntual» = on time.
at the end, I agreed → in the end, I agreed — «al final» = in the end.
in the end of the film → at the end of the film — final de algo concreto — at.

int_u123.text: Unidad 123 – Preposiciones de lugar: en, en, en (lección 1)
int_u123.note: en la habitación / en la parada del autobús / en la mesa
int_u123.exp:
Unit 123 — in / at / on como preposiciones de lugar, significados básicos.

In — dentro de un espacio tridimensional/cerrado: in the kitchen, in a car, in Paris, in the water, in the sky. At — en un punto, lugar como nodo: at the door, at the bus stop, at the traffic lights, at the top/bottom. On — sobre una superficie o línea: on the wall, on the floor, on the ceiling, on the coast, on page 10.

Ejemplos: There's somebody at the door — hay alguien en la puerta. The picture is on the wall — el cuadro está en la pared. She lives in a small village — vive en un pueblo pequeño.

Diferencias sutiles: in the corner of the room (dentro de la habitación, esquina interior) ↔ at the corner of the street (en la esquina de la calle, exterior). in the front row (en la primera fila) ↔ at the front of the building (en la fachada). La preposición modela la percepción del espacio — volumétrico, puntual o plano.
int_u123.exc:
in bed / in hospital / in prison — sin artículo, cuando se refiere a la función.

at the door (en la puerta), pero on the door (cartel sobre la puerta).
mistakes: on the car → in the car — dentro de un coche — in.
in the bus stop → at the bus stop — punto/lugar de encuentro — at.
at the picture → in the picture — dentro de la imagen — in.

int_u124.text: Unidad 124 – Preposiciones de lugar: in, at, on (lección 2)
int_u124.note: en el hospital / en casa / en la televisión / en el autobús
int_u124.exp:
Unit 124 — expresiones fijas con preposiciones de lugar, segunda ronda.

In + territorios grandes e «instituciones sin artículo»: in bed, in hospital (BrE), in prison, in the country, in the mountains, in the world, in the middle. At + lugares/eventos típicos: at home, at work, at school, at university, at the station/airport, at a concert, at a party, at Sue's (house). On: on the ground floor, on the second floor, on the way, on a farm, on an island, on the left/right, on the coast, on the river.

Ejemplos: He's been in hospital since Monday — está en el hospital desde el lunes. I met him at a party — lo conocí en una fiesta. We live on the fifth floor — vivimos en el quinto piso.

BrE vs AmE: in hospital (BrE, sobre el paciente) ↔ in the hospital (AmE). Medios: on TV, on the radio, pero in the newspaper. Estas combinaciones es mejor memorizarlas en bloque.
int_u124.exc:
BrE: in hospital; AmE: in the hospital.

on TV / on the radio, pero in the newspaper.

at home / at work / at school sin artículo.
mistakes: in home → at home — expresión fija.
in TV → on TV — programa on TV.
on the bus as passenger ok, pero in the bus raro — on the bus para transporte público.

int_u125.text: Unidad 125 – Preposiciones de lugar: in, at, on (lección 3)
int_u125.note: en el frente / en el frente / en el frente
int_u125.exp:
Unit 125 — tercera ronda: in / at / on delante de front, back, top, bottom, side, end, middle, beginning.
