El sentido se define por el par «lugar + preposición»: in the front/back — dentro del objeto (in the front of the car — en la parte delantera del interior); at the front/back — fuera, en la fachada/trasera (at the back of the queue — al final de la cola); on the front/back — en la superficie (on the back of the envelope — en el reverso del sobre).

Ejemplos: I sat at the back of the class — me senté al fondo de la clase. Write your name on the back of the photo — escribe tu nombre en el reverso de la foto. The driver sits in the front — el conductor va delante (dentro del coche).

At the top/bottom of — en la parte alta/baja (at the top of the hill). On top of — encima (on top of the box). At the end of — al final (espacial/temporal). Los pares exigen entender la geometría física.
int_u125.exc: in the front/back (of a car/room) — interior; at the front/back (of a building/queue) — parte delantera; on the front/back (of a page) — en la superficie.
mistakes: on the front of the class → at the front of the class — parte delantera — at.
at the front of the envelope → on the front of the envelope — sobre la superficie — on.
at the back of the car → in the back of the car — dentro del coche — in.

int_u126.text: Unidad 126 – Preposiciones de dirección: to, at, in, into
int_u126.note: ir a / llegar a / mirar / entrar
int_u126.exp:
Unit 126 — preposiciones de dirección y movimiento: to, into, at, in, on, onto.

To marca el destino: go to work, drive to London, return to the office. Into — movimiento hacia el interior de un espacio cerrado: walk into the room, get into the car, pour milk into the cup. At tras verbos de acción dirigida: look at, point at, throw at, shout at, laugh at, aim at — blanco.

Diferencia in vs into: She was swimming in the lake (dónde, proceso) ↔ She jumped into the lake (hacia dónde, movimiento).

Ejemplos: I'm going to bed — me voy a la cama. He ran into the house — entró corriendo en la casa. Don't throw stones at the dog — no le tires piedras al perro.

Sin preposición: go/come/get home. Arrive: arrive at + edificio/evento, arrive in + ciudad/país. Enter — transitivo, sin into: enter the building.
int_u126.exc:
Sin preposición: go home, get home.

arrive at (lugar), pero arrive in (ciudad/país).
mistakes: go to home → go home — home sin to.
arrive to London → arrive in London — arrive in + ciudad.
enter into the room → enter the room — enter sin preposición.

int_u127.text: Unidad 127 – En, dentro, en en diferentes situaciones
int_u127.note: en un autobús / en un taxi / en una fiesta
int_u127.exp:
Unit 127 — elección de in, on, at en situaciones donde las reglas se cruzan.

Transporte: on a bus/train/plane/ship/bike (grande, al que se «sube» — funciona como plataforma), pero in a car/taxi (se está sentado en el habitáculo). Eventos: at a party/meeting/concert/wedding/match/conference — punto de reunión. Islas/costas: in Sicily (isla-territorio grande), on a small island (punto en el mapa). Direcciones: at 25 Baker Street (número), on/in Baker Street (calle), in London (ciudad).

Ejemplos: I saw her on the train — la vi en el tren. There were 200 people at the concert — había 200 personas en el concierto. He grew up on a farm — creció en una granja.

Regla interna: transporte mediano/público ⇒ on; íntimo ⇒ in. Evento ⇒ at. Estas opciones se aprenden por analogía, no por regla lógica.
int_u127.exc:
on a bus/train/plane/ship/bike, pero in a car/taxi.

at a party / at a concert / at a meeting.
mistakes: in the bus → on the bus — transporte público — on.
on a taxi → in a taxi — taxi — in.
in the party → at the party — evento — at.

int_u128.note: en coche / por casualidad / por el tiempo / por él mismo
int_u128.exp:
Unit 128 — la preposición polifuncional by.

Significados: (1) medio de transporte sin artículo: by car, by bus, by train, by plane, by sea, by air; pero on foot. (2) Agente de la pasiva: Hamlet was written by Shakespeare — escrito por Shakespeare. (3) Modo/instrumento de acción: pay by credit card, contact me by email, by mistake, by chance, by accident. (4) Ubicación al lado: Sit by me — siéntate a mi lado. (5) Plazo límite: I'll be back by 10 — volveré no más tarde de las 10. (6) Soledad: by myself/yourself = alone.

Ejemplos: I'll send the file by email — te envío el archivo por email. She lives by the sea — vive junto al mar. Finish the project by Friday — terminen el proyecto para el viernes.

By vs until: by = para un momento (terminación puntual), until = hasta (duración): Wait until 6 ↔ Finish by 6. With vs by: instrumento (cut with a knife) vs modo/agente (killed by a soldier).
int_u128.exc:
Sin artículo: by car/bus/train/plane, pero on foot.

by myself = alone; by himself (reflexivo con by).
mistakes: by a car → by car — medio de transporte sin artículo.
by foot → on foot — a pie = on foot.
until the time → by the time — «para el momento» = by the time.

int_u129.note: una razón para / una falta de / dificultad en
int_u129.exp:
Unit 129 — sustantivo + preposición: combinaciones fijas.

For: a reason for, a need for, a demand for, a request for. Of: a cause of, a photo of, an example of, an advantage of, a lack of. In: an increase/rise/fall/drop/decrease in, a change in, difficulty in, interest in, belief in. To: damage to, an answer to, a solution to, a reply to, an attitude to/towards, invitation to. With: a relationship/connection with, contact with. Between: difference between.

Ejemplos: There's no reason for worry — no hay motivo de preocupación. The cause of the fire is unknown — se desconoce la causa del incendio. There's been a rise in prices — ha habido una subida de precios. What's the solution to this problem? — ¿cuál es la solución a este problema?

Pares trampa: cause of ↔ reason for (ambos = causa). increase in (no of) al describir el parámetro que crece. Tras la preposición va el gerundio: a reason for leaving early.
int_u129.exc:
cause of, pero reason for.

an increase/rise/fall in (no of).

damage to, solution to.
mistakes: reason of → reason for — causa de algo — for.
cause for → cause of — causa — of.
solution of → solution to — solución — to.
increase of prices → increase in prices — subida — in.

int_u13.text: Unidad 13 – Presente perfecto versus pasado simple (Parte 1)
int_u13.exp:
Unit 13 — contraste principal Present Perfect vs. Past Simple (parte 1).

Present Perfect — conexión con el presente, sin tiempo concreto: I've finished the report (está listo ahora).
Past Simple — acción terminada en momento pasado concreto: I finished it at 3 p.m.

Marcadores decisivos:
  • Perfect: ever, never, just, already, yet, so far, recently, this week (período actual).
  • Past Simple: yesterday, ago, last week/month, in 2010, when.
Ejemplos:
  • Have you seen the news? — ¿Has visto las noticias? vs. Did you see the news this morning? — período cerrado.
  • I've been to Rome (experiencia) vs. I went to Rome in May (hecho).
Preguntas con when — siempre Past Simple: When did you arrive?
int_u13.exc:
En inglés americano Past Simple sustituye a menudo a Present Perfect: Did you eat? = Have you eaten?
Con preguntas when — solo Past Simple: When did you arrive?
mistakes: When have you arrived? → When did you arrive? — con when sobre el pasado — Past Simple.
I have lost my keys yesterday. → I lost my keys yesterday. — yesterday → Past Simple.
Did you ever visit Japan? → Have you ever visited Japan? — experiencia de vida — Perfect.

int_u130.note: bueno en / miedo de / diferente de
int_u130.exp:
Unit 130 — adjetivo + preposición, parte 1.

Of (emociones y características): afraid of, frightened of, scared of, terrified of; ashamed of, aware of, conscious of; capable of, fond of, full of, jealous of, proud of, short of, tired of, typical of. At (habilidades/reacciones): good/bad/brilliant/hopeless/clever/terrible at; surprised/amazed/shocked at/by. From: different from, absent from, safe from. For: famous for, responsible for, ready for, suitable for, sorry for.

Ejemplos: She's afraid of spiders — tiene miedo de las arañas. He's very good at chess — juega muy bien al ajedrez. This region is famous for its wines — la región es famosa por sus vinos.

Matices: sorry for sb (compadecer) ↔ sorry about sth (disculparse por). good at + sustantivo/-ing, pero good to sb = amable con alguien, good for sb/sth = beneficioso. different from — variante neutral; to/than — coloquiales.
int_u130.exc:
different from (BrE preferida), different to/than también aparecen.

good at + sustantivo/-ing, pero good to sb (amable con).
mistakes: afraid from → afraid of — of.
good in math → good at math — at.
different of → different from — from.
famous of → famous for — for.

int_u131.exp:
Unit 131 — adjetivo + preposición, parte 2.

With: angry/annoyed/furious/pleased/happy/satisfied/bored/delighted/disappointed with. About: angry/annoyed/worried/upset/excited/happy/nervous about. In: interested in, involved in, successful in, experienced in, rich in, weak in. On: keen on, dependent on, based on. To: married to, engaged to, similar to, close to, related to, kind/nice/rude/polite to, opposed to, addicted to. By: surprised/shocked/impressed/amazed/astonished by/at.

Ejemplos: I'm not interested in politics — la política no me interesa. She's married to a doctor — está casada con un médico. He was very kind to me — fue muy amable conmigo.

Contrastes: angry with sb (con una persona) ↔ angry about sth (por una situación). tired of = harto ↔ tired from = cansado por (esfuerzo). interested in (interés duradero) ↔ interesting to sb (interesante para alguien).
int_u131.exc:
interested in (interés permanente), pero interesting to sb.

tired of (harto) vs. tired from (cansado por).
mistakes: interested on music → interested in music — in.
satisfied of → satisfied with — with.
keen in → keen on — on.
proud for → proud of — of.

int_u132.text: Unidad 132 – Preposiciones en y después de los verbos
int_u132.note: mirar / mirar a / apuntar a / tirar a
int_u132.exp:
Unit 132 — verbo + at vs + to: acción dirigida vs comunicación.

At — dirección de la mirada/agresión/objetivo: look at, stare at, glance at, glare at, point at, laugh at, smile at, wave at, shout at, throw at, aim at, shoot at, fire at. To — transmisión de información/destinatario: speak to, talk to, listen to, say sth to, explain to, describe to, mention to, suggest to, apologise to, write to, reply to.

Ejemplos: Don't shout at me! — ¡no me grites! (agresión). She shouted to me across the street — me gritó desde el otro lado de la calle (para que la oyera). He threw the ball to me (a mí, para que la agarrara) ↔ threw a stone at the window (a la ventana, para acertarle).

Pares con cambio de sentido: shout at = regañar, shout to = llamar en voz alta. throw at (con intención de acertar) vs throw to (pasar). laugh at (burlarse) — siempre at. smile at — destinatario. Tras listen siempre to: listen to music.
int_u132.exc:
shout at (agresivamente) vs. shout to (para que oiga).

throw at (con intención de acertar) vs. throw to (pasar).
mistakes: look to the picture → look at the picture — at.
point to me angrily → point at me — at cuando se señala a alguien.
laugh of him → laugh at him — at.

int_u133.text: Unidad 133 – Preposiciones sobre, para, de, después
int_u133.note: preocuparse por / esperar / soñar con / cuidar
int_u133.exp:
Unit 133 — verbos con about, for, of, after.

About (tema de pensamiento/conversación): talk/speak/read/write/hear/know/think/dream/worry/complain/argue about. For (objetivo/espera): ask for, apply for, wait for, search for, look for, care for, pay for, hope for, long for, vote for. Of: consist of, smell of, taste of, think of, hear of, dream of, die of. After: look after (cuidar), take after (parecerse a un familiar), ask after (preguntar por la salud), run after (perseguir).

Ejemplos: I'm looking for my keys — busco mis llaves. Who looks after the children? — ¿quién cuida de los niños? She takes after her mother — se parece a su madre. The soup smells of garlic — la sopa huele a ajo.

Trío look: look at (mirar), look for (buscar), look after (cuidar). Confundirlos es un error típico. wait for — la preposición es obligatoria, incluso con pronombre: wait for me.
int_u133.exc:
wait for sb, pero wait on = servir.

look after (cuidar) ≠ look for (buscar) ≠ look at (mirar).
mistakes: worry for → worry about — about.
wait sb → wait for sb — for obligatorio.
dream for → dream of/about — of/about.
look for the kids (= take care) → look after the kids — cuidar — after.

int_u134.text: Unidad 134 – Preposiciones sobre y de: oír, pensar, soñar
int_u134.exp:
Unit 134 — verbos que cambian de sentido con about vs of.

Think about (reflexionar, concentrarse mentalmente) ↔ think of (opinión; venir a la mente; tener intención): I'm thinking about what to do — estoy pensando qué hacer; What do you think of my idea? — ¿qué te parece mi idea? I can't think of the name — no logro recordar el nombre. Hear about (enterarse de una noticia) ↔ hear of (conocer la existencia): Have you heard of Murphy's book? — ¿has oído hablar del libro de Murphy? Dream about (soñar con, en el sueño) ↔ dream of (soñar/imaginar).

Remind: dos patrones. Remind sb of sb/sth = recordar (parecerse): She reminds me of my sister. Remind sb about/to do sth = recordar (instar): Remind me to call him.

Complain: complain to sb about sth — a quién y de qué. Warn: warn sb about/of sth — ambas aparecen. La preposición suele distinguir «tema» (about) y «contenido/naturaleza» (of).
int_u134.exc:
think of (opinión/recordar) vs. think about (reflexionar).

remind of (recuerda a alguien) vs. remind about (recordar sobre un asunto).
mistakes: hear from news → hear about the news — about.
dream for you → dream of/about you — of/about.
remind me to her → remind me of her — parecido — of.

int_u135.text: Unidad 135 – Preposiciones de, para, desde, en
int_u135.exp:
Unit 135 — verbos + of, for, from, on.

Of: accuse sb of, approve of, consist of, dispose of, remind sb of, suspect sb of. For: apologise for, blame sb for, forgive sb for, pay for, punish sb for, thank sb for, charge sb for, pray for, long for. From: borrow from, suffer from, recover from, protect/save/prevent/stop sb from, differ from, escape from, translate from. On: depend on, rely on, count on, concentrate on, insist on, spend (money) on, live on, congratulate sb on.

Ejemplos: I depend on you — cuento contigo. She apologised for being late — se disculpó por llegar tarde. He suffers from asthma — padece asma. They accused him of stealing — lo acusaron de robar.

Patrón «prohibición»: prevent/stop/protect/save sb from doing sth — de algo. Spend on + objeto/asunto, spend (time) -ing sin preposición. It depends aislado — sin on, pero it depends on you — con preposición.
int_u135.exc:
depend on, pero it depends (sin on) como respuesta corta.

suffer from (enfermedad) vs. suffer (because) of (causa).
mistakes: approve for → approve of — of.
suffer of flu → suffer from flu — from.
depend of → depend on — on.
consist from → consist of — of.

int_u136.text: Unidad 136 – Preposiciones en, dentro, con, a, en
int_u136.note: creer en / chocar contra / tener éxito en
int_u136.exp:
Unit 136 — cierre de la serie: verbos + in, into, with, to.

In: believe in, specialise in, succeed in, result in, invest in. Into (movimiento/transformación): crash/bump/run into, divide into, translate into, break into (a house). With: agree/disagree with, argue with, discuss sth with sb, collide with, deal with, provide sb with, share sth with sb, compare X with Y. To: belong to, happen to, listen to, refer to, react to, object to, prefer X to Y.

Ejemplos: Do you believe in ghosts? — ¿crees en los fantasmas? The car crashed into a tree — el coche chocó contra un árbol. I prefer tea to coffee — prefiero el té al café. This book belongs to Tom — este libro es de Tom.

No confundir: believe sb (creer a alguien) ≠ believe in sb/sth (creer en la existencia/valor). happen to sb — ocurrirle a, no with. Tras discuss no hay about: discuss the plan, pero talk about the plan. Las preposiciones moldean el sentido del verbo.
int_u136.exc:
succeed in + -ing, y success in (sustantivo) también con in.

happen to sb (ocurrirle a), happen with es más raro.
mistakes: believe on God → believe in God — in.
crash in the tree → crash into the tree — into.
succeed on → succeed in — in.

int_u137.exp:
Unit 137 — phrasal verbs: estructura general.

Forma: verbo + partícula (up, down, in, out, on, off, away, back). Tres clases:
  • Intransitivos — sin objeto: My car broke down. She grew up in Leeds.
  • Transitivos separables — objeto-sustantivo antes o después de la partícula, pronombre solo en medio: Turn on the radio = Turn the radio on; Turn it on (NO turn on it).
  • Transitivos inseparables (prepositional verbs) — el objeto siempre tras la partícula: look after the children = look after them (NO look them after).
El significado suele ser idiomático: give up = abandonar, no «dar arriba».

Ejemplos: Please put your phone away — guarda el teléfono. I ran into an old friend — me encontré con un viejo amigo.

Guía: partículas simples de movimiento suelen ser separables (put on, take off), las combinaciones con preposición «plena» suelen ser inseparables (look after, get over, run into). Las marcas del diccionario (sep/insep) son la fuente fiable.
int_u137.exc:
Algunos phrasal verbs son separables: turn it on (no turn on it si es pronombre).

Inseparables: look after him (no look him after).
mistakes: turn on it → turn it on — pronombre entre verbo y partícula.
look him after → look after him — look after inseparable.
get on it (the bus) → get on it ok, pero get it on cambia el sentido.

int_u138.text: Unidad 138 – Verbos compuestos con entrada y salida
int_u138.exp:
Unit 138 — phrasal verbs con in y out.

In: get in (entrar/llegar), come in (entrar), drop in (pasarse), join in (sumarse a una actividad), fill in (rellenar un formulario), break in (irrumpir), check in (registrarse en hotel/aeropuerto). Out: find out (averiguar), work out (resolver/salir bien/entrenar), figure out (entender), sort out (poner en orden), point out (señalar), try out (probar), run out (of) (agotarse), eat out (comer fuera), go out (salir/apagarse).

Ejemplos: I've just found out she's moving — acabo de enterarme de que se muda. Please fill in this form = fill this form in = fill it in — rellene este formulario. We've run out of milk — se nos ha acabado la leche.

Lógica semántica: out suele = hacia fuera + terminación/plenitud (sell out, tire out, burn out). In = hacia dentro/llenado (fill in, hand in). Break in (irrumpir) ≠ break into + objeto (entrar forzando).
int_u138.exc:
find out (averiguar) con pronombre: find it out es raro — normalmente find out the truth.

break in = irrumpir; break into + objeto.
mistakes: fill the form in (OK) / fill in it → fill it in — pronombre dentro.
break in the house → break into the house — con objeto — into.
find out it → find it out.

int_u139.note: llevar a cabo / ordenar / sacar / desgastar
int_u139.exp:
Unit 139 — continuación: phrasal verbs con out.

Sentido «hasta el final/del todo»: carry out (llevar a cabo), sort out (resolver), work out (calcular/salir bien), cross out (tachar), leave out (omitir), cut out (recortar/dejar de), check out (salir del hotel). Sentido «manifestarse»: turn out (resultar), come out (salir — libro/sol), break out (estallar — guerra/incendio), stand out (destacar), make out (distinguir señal débil).

Ejemplos: The plan didn't work out — el plan no salió. It turned out (that) he was right — resultó que tenía razón. His new novel came out last week — su nueva novela salió la semana pasada. We must carry out the order — debemos cumplir la orden.

Construcciones: turn out to be + adj/noun o turn out that + clause. run out es intransitivo (time ran out), con objeto necesita preposición: run out of time. check out (salir) ≠ check in (registrarse).
int_u139.exc:
turn out (resultar) va con to be / that: It turned out to be wrong.

run out of (no solo run out, si hay objeto): We ran out of milk.
mistakes: run out milk → run out of milk — of.
carry on the plan out → carry out the plan — separación incorrecta.
turn out that to be → turn out to be.

int_u14.text: Unidad 14 – Presente perfecto versus pasado simple (Parte 2)
int_u14.note: Es la primera vez que hago esto.
int_u14.exp:
Unit 14 — casos sutiles Present Perfect vs. Past Simple (parte 2).

Present Perfect — obligatorio:
  • Tras It's the first/second/best/worst time…: It's the first time I've eaten sushi. — Es la primera vez que como sushi.
  • Tras This is the best… I've ever…: This is the best book I've ever read.
  • Cuando el período aún no ha terminado: I've drunk three coffees today (el día no ha terminado).
Período abierto vs. cerrado:
  • I haven't seen her this morning (la mañana sigue) vs. I didn't see her this morning (la mañana ya pasó).
Noticias vs. detalles: titular — Perfect, detalles — Past Simple: A plane has crashed. It took off at 6 a.m. and…
Clave: ¿la situación es relevante ahora o es hecho cerrado del pasado?
int_u14.exc: Tras It's the first/second/best time… — Present Perfect: It's the best book I've ever read.
mistakes: It's the first time I see this. → It's the first time I've seen this. — tras It's the first time — Perfect.
This is the best coffee I ever drank. → This is the best coffee I've ever drunk. — se necesita Perfect.
Have you eaten? — Yes, I have eaten an hour ago. → Yes, I ate an hour ago. — ago — Past Simple.

int_u140.text: Unidad 140 – Verbos compuestos con encendido y apagado (lección 1)
int_u140.note: poner / quitar / poner en marcha / cancelar
int_u140.exp:
Unit 140 — phrasal verbs con on y off, primera ronda.

On (encender/continuar/ponerse): turn/switch on (encender), put on (ponerse/ganar peso), go on (seguir/ocurrir), carry on (continuar), hold on (esperar), get on (with) (continuar/llevarse bien). Off (apagar/separar/partir): turn/switch off, take off (quitarse; despegar), set off (ponerse en camino), put off (posponer; desanimar), call off (cancelar), see sb off (despedir).

Ejemplos: The meeting has been called off — la reunión se ha cancelado. What's going on here? — ¿qué está pasando aquí? We set off at dawn — partimos al amanecer. Put off the decision until Monday — pospón la decisión hasta el lunes.

Semántica de las partículas: on = contacto/continuidad, off = ruptura/alejamiento. put off tiene dos sentidos — posponer y desanimar. take off: ropa (quitar) y avión (despegar) — la misma metáfora de separación.
int_u140.exc:
put on clothes = ponerse; take off clothes = quitarse.

call off = cancelar (= cancel); set off = ponerse en camino.
mistakes: put on it → put it on — pronombre dentro.
cancel off → call off / cancel — no se combinan.
set off to the trip → set off on the trip — on.

int_u141.text: Unidad 141 – Verbos compuestos con encendido y apagado (lección 2)
int_u141.exp:
Unit 141 — on/off, segunda ronda.

Get on — subir al autobús/tren/avión; get off — bajarse. Para coche/taxi: get in/into, get out of. Get on with sb — llevarse bien; get on with sth — seguir con una tarea. Fall off — caerse de; caída de ventas: Sales have fallen off. Go off tiene varios sentidos: dispararse (The alarm went off), estropearse (The milk has gone off), marcharse (She went off to Paris), dejar de gustar (I've gone off coffee). Show off — presumir. Pay off — dar resultado / liquidar deuda. Doze off, nod off — quedarse dormido.

Ejemplos: My hard work paid off — mi esfuerzo dio frutos. He's always showing off his new car — siempre presume de su coche nuevo. I get on well with my neighbours — me llevo bien con mis vecinos.

Matiz: switch off ≈ turn off para aparatos — intercambiables. get on y get off con transporte público — par mnemotécnico.
int_u141.exc:
get on (autobús/tren), pero get in (coche/taxi).

switch off / turn off — sinónimos para aparatos.
mistakes: get in the bus → get on the bus — on.
get on a taxi → get in a taxi — in.
switch off it → switch it off.

int_u142.text: Unidad 142 – Verbos compuestos con arriba y abajo
int_u142.exp:
Unit 142 — phrasal verbs con up y down: metáforas de altura.

Up suele = aumento/intensificación/terminación: grow up (crecer, personas), speed up (acelerar), save up (ahorrar), clean up (limpiar del todo), wash up (fregar BrE), set up (fundar/instalar), cheer up (animar), eat up (acabarse la comida), use up (gastar del todo). Down = reducción/cese: break down (averiarse/derrumbarse emocionalmente), slow down (reducir velocidad), calm down (calmarse), turn down (bajar/rechazar), cut down (on) (reducir consumo), let sb down (defraudar), write down (anotar).

Ejemplos: He's trying to cut down on sugar — intenta reducir el azúcar. Don't let me down — no me falles. Our car broke down on the motorway — nuestro coche se averió en la autopista.

Pares de contraste: break down (mecanismo/persona) ≠ break up (relaciones terminan; empiezan las vacaciones). turn up (subir volumen/aparecer) ↔ turn down (bajar/rechazar oferta).
int_u142.exc:
break down (coche averiado) ≠ break up (relaciones terminadas).

grow up (una persona crece) ≠ grow (una planta crece).
mistakes: I grow up in Moscow (now) → I grew up in Moscow — normalmente en past simple.
slow → slow down — para pedir reducir velocidad — down.
set up on business → set up a business — sin on.

int_u143.exp:
Unit 143 — phrasal verbs con up, continuación: capa clave.

Pick up — recoger; recoger a alguien; pillar (enfermedad/idioma); arreciar (viento). Give up — dejar (+ -ing/noun): give up smoking. Make up — inventar una historia; reconciliarse (make (it) up with sb); componer un todo; maquillarse. Keep up (with) — no quedarse atrás. End up — acabar (+ -ing/adjective/lugar): We ended up staying home. Put up (with) — soportar, aguantar. Take up — empezar una afición; ocupar espacio/tiempo. Use up — gastar del todo.

Ejemplos: I can't put up with this noise — no soporto este ruido. She took up painting last year — empezó a pintar el año pasado. Can you pick me up at the station? — ¿puedes recogerme en la estación?

Combinatoria: give up exige gerundio, no infinitivo. put up with — compuesto inseparable: I can't put up with it, NO put up it with. make up polisémico — el contexto decide.
int_u143.exc:
make up tiene varios sentidos: inventar (a story), reconciliarse (with sb), componer.

give up + -ing / sustantivo, no to-infinitive.
mistakes: give up to smoke → give up smoking — forma -ing.
pick up it → pick it up.
keep up from me → keep up with me — with.

int_u144.exp:
Unit 144 — más up: aparición, descubrimiento, creación.

Turn up / show up — aparecer, presentarse (a menudo inesperadamente o tarde). Come up — surgir (tema, problema); acercarse. Come up with — ocurrirse (idea, solución). Look up — buscar en el diccionario/manual (separable: look it up). Set up — fundar, organizar. Bring up — criar a un niño; sacar un tema. Split up — separarse. Wake up — despertarse/despertar. Blow up — explotar; inflar. Do up — abrochar; reformar.

Ejemplos: She didn't turn up to the party — no apareció en la fiesta. I came up with a new idea — se me ocurrió una idea nueva. Can you look up the word in the dictionary? — busca la palabra en el diccionario.

Distinguir: come up (surgir solo) ≠ come up with (generar conscientemente). bring up vs raise como sinónimos de «criar»; bring up también «plantear en conversación». set up a business = start a business.
int_u144.exc:
come up with = ocurrirse; come up (sin with) = surgir.

look up (palabra en diccionario) separable: look it up.
mistakes: come up an idea → come up with an idea — with.
look up it → look it up.
show on / turn on (arrive) → show up / turn up — «aparecer» = show/turn up.

int_u145.text: Unidad 145 – Verbos compuestos con lejos y atrás
int_u145.exp:
Unit 145 — cierre de la serie: away y back.

Away (alejamiento, eliminación, continuidad): go away (irse), run away (huir), get away (escapar; irse de vacaciones), throw away (tirar; desaprovechar), give away (regalar; delatar un secreto), put away (guardar en su sitio), keep away (from) (mantenerse lejos), pass away (fallecer, eufem.). Back (regreso, respuesta): come back, go back, get back (volver); give back, pay back, send back (devolver); call/ring/phone back (devolver la llamada); look back (mirar atrás).

Ejemplos: Don't throw those papers away — no tires esos papeles. He gave away all his money to charity — donó todo su dinero a la caridad. I'll call you back later — te llamo luego.

Sinónimos/matices: throw away ≈ throw out (tirar). get back (regresar) ≠ get sth back (recuperar algo). come back home — sin to. put away (guardar) ≠ put off (posponer).
int_u145.exc:
get back = regresar; get sth back = recuperar algo.

throw away / throw out — sinónimos (tirar).
mistakes: come back to home → come back home — home sin to.
go away from here (imperative tone) ok, pero go out away — go away sin partículas sobrantes.
throw away it → throw it away.

int_u15.note: Ella ya se había ido cuando llegué.
int_u15.exp:
Unit 15 — Past Perfect («pluscuamperfecto») muestra que una acción ocurrió ANTES de otra acción o momento pasado.

Fórmula: had + V3 (igual para todas las personas). Contracción: 'd.

Casos:
  • Secuencia: When I arrived, the film had already started. — Cuando llegué, la película ya había empezado.
  • Causa/estado: She was tired because she hadn't slept. — Estaba cansada porque no había dormido.
  • Estilo indirecto: He said he had seen it.
Señales: already, just, never, by the time, before, after, when.
Contraste con Past Simple: no hay desplazamiento al pasado si la secuencia es clara (I woke up and had breakfast). Past Perfect se necesita cuando el orden de eventos es importante y no obvio.
int_u15.exc:
Past Perfect no se necesita si la secuencia ya es clara (especialmente con before, after): After I ate, I left.
Los stative verbs no van en Perfect Continuous — solo en Past Perfect: I had known.
mistakes: When I came, she already left. → When I came, she had already left. — pasado más remoto.
He said he saw the film before. → He said he had seen the film before. — en estilo indirecto.
I had ate before he arrived. → I had eaten before he arrived. — se necesita V3 (eaten).

int_u16.note: Había estado esperando durante una hora antes de que ella llegara.
int_u16.exp:
Unit 16 — Past Perfect Continuous indica un proceso que estaba en curso ANTES de un momento concreto del pasado (a veces — con resultado en ese momento).

Fórmula: had been + V-ing.

Significados:
  • Duración hasta momento pasado: By 2020, she had been teaching for 15 years. — Para 2020, llevaba enseñando 15 años.
  • Causa de estado pasado: His eyes were red because he had been crying. — Los ojos rojos porque había estado llorando.
  • Proceso interrumpido: I had been waiting for an hour when the bus finally came.
Señales: for, since, how long, all day, before.
Stative verbs no van en Continuous — usamos Past Perfect: I had known him for years before he told me.
Contraste: Past Perfect — hecho/resultado; Past Perfect Continuous — proceso/duración.
int_u16.exc: Los stative verbs no se usan — ponemos Past Perfect: I had known him for years.
mistakes: She was tired because she worked all day. → She was tired because she had been working all day. — acción en curso hasta momento pasado.
He had been knowing her for years. → He had known her for years. — know es estado.
I had waiting for an hour. → I had been waiting for an hour. — se necesita forma had been + -ing.

int_u17.exp:
Unit 17 — have got (BrE) y have (AmE/universal) para posesión.

Significados idénticos: I've got a brother = I have a brother.

Formas:
  • have got: (+) I've got / She's got; (−) I haven't got / She hasn't got; (?) Have you got…? Has she got…?
  • have: (+) I have / She has; (−) I don't have / She doesn't have; (?) Do you have…? Does she have…?
Restricción importante: have got — solo en presente. En pasado — solo had (sin got): I had a bike when I was 10. Pregunta: Did you have…?

Acciones (no posesión) — solo have sin got: have breakfast/a shower/a party/a good time. Aquí have = «realizar/pasar».
int_u17.exc:
Have got solo en presente. En pasado — had (sin got): I had a car then.
Para acciones (no posesión) usamos have: have lunch, have a shower (no have got).
mistakes: I've got a shower every morning. → I have a shower every morning. — acción, no posesión.
She had got a car last year. → She had a car last year. — have got solo en Present.
Have you a pen? → Have you got a pen? / Do you have a pen? — se necesita forma correcta.

int_u18.exp:
Unit 18 — used to + infinitive describe hábitos regulares o estados en el pasado que ya no existen.

Fórmula: (+) used to + V1; (−) didn't use to + V1; (?) Did + S + use to + V1?

Ejemplos:
  • I used to play the guitar. — Antes tocaba la guitarra (ahora no).
  • She used to have long hair. — Antes tenía el pelo largo (estado).
  • Didn't you use to smoke? — ¿No fumabas?
  • We didn't use to have internet. — No teníamos internet.
Importante:
  • No hay forma de presente: para hábitos actuales — Present Simple (I play tennis).
  • Tras did — use to (sin -d).
¡No confundir! used to do (hábito pasado) vs. be used to doing (estar acostumbrado a algo, ver Unit 61).
int_u18.exc:
No hay forma de presente: used to — solo sobre el pasado.
No confundir: be/get used to + -ing = acostumbrarse (I'm used to getting up early).
mistakes: I use to play tennis when I was young. → I used to play tennis… — forma used to.
I am used to smoke. → I used to smoke. — para hábitos pasados.
Did you used to live there? → Did you use to live there? — tras did — use to.

int_u19.text: Unidad 19 – Presente Continuo y Simple para el futuro
int_u19.note: Me reuniré con ella mañana. El tren sale a las 8.
int_u19.exp:
Unit 19 — uso de los presentes para expresar el futuro.

Present Continuous — planes personales y acuerdos: lo ya organizado/acordado con alguien.
  • I'm meeting Sarah at 6. — Quedo con Sarah a las 6 (acordado).
  • We're flying to Madrid on Monday. — Volamos a Madrid el lunes (hay billetes).
Present Simple — horarios, programas, calendarios (no dependen del hablante): trenes, películas, clases.
  • The train leaves at 7:45. — El tren sale a las 7:45.
  • The film starts at 8. — La película empieza a las 8.
  • Term begins on September 1st. — El curso empieza el 1 de septiembre.
Contraste con going to: Present Continuous enfatiza acuerdo, going to — intención. A menudo intercambiables. Stative verbs (be, know, need) — en Simple.
int_u19.exc: Stative verbs → Present Simple: I need help tomorrow (no I'm needing).
mistakes: I meet her tomorrow. → I'm meeting her tomorrow. — plan personal → Continuous.
The train is leaving at 8 every day. → The train leaves at 8 every day. — horario → Simple.
What you doing tonight? → What are you doing tonight? — se necesita are.

int_u20.note: Voy a estudiar más. Va a nevar.
int_u20.exp:
Unit 20 — be going to para intenciones y predicciones con señales evidentes.

Fórmula: am/is/are + going to + V1.

Dos significados:
  • Intención (decisión ya tomada): I'm going to quit my job next month. — Voy a dejar mi trabajo.
  • Predicción con evidencias visibles: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain! — ¡Mira esas nubes — va a llover!
Pasado — was/were going to (planeaba, pero no hice): I was going to call you, but I forgot. — Iba a llamarte, pero olvidé.

Ejemplos:
  • She's going to have a baby. — Va a tener un bebé (visible/planeado).
  • We're going to visit Spain this summer.
Estilística: en vez de be going to go/come a menudo se dice solo Present Continuous: I'm going to the cinema.
int_u20.exc: A menudo en vez de be going to go/come se dice solo Present Continuous: I'm going to the party (no I'm going to go).
mistakes: Look — it going to rain! → Look — it's going to rain! — se necesita is.
I going to study tonight. → I'm going to study tonight. — auxiliar am.
She's going quit her job. → She's going to quit her job. — se necesita to antes del infinitivo.

int_u21.note: Yo te ayudaré. Mañana hará frío.
int_u21.exp:
Unit 21 — will/shall para decisiones espontáneas, promesas, peticiones y predicciones sin señales evidentes.

Fórmula: will/'ll + V1; negación won't; pregunta Will + S + V1?

Significados:
  • Decisión espontánea: The phone's ringing — I'll get it! — ¡Yo contesto!
  • Promesa: I'll call you tomorrow. I promise.
  • Predicción (opinión): I think Brazil will win.
  • Oferta/rechazo: I'll help you / The door won't open.
Regla clave: tras if, when, as soon as, before, after, until — nunca will, usamos Present: When he arrives, I'll tell him.
Shall (BrE, raro en AmE) — con I/we para sugerencias: Shall I open the window? Shall we dance?
Contraste: will = decisión ahora; going to = decisión tomada antes.
int_u21.exc: Tras if, when, as soon as, before, after — nunca will: When he comes, I'll tell him (no when he will come).
mistakes: I'll call you when I will arrive. → …when I arrive. — en subordinadas de tiempo will prohibido.
I think it'll rain probably. → I think it will probably rain. — adverbio entre will y verbo.
He'll to help us. → He'll help us. — tras will — infinitivo sin to.

int_u22.exp:
Unit 22 — funciones de will/shall: ofertas de ayuda, peticiones, acuerdo, rechazo.

Shall I/we…? — oferta de ayuda o sugerencia conjunta:
  • Shall I carry your bag? — ¿Te llevo la bolsa?
  • Shall we go to the cinema? — ¿Vamos al cine?
Will you…? — petición o invitación:
  • Will you close the door, please? — ¿Cierras la puerta?
  • Will you have some tea? — ¿Tomas té?
Rechazo/desobediencia del objeto (won't): The car won't start. — El coche no arranca.

Promesa/amenaza firme: I'll be there, don't worry. / You'll regret this.

Importante: shall con you/he/she/they suena arcaico; en peticiones — will/would. En AmE shall casi no se usa. Tras todas estas formas — infinitivo sin to.
int_u22.exc: Shall con I/we para sugerencias y preguntas; en inglés americano casi no se usa.
mistakes: Shall you open the window? → Will you open the window? / Shall I open it? — shall solo con I/we.
I'll to help you. → I'll help you. — will + V sin to.
Will I carry your bag? → Shall I carry your bag? — oferta de ayuda — shall I.

int_u23.exp:
Unit 23 — diferencia clave will vs. going to.

will — decisión EN EL MOMENTO de hablar:
  • A: We need milk. B: OK, I'll buy some. — Decidido justo ahora.
going to — decisión ANTES del momento de hablar:
  • A: Why are you going to the shop? B: I'm going to buy milk. — Ya lo tenía planeado.
Predicciones:
  • will — opinión/sensación sin evidencia: I think she'll like the gift.
  • going to — hay evidencia visible: She's going to have a baby (se ve) / The sky is dark — it's going to rain.
Pregunta-test: «¿Lo he decidido ahora mismo?» — will. «¿Ya lo pensaba?» — going to.
Predicción del tiempo del locutor: will (pronóstico oficial) o going to (señales visibles).
int_u23.exc: En predicciones basadas en opinión (sin evidencia) — will: I think she'll win. En predicciones basadas en evidencias visibles — going to: Look at the clouds — it's going to rain.
mistakes: The phone's ringing. I'm going to answer it. → I'll answer it. — decisión espontánea → will.
I've decided: I will buy a new car. → I'm going to buy a new car. — decisión ya tomada → going to.
Look at the sky! It will rain. → It's going to rain. — hay evidencia → going to.

int_u24.text: Unidad 24 - Futuro continuo y futuro perfecto
int_u24.note: Estaré trabajando a las 8. Para entonces habré terminado.
int_u24.exp:
Unit 24 — Future Continuous y Future Perfect.

Future Continuous (will be + V-ing) — acción que estará en curso en un momento futuro concreto o sobre planes/preguntas corteses.
  • This time tomorrow I'll be flying to Rome. — Mañana a esta hora estaré volando a Roma.
  • Will you be using your car tonight? — ¿Usarás el coche esta noche? (consulta cortés).
Future Perfect (will have + V3) — acción terminada PARA un momento concreto.
  • By 2030, we will have moved. — Para 2030 ya nos habremos mudado.
  • She'll have finished the report by Friday.
Señales: by + tiempo, by the time… → Perfect; at + tiempo, this time next… → Continuous.
Future Perfect Continuous (will have been V-ing) — forma rara para duración: By June I'll have been working here for 10 years.
int_u24.exc: Future Perfect Continuous (will have been doing) se usa raramente — para enfatizar duración: By next month I'll have been working here for 10 years.
mistakes: At 10 tomorrow I will sleep. → At 10 tomorrow I'll be sleeping. — en momento concreto — Continuous.
By Friday I'll finish the report. → By Friday I'll have finished the report. — hasta fecha límite — Perfect.
I will been working. → I will have been working. — se necesita forma will have been.

int_u25.exp:
Unit 25 — First Conditional: condiciones futuras reales/probables.

Fórmula: If + Present Simple, will/can/may/might + V1. El orden de las partes puede cambiar (coma solo si la parte if va primero).

Ejemplos:
  • If it rains, we'll stay home. — Si llueve, nos quedaremos en casa.
  • You'll pass the exam if you study. — Aprobarás si estudias.
  • If you need help, call me. (imperativo en la principal).
Regla férrea: tras if, when, as soon as, unless, until, before, after — NUNCA will.

Conjunciones alternativas:
  • unless = if…not: Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
  • as long as / provided (that) — siempre que: I'll lend it as long as you return it.
  • in case — por si: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
int_u25.exc:
Tras if — nunca will.
Unless = if…not: Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
mistakes: If it will rain, we'll stay home. → If it rains, we'll stay home. — tras if no hay will.
If you will call me, I answer. → If you call me, I'll answer. — will solo en la parte principal.
Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late. → Unless you hurry… — unless ya contiene negación.

int_u26.note: Puedo nadar. Pude escapar. Podré venir.
int_u26.exp:
Unit 26 — expresar capacidad: can, could, be able to.

can — capacidad/posibilidad en el presente: I can speak French. Forma can't/cannot.
could — capacidad general en el pasado (lo que solía poder): I could swim when I was 5.
was/were able to — éxito único en una situación pasada concreta: The door was locked but I was able to open it. — La puerta estaba cerrada, pero logré abrirla.

be able to — única forma para:
  • Futuro: I'll be able to help tomorrow.
  • Perfect: I haven't been able to contact her.
  • Tras modales: You must be able to drive.
  • Infinitivo: I want to be able to speak Spanish.
En negación couldn't también funciona para casos únicos: I couldn't open the door. = I wasn't able to.
int_u26.exc:
Para éxito único en el pasado — solo was/were able to, no could: I was able to escape (no I could escape).
En negación ambas formas son posibles: couldn't / wasn't able to.
mistakes: Yesterday I could finally find my keys. → Yesterday I was able to find my keys. — éxito único.
She can to swim. → She can swim. — tras modal — sin to.
I will can help you. → I will be able to help you. — tras will — be able to.

int_u27.exp:
Unit 27 — significados adicionales de could.

1. Petición cortés: Could you pass the salt, please? — ¿Podría pasarme la sal?
2. Posibilidad teórica (presente/futuro): It could rain later. — Podría llover (= might).
3. Sugerencia: We could go to the beach. — Podríamos ir a la playa.
4. Could + have + V3 — posibilidad en el pasado:
  • Oportunidad perdida: You could have told me! — ¡Podrías habérmelo dicho!
  • Suposición del pasado: She could have missed the train. — Quizá perdió el tren.
Couldn't have + V3 — certeza de que algo no pudo pasar: He couldn't have known — he wasn't there.

Contraste con can: could — más suave, más cortés, menos definido. No confundir: could-petición y could-pasado — el contexto decide.
int_u27.exc: Could have + V3 = podría haber, pero no hice: You could have told me!
mistakes: Could you to help me? → Could you help me? — sin to.
It could to rain. → It could rain. — tras modal — infinitivo sin to.
He could have told, but he didn't tell. → He could have told me but he didn't. — no duplicamos el verbo.

int_u28.text: Unidad 28 - Debe y no puede: confianza
int_u28.note: Debe estar cansada. No puede hablar en serio.
int_u28.exp:
Unit 28 — must y can't para deducciones lógicas (certeza).

must — deducción positiva segura: «seguramente, debe de».
  • You must be exhausted after the flight. — Debes de estar agotado tras el vuelo.
  • He must know — he was there. — Seguro que lo sabe.
can't — deducción negativa segura: «no puede ser»:
  • That can't be John — he's in Paris. — No puede ser John.
  • She can't be hungry — she just ate.
Sobre el pasado:
  • must have + V3: She must have left early. — Debe de haberse ido temprano.
  • can't have + V3: He can't have seen us. — No puede habernos visto.
¡Importante! mustn't ≠ deducción; es prohibición («no se puede»). Para la deducción negativa usa can't, no mustn't.
Contraste: must (lógica, 95% certeza) vs. may/might (posibilidad, 50%).
int_u28.exc: Para certeza sobre el pasado: must have + V3 / can't have + V3: She must have left. He can't have seen us.
mistakes: She mustn't be tired — she's smiling. → She can't be tired — she's smiling. — mustn't — prohibición, no deducción negativa.
He must to be rich. → He must be rich. — tras modal — sin to.
You must be joking yesterday. → You must have been joking yesterday. — sobre el pasado — must have.

int_u29.note: Podría llover. Puede que ella no venga.
int_u29.exp:
Unit 29 — may y might para expresar posibilidad en presente/futuro.

Ambos significan «quizás, puede ser» (≈ 40–60%). Diferencia sutil: might algo menos seguro, pero a menudo intercambiables.

Fórmulas:
  • (+) may/might + V1
  • (−) may not / might not (mightn't) — ¡la contracción mayn't no se usa!
  • (?) raramente — normalmente mediante Do you think…?
Ejemplos:
  • She may/might be at home. — Quizás está en casa.
  • It may/might rain tomorrow.
  • I might go to the party, I'm not sure.
may — también permiso formal: You may leave now.

Contraste con modales:
  • will — 100%, must — 95%, may/might — 50%, could — posibilidad teórica.
Tras may/might — infinitivo sin to, forma invariable por persona.
int_u29.exc:
No existe contracción mayn't. Usamos may not / might not / mightn't.
May para permiso: You may leave.
mistakes: He mays come. → He may come. — los modales no varían por persona.
She might to call. → She might call. — sin to.
It mayn't rain. → It may not rain. — no existe contracción mayn't.

int_u30.text: Unidad 30 – Mayo y poder (lección 2): el pasado
int_u30.exp:
Unit 30 — may/might + have + V3 para suposiciones sobre el pasado.

Fórmula: may/might (not) + have + V3. Significa «quizás (no) hizo» (≈ 50%).

Ejemplos:
  • She's late — she might have missed the bus. — Llega tarde — quizá perdió el autobús.
  • I don't know where he is. He may have gone home. — Puede que se fuera a casa.
  • They may not have received the letter yet.
Escala de certeza sobre el pasado:
  • must have (seguro que sí): She must have forgotten.
  • may/might have (quizás): She might have forgotten.
  • can't/couldn't have (seguro que no): She can't have forgotten.
Significado especial de might have: reproche — «podrías haber…»: You might have told me! — ¡Podrías habérmelo dicho!
int_u30.exc: Might have + V3 también expresa reproche: You might have told me! (podrías haber dicho).
mistakes: She may missed the bus. → She may have missed the bus. — sobre el pasado — may have + V3.
He might have took the wrong road. → He might have taken the wrong road. — se necesita V3.
They might been late. → They might have been late. — se necesita forma have been.

int_u31.exp:
Unit 31 — must y have to: necesidad/obligación.

must — necesidad interna, convicción personal del hablante, orden del hablante:
  • I must call my mother. — Tengo que llamar a mi madre (yo lo creo).
  • You must see this film! — ¡Tienes que ver esta película!
have to — exigencia externa: reglas, circunstancias, otras personas:
  • I have to wear a uniform at work. — Tengo que llevar uniforme (regla).
  • She has to leave early on Fridays.
Formas de have to: (−) don't have to; (?) Do you have to…?; pasado had to, futuro will have to.

Must no tiene pasado/futuro — usa had to / will have to.
En preguntas más a menudo have to: Do I have to go?
Diferencia clave en negación: mustn't = prohibición (no se puede); don't have to = ausencia de necesidad (no estoy obligado).
int_u31.exc:
En pasado — solo had to (must no tiene pasado).
Mustn't ≠ don't have to: prohibición vs. no obligación.
mistakes: I musted work late. → I had to work late. — pasado — had to.
She mustn't go — it's optional. → She doesn't have to go. — mustn't = no se puede, no «no obligatorio».
Do I must go? → Do I have to go? / Must I go? — must no usa do.

int_u32.text: Unidad 32 - Debe, no debe, no necesita
int_u32.exp:
Unit 32 — must, mustn't, needn't / don't need to.

must — obligación/necesidad: You must wear a helmet.
mustn't — prohibición, «no se puede»: You mustn't smoke here.
needn't / don't need to / don't have to — ausencia de necesidad, «no estoy obligado»: You needn't come early — we'll wait.

Diferencia principal: mustn't vs. needn't — son opuestos en significado:
  • You mustn't tell anyone. — No se puede decir a nadie.
  • You needn't tell anyone. — Puedes no decir (no obligado).
Pasado:
  • needn't have + V3 — no era necesario, pero lo hice (en vano): You needn't have cooked — we ate.
  • didn't need to + V1 — no hizo falta (y no lo hice): I didn't need to cook — we ordered pizza.
needn't — modal, sin to; need to — verbo normal, con to.
int_u32.exc:
Needn't have + V3 = no era necesario (pero lo hice): You needn't have cooked — we ate.
Didn't need to = no hizo falta (no lo hice).
mistakes: You mustn't worry — it's not required. → You needn't worry. — mustn't = prohibición.
She needn't to come. → She needn't come. — needn't — modal, sin to.
You mustn't to smoke here. → You mustn't smoke here. — sin to.

int_u33.exp:
Unit 33 — should para consejos, recomendaciones, opinión personal.

Fórmula: should (not) + V1. Sinónimo — ought to (más formal).

Significados:
  • Consejo/recomendación: You should eat more vegetables. — Deberías comer más verduras.
  • Opinión sobre lo correcto: The government should do more.
  • Pregunta por consejo: What should I do?
should have + V3 — reproche/arrepentimiento sobre el pasado («debía haberlo hecho, pero no»):
  • You should have called. — Deberías haber llamado.
  • I shouldn't have said that. — No debía haber dicho eso.
Contraste: should — consejo suave; must/have to — obligación; could — opción posible.
Frases frecuentes: I think you should…, Do you think I should…?, You'd better (consejo más fuerte, ver Unit 35).
int_u33.exc:
Should have + V3 = debía haber hecho, pero no: You should have called.
Ought to — sinónimo de should, pero más formal.
mistakes: You should to see a doctor. → You should see a doctor. — sin to.
She should went earlier. → She should have gone earlier. — arrepentimiento del pasado.
You shouldn't to smoke. → You shouldn't smoke. — sin to.

int_u34.exp:
Unit 34 — should para expresar expectativas y suposiciones.

Significados:
  • Expectativa (debería ser así): She should be here by now. — Ya debería estar aquí.
  • Resultado probable: The package should arrive tomorrow. — El paquete debería llegar mañana.
  • Sorpresa/indignación (Why should…?): Why should I pay? — ¿Por qué tendría que pagar yo?
should have + V3 — se esperaba, pero quizá no ocurrió: The letter should have arrived yesterday. — La carta debía haber llegado ayer (pero…).

En construcciones formales tras It's strange/surprising/funny that…, I'm surprised/sorry that…: It's odd that he should say that. — Es raro que diga eso.

Contraste: should (expectativa, 80%) vs. must (certeza, 95%) vs. might (posibilidad, 50%).
int_u34.exc: Should have + V3 = ya debía haber ocurrido: The letter should have arrived by now.
mistakes: The bus must be here any minute. → The bus should be here any minute. — expectativa — should.
She should has finished. → She should have finished. — should have, no has.
The train should to arrive at 10. → The train should arrive at 10. — sin to.

int_u35.text: Unidad 35 – Es hora y será mejor que lo hagas.
int_u35.note: Es hora de irse. Será mejor que te des prisa.
int_u35.exp:
Unit 35 — It's time y had better.

It's time + to-infinitive — es hora de (general): It's time to go.
It's (high/about) time + subject + Past Simple — ya es hora de (reproche de que la acción se retrasa): It's time you went to bed. — Ya es hora de que te acuestes. ¡Subjuntivo, Past Simple con sentido presente!

had better ('d better) + V1 — mejor (consejo con advertencia de consecuencias desagradables):
  • You'd better hurry or you'll miss the train. — Mejor date prisa.
  • You'd better not tell her. — Mejor no se lo digas.
Particularidades de had better:
  • Se refiere a una situación concreta ahora/pronto, no a consejos generales.
  • Siempre forma pasada had, pero sentido presente/futuro.
  • Negación: 'd better not (no hadn't better).
  • Más fuerte que should: implica amenaza/problema.
int_u35.exc:
Tras It's time con sujeto — Past Simple: It's time you went (no you go).
Had better — solo presente/futuro, no pasado.
mistakes: It's time you go to bed. → It's time you went to bed. — con sujeto — Past Simple.
You'd better to hurry. → You'd better hurry. — sin to.
You better not smoke. → You'd better not smoke. — se necesita 'd (had).

int_u36.exp:
Unit 36 — múltiples funciones de would.

1. Ofertas y deseos corteses:
  • Would you like some coffee? — ¿Quiere café?
  • I'd like to book a table. — Quisiera reservar mesa.
2. Hipotético/imaginario (Tipo 2):
  • If I had money, I would travel more.
  • What would you do? — ¿Qué harías tú?
3. Acciones regulares del pasado (= used to, pero solo para acciones):
  • As a child, I would spend hours reading. — De niño leía horas.
4. Rechazo en el pasado (won't en el pasado): The car wouldn't start. — El coche no arrancaba.

Fórmulas: would + V1; would have + V3 (Tipo 3 e hipotético pasado).
¡Atención! Para ESTADOS pasados — solo used to o Past Simple: I had a dog (no I would have). Tras would like — infinitivo con to.
int_u36.exc:
Would like + to-infinitive (no -ing): I'd like to go.
Would para hábitos pasados — solo acciones (no estados): I had a bike (no I would have).
mistakes: Would you like going? → Would you like to go? — tras would like — infinitivo con to.
I would to travel more. → I'd like to travel more. — se necesita like.
When I was a child I would have a dog. → …I had a dog. — would no para estados.

int_u37.exp:
Unit 37 — peticiones y permisos con can, could, would, may.

Petición de acción (¿harías?):
  • Can you help me? — neutral.
  • Could you help me? — más cortés.
  • Would you help me? — cortés/formal.
  • Would you mind + V-ing? — lo más cortés: Would you mind closing the window?
Petición de permiso (¿puedo?):
  • Can I…? — informal.
  • Could I…? — cortés.
  • May I…? — formal: May I come in?
Respuestas: Sure / Of course / Certainly / No problem / I'm afraid not.
Importante con Would you mind: aceptación = No, not at all / No, of course not (= «no me importa»). La respuesta Yes significa «me importa». Tras Would you mind — gerundio (-ing), no infinitivo.
int_u37.exc:
Tras Would you mind — gerundio: Would you mind opening the window?
Respuesta afirmativa a Would you mind…? = No, not at all (= no me importa).
mistakes: Would you mind to open the window? → Would you mind opening the window? — se necesita -ing.
Could you to help? → Could you help? — sin to.
Would you mind? — Yes, of course (quería aceptar). → No, not at all. — yes significa «me opongo».

int_u38.text: Unidad 38 - Condicional Tipo 1 y 2 (Si lo hago/Si lo hiciera)
int_u38.note: Si la veo, se lo diré. Si fuera rico...
int_u38.exp:
Unit 38 — comparación de First y Second Conditional.

Tipo 1 (futuro real/probable): If + Present Simple, will + V1
  • If I see her, I'll tell her. — Si la veo, le digo.
Tipo 2 (presente o futuro irreal/improbable): If + Past Simple, would + V1
  • If I were rich, I would travel the world. — Si fuera rico, viajaría por el mundo.
  • If I knew her number, I'd call her (pero no lo sé).
Diferencia clave — probabilidad:
  • Tipo 1: situación real, puede pasar.
  • Tipo 2: hipótesis, no es verdad ahora o improbable.
Subjuntivo were para todas las personas en Tipo 2: If I were you… (si estuviera en tu lugar) — expresión estándar de consejo. En habla coloquial se admite was. Tras if nunca will/would.
int_u38.exc:
En Tipo 2 en lugar de was a menudo were para todas las personas: If I were you…
Tras if no ponemos will/would.
mistakes: If I was you, I'd study. → If I were you, I'd study. — formalmente — were.
If I would have time, I would help. → If I had time, I would help. — tras if no hay would.
If he comes, I would tell him. → If he comes, I'll tell him. / If he came, I would tell him. — concordancia de tipos.

int_u39.exp:
Unit 39 — construcción I wish para presente irreal (= Tipo 2).

Fórmula: I wish + subject + Past Simple/were — arrepentimiento por la situación actual, deseo de que ahora fuera diferente.

Ejemplos:
  • I wish I knew the answer. — Ojalá supiera la respuesta.
  • I wish I had more time. — Si tuviera más tiempo.
  • I wish she were here. — Ojalá estuviera aquí.
  • He wishes he could speak Spanish. — Lástima que no sepa hablar español.
Sinónimo — If only: expresión más emocional del mismo sentido: If only I knew!

Importante:
  • Tras I wish — Past Simple (¡no Present!) para presente irreal.
  • I wish I were (formal) o I wish I was (coloquial).
  • Modales: can → could. No se puede I wish I will/would (a uno mismo).
int_u39.exc:
I wish I were… (formal) o I wish I was… (coloquial).
Tras I wish para presente — Past Simple (no Present).
mistakes: I wish I have a car. → I wish I had a car. — para presente irreal — Past Simple.
I wish I am taller. → I wish I were taller. — subjuntivo were.
I wish I can fly. → I wish I could fly. — can → could.

int_u40.text: Unidad 40 - Condicional Tipo 3 (Si lo hubiera hecho)
int_u40.exp:
Unit 40 — Third Conditional: pasado irreal.

Fórmula: If + Past Perfect, would/could/might have + V3. Hablamos de lo que NO ocurrió en el pasado y de consecuencias imaginadas.

Ejemplos:
  • If I had studied, I would have passed. — Si hubiera estudiado, habría aprobado (pero no estudié y no aprobé).
  • If you hadn't helped me, I couldn't have finished.
  • She would have come if you had invited her.
Tipo mixto (Mixed Conditional): condición pasada + resultado presente o viceversa:
  • If I had taken the job, I would be in Tokyo now. — Si hubiera aceptado el trabajo (pasado), estaría ahora en Tokio (presente).
  • If I were more organised, I would have finished. — Si fuera más organizado (carácter), habría terminado.
Atención: tras if — nunca would have. Siempre had + V3.
int_u40.exc: Tipo mixto: condición pasada + resultado presente: If I had studied, I would know it now.
mistakes: If I would have known, I would have helped. → If I had known, I would have helped. — tras if — had + V3.
If she had came, we would have seen her. → If she had come, we would have seen her. — V3 de come — come.
If you listened, you would pass. (sobre el pasado) → If you had listened, you would have passed. — Tipo 3 completo.

int_u41.note: Ojalá pudiera volar. Ojalá no hubiera hecho eso.
int_u41.exp:
Unit 41 — todas las construcciones con wish e if only.

1. wish + Past Simple — arrepentimiento sobre el presente: I wish I had a car. — Ojalá tuviera coche.
2. wish + Past Perfect — arrepentimiento sobre el pasado: I wish I hadn't said that. — Ojalá no hubiera dicho eso.
3. wish + would — irritación por comportamiento de otro o deseo de cambio:
  • I wish you would stop smoking. — Ojalá dejaras de fumar.
  • I wish it would stop raining.
4. wish + could — deseo de tener capacidad:
  • I wish I could help. — Ojalá pudiera ayudar.
Reglas importantes:
  • I wish I would… — NO SE PUEDE (a uno mismo — solo could).
  • wish + would — sobre comportamiento irritante o cambios externos.
If only — mismas fórmulas, tono más emocional/dramático: If only I had listened!
int_u41.exc: I wish + would no se aplica a uno mismo: no I wish I would… — usamos could.
mistakes: I wish I would be taller. → I wish I were taller. — a uno mismo would no se aplica.
I wish I didn't say that yesterday. → I wish I hadn't said that yesterday. — sobre el pasado — Past Perfect.
If only he will stop. → If only he would stop. — se necesita would.

int_u42.note: La ventana estaba rota. El queso se elabora con leche.
int_u42.exp:
Unit 42 — fundamentos de la voz pasiva: foco en la acción/objeto, no en el ejecutor.

Fórmula: to be (en el tiempo requerido) + V3.

Tiempos principales:
  • Present Simple: is/are + V3: Coffee is grown in Brazil.
  • Past Simple: was/were + V3: The house was built in 1890.
  • Future Simple: will be + V3: The results will be announced tomorrow.
Frase by se usa solo si el agente es importante: Hamlet was written by Shakespeare. Si no, se omite: My wallet was stolen (no importa por quién).

Cuándo se usa la pasiva:
  • El agente es desconocido o no importante.
  • La acción es más importante que el agente.
  • En textos científicos y oficiales.
Atención: los verbos intransitivos (arrive, happen, go, sleep, come, exist) NO forman pasiva. Regulares — -ed; irregulares — tabla V3.
int_u42.exc:
Los verbos intransitivos (arrive, happen, sleep) no pueden ir en pasiva.
Hay que saber V3 de los irregulares: break/broken, write/written, take/taken.
mistakes: The window broke by the kids. → The window was broken by the kids. — se necesita be + V3.
He was stole the money. → The money was stolen. — la pasiva se aplica al objeto.
The accident was happened yesterday. → The accident happened yesterday. — happen es intransitivo.

int_u43.text: Unidad 43 – Voz pasiva en todos los tiempos (lección 2)
int_u43.note: se está haciendo / se ha encontrado / se construirá
int_u43.exp:
Unit 43 — pasiva en todos los tiempos y con modales.

Regla general: be concuerda con el tiempo, V3 invariable.

Tabla completa:
  • Present Simple: is made
  • Present Continuous: is being made
  • Present Perfect: has been made
  • Past Simple: was made
  • Past Continuous: was being made
  • Past Perfect: had been made
  • Future: will be made
  • Future Perfect: will have been made
Modales + pasiva: modal + be + V3: must be done, can be fixed, should be repaired, might be lost.

Ejemplos:
  • The road is being repaired. — Están reparando la carretera.
  • The letter has been sent. — La carta ha sido enviada.
  • This can't be true. (forma activa de be con modal).
Atención: Perfect Continuous y Future Continuous en pasiva se usan raramente.
int_u43.exc: Modales en pasiva: modal + be + V3: must be done, can be seen, should be repaired.
mistakes: The road is repairing now. → The road is being repaired now. — Continuous Passive = is being + V3.
The letter has sent. → The letter has been sent. — Perfect Passive = has been + V3.
It must done. → It must be done. — tras modal — be + V3.

int_u44.note: Me dijeron. Le regalaron un libro.
int_u44.exp:
Unit 44 — pasiva de verbos con dos complementos.

Los verbos give, tell, show, offer, ask, send, teach, pay, promise, lend tienen dos objetos (a quién + qué). En pasiva se puede hacer cualquiera sujeto:

Active: They gave Anna a present.
Passive 1 (más común): Anna was given a present. — A Anna le regalaron un regalo.
Passive 2: A present was given to Anna. (se necesita to).

Más ejemplos:
  • I was told the truth. — Me dijeron la verdad.
  • He was offered a job. — Le ofrecieron un trabajo.
  • She was shown the results.
Verbos especiales:
  • explain, describe, suggest, say, mention — la persona NO pasa a ser sujeto de la pasiva. No I was explained → It was explained to me.
Elección frecuente: los nativos ponen más a menudo a la persona en pasiva (sujeto humano), porque se habla de ella.
int_u44.exc: El verbo explain no se pasiviza con persona como sujeto: no I was explained — solo It was explained to me.
mistakes: I was explained the rule. → The rule was explained to me. / I was told the rule. — explain es especial.
A book was given me. → A book was given to me. / I was given a book. — se necesita to.
He was offered by a job. → He was offered a job. — sin by ante complemento directo.

int_u45.text: Unidad 45 – Se dice… / Se dice que es…
int_u45.note: Se dice que es rico. Se dice que es rico.
int_u45.exp:
Unit 45 — construcciones pasivas de reporte: It is said that… / He is said to….

Verbos de opinión/informe: say, think, believe, report, know, expect, consider, understand, assume. Se usan en construcciones impersonales cuando transmitimos opinión general, rumores, noticias.

Dos variantes de traducir un mismo sentido:
  • It is said that he is very rich. (impersonal)
  • He is said to be very rich. (personal con infinitivo)
Fórmulas:
  • It + is/was + said + that + clause
  • Subject + is/was + said + to-infinitive
Presente vs. pasado:
  • Presente: He is believed to live in Spain.
  • Pasado: He is believed to have lived in Spain. (infinitivo perfecto)
Ejemplos:
  • The painting is thought to be genuine.
  • She is known to have been a spy.
int_u45.exc: Pasado en esta construcción: He is said to have been — infinitivo perfecto: She is believed to have left the country.
mistakes: He is said he is rich. → He is said to be rich. / It is said that he is rich. — construcción correcta.
She is known has been rich. → She is known to have been rich. — se necesita to have + V3.
It said that he is clever. → It is said that he is clever. — se necesita is.

int_u46.exp:
Unit 46 — construcción causativa have/get something done: contratar a alguien para hacer el trabajo.

Fórmula: have/get + object + V3.

Significado: no lo hacemos nosotros — organizamos que alguien lo haga para nosotros (o que nos ocurra algo).

Ejemplos:
  • I had my hair cut. — Me cortaron el pelo (fui a la peluquería).
  • She's having her house painted. — Le están pintando la casa (contrató a alguien).
  • Where do you get your car serviced? — ¿Dónde revisas el coche?
  • I need to get my eyes tested. — Tengo que revisarme la vista.
have vs. get: significados idénticos; get más coloquial.

Segundo significado — contratiempo: He had his wallet stolen. — Le robaron la cartera.

Contraste:
  • I repaired my car — lo arreglé yo mismo.
  • I had my car repaired — me lo arreglaron.
Orden: object estrictamente antes de V3.
int_u46.exc:
En estilo coloquial get something done = have something done: I got my hair cut.
En idioms have — sobre eventos desagradables: He had his wallet stolen.
mistakes: I cut my hair yesterday. (realmente — en peluquería) → I had my hair cut yesterday. — no lo hizo él mismo.
She is having painted her house. → She is having her house painted. — orden: have + object + V3.
I had repaired my car. (organizó) → I had my car repaired. — orden de palabras.

int_u47.note: Ella dijo que estaba cansada. Me dijo que vendría.
int_u47.exp:
Unit 47 — estilo indirecto: reglas de desplazamiento de tiempos.

Desplazamientos principales (si el verbo introductorio está en pasado):
  • Present Simple → Past Simple
  • Present Continuous → Past Continuous
  • Present Perfect → Past Perfect
  • Past Simple → Past Perfect
  • will → would, can → could, may → might, must → had to
Cambios de pronombres y adverbios:
  • I → he/she, my → his/her, we → they
  • today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, yesterday → the day before, here → there, this → that
Ejemplos:
  • "I'm tired." → She said (that) she was tired.
  • "I'll call you." → He said he would call me.
say vs. tell:
  • say (to smb) that…: She said that she was late.
  • tell + somebody + that…: She told me that she was late. No tell that.
El desplazamiento no se necesita si lo dicho sigue siendo verdad: She said the Earth is round.
int_u47.exc:
Si lo dicho aún es verdad, el desplazamiento de tiempos es opcional: She said the Earth is round.
Tras tell es obligatoria indicación del destinatario: She told me…
mistakes: She said me she was tired. → She told me she was tired. / She said she was tired. — tras say — sin destinatario.
He said he will come. → He said he would come. — desplazamiento: will → would.
She told that she was late. → She said that she was late. / She told me that… — tell requiere objeto.

int_u48.text: Unidad 48 - Discurso indirecto (Lección 2): Preguntas y órdenes
int_u48.note: Me preguntó dónde vivía. Ella me dijo que esperara.
int_u48.exp:
Unit 48 — preguntas indirectas, órdenes, peticiones.

Preguntas indirectas:
  • Especiales: ask + wh-word + orden directo: "Where do you live?" → He asked where I lived.
  • Generales (yes/no): ask + if/whether + orden directo: "Are you ready?" → She asked if I was ready.
Atención: en la pregunta indirecta:
  • No hay inversión (where I lived, no where did I live).
  • No hay do/does/did.
  • No hay signo de interrogación.
  • Hay desplazamiento de tiempos.
Órdenes/peticiones indirectas: tell/ask/order/warn + object + (not) to-infinitive:
  • "Wait!" → She told me to wait.
  • "Please help me." → He asked me to help him.
  • "Don't go." → She told me not to go.
Verbos introductorios: tell, ask, order, beg, advise, warn, remind, invite, encourage.
int_u48.exc: En la pregunta indirecta orden de palabras directo — sin do/does/did: He asked where I lived (no where did I live).
mistakes: He asked where did I live. → He asked where I lived. — orden directo en pregunta indirecta.
She asked me wait. → She told me to wait. / She asked me to wait. — se necesita to-inf.
He asked if am I ready. → He asked if I was ready. — orden directo + desplazamiento de tiempo.

int_u49.text: Unidad 49 - Todos los tipos de preguntas (Lección 1)
int_u49.exp:
Unit 49 — construcción de todos los tipos de preguntas.

Preguntas generales (yes/no): inversión del verbo auxiliar/modal y sujeto.
  • Are you ready?
  • Do you speak French?
  • Has she arrived?
  • Can he swim?
Preguntas especiales: wh-word + aux + subject + verb:
  • Where do you live?
  • What is she doing?
  • When did he leave?
Atención preguntas al sujeto — sin inversión, sin do/does/did:
  • Who called? (no Who did call?)
  • What happened?
  • Which bus goes to the centre?
Preguntas con preposición: preposición normalmente al final: Who are you waiting for? What are you talking about? Where does she come from?
Negative questions (Don't you know? Isn't it strange?) expresan sorpresa o invitan al acuerdo.
int_u49.exc: En preguntas al sujeto no hay inversión ni do/does: Who called? What happened?
mistakes: Who did call you? → Who called you? — pregunta al sujeto sin did.
Where she lives? → Where does she live? — se necesita inversión con does.
What means this word? → What does this word mean? — se necesita does.

int_u50.text: Unidad 50 - Preguntas en oraciones simples y complejas (lección 2)
int_u50.exp:
Unit 50 — preguntas incrustadas (indirectas) en oraciones complejas.

Tras frases introductorias Do you know…?, Can you tell me…?, I wonder…, I'd like to know…, Have you any idea…?, Could you tell me…? — se usa orden directo de palabras, sin inversión y sin do/does/did.

Comparación:
  • Directa: Where does she live?
  • Incrustada: Do you know where she lives?
  • Directa: What is the time?
  • Incrustada: Can you tell me what the time is?
Para yes/no-preguntas — if/whether:
  • I wonder if he is coming.
  • Do you know whether they are open?
Ejemplos:
  • Have you any idea how much this costs?
  • I'd like to know when the meeting starts.
Atención: esta regla se extiende también al estilo indirecto (Unit 48). El signo de interrogación va solo si toda la oración es pregunta.
int_u50.exc:
Tras I wonder, Do you know, Can you tell me — orden directo de palabras.
Para preguntas sí/no — if/whether: I wonder if she is coming.
mistakes: Do you know where does she live? → Do you know where she lives? — orden directo.
Can you tell me what is the time? → Can you tell me what the time is? — sin inversión.
I wonder does he know. → I wonder if he knows. — se necesita if/whether.

int_u51.text: Unidad 51 - Verbos auxiliares en una oración
int_u51.exp:
Unit 51 — verbos auxiliares: respuestas cortas, acuerdo, énfasis, evitar repetición.

1. Respuestas cortas: usamos el auxiliar de la pregunta.
  • Are you tired? — Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
  • Has she left? — Yes, she has.
2. So/Neither + auxiliary + subject — acuerdo/desacuerdo breve:
  • I'm tired. — So am I. — Yo también.
  • I can't swim. — Neither can I. / Nor can I.
3. do/does/did enfático — énfasis en afirmación:
  • I do love you! — ¡De verdad que te quiero!
  • She does know the answer.
  • He did call, I swear.
4. Evitar repetición:
  • I haven't read the book, but Jane has.
  • A: Who can cook? B: I can.
Atención: con So/Neither — inversión (aux antes del sujeto). Me too / Me neither — variantes coloquiales.
int_u51.exc: So am I / Neither am I — inversión. Me too / Me neither — variante coloquial.
mistakes: I like coffee. — So I do. → So do I. — inversión: so + aux + subject.
I don't like it. — Neither I do. → Neither do I. — orden correcto.
I do liked it. → I did like it. / I do like it. — do/did enfático + V1.

int_u52.text: Unidad 52 — Preguntas de etiquetas: preguntas divididas
int_u52.exp:
Unit 52 — preguntas coletilla (tag questions) para confirmación o comprobación.

Regla: oración afirmativa → «coletilla» negativa; negativa → afirmativa.

Fórmula: auxiliary + (n't) + subject pronoun.

Ejemplos:
  • You're tired, aren't you?
  • She can swim, can't she?
  • He hasn't called, has he?
  • They don't know, do they?
Casos especiales:
  • I am → aren't I? (no amn't I): I'm late, aren't I?
  • Let's → shall we?: Let's go, shall we?
  • Imperativo → will you / won't you / would you: Close the door, will you?
  • Tras nothing, nobody, no one — usamos it/they: Nobody called, did they?
Entonación:
  • Descendente ↓ — el hablante está seguro, espera acuerdo.
  • Ascendente ↑ — pregunta real, no está seguro.
int_u52.exc:
I am → aren't I?
Let's → shall we?
Imperativo → will you / won't you?
mistakes: I'm late, amn't I? → I'm late, aren't I? — excepción para I am.
Let's go, will we? → Let's go, shall we? — tras let's — shall we.
She can swim, can she? → She can swim, can't she? — afirmación → coletilla negativa.

int_u53.note: Disfruto nadando. Ella terminó de trabajar.
int_u53.exp:
Unit 53 — verbos tras los que va gerundio (-ing).

Verbos clave con -ing: enjoy, finish, stop, mind, avoid, suggest, recommend, admit, deny, consider, imagine, risk, practise, postpone, delay, keep, give up, put off, can't stand, can't help, miss, feel like.

Ejemplos:
  • I enjoy reading. — Me gusta leer.
  • She suggested going for a walk. — Sugirió ir a pasear.
  • He denied stealing the money. — Negó haber robado el dinero.
  • I can't help laughing. — No puedo evitar reír.
  • Would you mind waiting?
Negación del gerundio: not + V-ing: She admitted not telling the truth.

Errores frecuentes: especial atención con suggest y recommend — solo -ing, no to-inf: He suggested going (no to go).
Gerundio como sustantivo: puede ser sujeto: Swimming is good for you.
int_u53.exc: Tras suggest, recommend — solo gerundio (no to-inf): He suggested going by train.
mistakes: I enjoy to swim. → I enjoy swimming. — tras enjoy — gerundio.
She suggested to go to the cinema. → She suggested going to the cinema. — tras suggest — -ing.
I avoid to speak to him. → I avoid speaking to him. — tras avoid — gerundio.

int_u54.text: Unidad 54 - Infinitivo después de los verbos (lección 1)
int_u54.exp:
Unit 54 — verbos tras los que sigue infinitivo con to.

Verbos clave con to-inf: want, need, hope, plan, decide, expect, agree, refuse, offer, promise, threaten, manage, learn, afford, pretend, seem, appear, arrange, tend, fail.

Ejemplos:
  • I want to go home. — Quiero ir a casa.
  • She decided to stay. — Decidió quedarse.
  • They promised to help. — Prometieron ayudar.
  • He managed to escape. — Logró escapar.
  • We can't afford to buy a new car.
Negación: not + to-infinitive: He decided not to go.

Help — especial: puede ir con to o sin: help (to) carry.
Contraste con Unit 53: hay que recordar qué verbo requiere qué forma. Algunos admiten ambas variantes (ver Unit 56/57).
int_u54.exc:
Hope, decide, plan, manage — solo infinitivo con to.
Tras help es posible con to y sin: help (to) do.
mistakes: She decided going. → She decided to go. — tras decide — to-inf.
I hope seeing you. → I hope to see you. — tras hope — to-inf.
He managed escaping. → He managed to escape. — tras manage — to-inf.

int_u55.text: Unidad 55 - Infinitivos después de los verbos (lección 2)
int_u55.note: Ella me dijo que esperara. Él le pidió que no fuera.
int_u55.exp:
Unit 55 — construcción verb + object + to-infinitive (verbo + a quién + qué hacer).

Verbos clave: want, ask, tell, expect, allow, advise, encourage, order, remind, warn, invite, persuade, force, teach, help, enable.

Ejemplos:
  • She asked me to wait. — Me pidió que esperara.
  • They expect us to be on time.
  • He persuaded her to come.
  • I warned you not to go there. (negación: not to)
  • She taught me to drive.
Excepciones (bare infinitive — sin to):
  • make smb do sth — obligar: She made me laugh.
  • let smb do sth — permitir: Let me think.
Pero en pasiva make requiere to: I was made to wait. Let en pasiva — mediante allowed: I was allowed to leave.

Negación: I want you not to worry. No confundir con want that… (¡no se usa!).
int_u55.exc:
Tras make, let — infinitivo sin to: She made me wait. Let me go.
Pero en pasiva: I was made to wait.
mistakes: She made me to wait. → She made me wait. — tras make — sin to.
He let me to go. → He let me go. — tras let — sin to.
I want that you come. → I want you to come. — construcción want smb to do.

int_u56.text: Unidad 56 - Infinitivo o Gerundio: Regla General
int_u56.note: Me gusta nadar / Me gusta nadar - ¿diferencia?
int_u56.exp:
Unit 56 — verbos que admiten infinitivo y gerundio.

1. Sin diferencia de sentido (begin, start, continue, intend, propose, bother):
  • It started raining / to rain.
2. Diferencia de matiz (like, love, hate, prefer):
  • like + -ing — gusta el proceso: I like swimming (disfruto).
  • like + to-inf — lo considero útil/correcto: I like to exercise in the morning (hábito, lo considero importante).
  • En AmE — más a menudo to-inf.
3. Tras would like/love/hate/prefer — solo to-infinitive:
  • I'd like to go to Japan.
  • She'd love to meet you.
Ejemplos:
  • I love cooking (proceso) vs. I'd love to cook for you tonight (concreto).
Atención: con sentido like = find pleasure — gerundio; like = think it's good — to-inf. Pero en inglés actual esta distinción es cada vez menos estricta.
mistakes: I'd like swimming. → I'd like to swim. / I like swimming. — con would — to-inf.
I started to doing yoga. → I started doing yoga. / I started to do yoga. — ambas variantes, no juntas.
He continued to playing. → He continued playing / to play. — escogemos una forma.

int_u57.text: Unidad 57 – Intenta, necesitas, ayuda: infinitivo o gerundio
int_u57.note: intentar hacer versus intentar hacer / necesita limpieza
int_u57.exp:
Unit 57 — verbos con distinto sentido según la forma.

remember/forget + to-inf — recordar/olvidar hacer (futuro/pendiente):
  • Remember to lock the door! — ¡No olvides cerrar la puerta!
remember/forget + -ing — recordar/olvidar que hacía (pasado):
  • I remember locking the door. — Recuerdo haberla cerrado.
try + to-inf — intentar (con esfuerzo):
  • I tried to open the window, but it was stuck.
try + -ing — probar (experimento):
  • Try turning it off and on again.
stop + to-inf — detenerse para:
  • He stopped to smoke (paró para fumar).
stop + -ing — dejar de hacer:
  • He stopped smoking (dejó de fumar).
need + to-inf — necesitar hacer: I need to rest.
need + -ing — necesitar acción (sentido pasivo): My car needs washing = needs to be washed.
